```
"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI to Tool conversion logic.\\n--\\n-- This module converts parsed and dereferenced OpenAPI operations into the\\n-- internal Tool representation used by the agents system. It handles:\\n--\\n-- * Converting OpenAPI specs to lists of tools\\n-- * Building tool parameter schemas from OpenAPI parameters\\n-- * Naming conventions (operationId or path/method based)\\n-- * Parameter naming (p_ prefix for path/query, b for body)\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    OpenAPITool (..),\\n    ToolParameters (..),\\n    PropertySchema (..),\\n    ToolHandler,\\n\\n    -- * Main conversion functions\\n    convertOpenAPIToTools,\\n    convertOperation,\\n\\n    -- * Parameter building\\n    buildToolProperty,\\n    paramToToolName,\\n    buildToolParameters,\\n\\n    -- * Description building\\n    buildToolDescription,\\n\\n    -- * Required parameters\\n    getRequiredParams,\\n\\n    -- * Naming\\n    deriveToolName,\\n    sanitizePathForName,\\n\\n    -- * OpenAI conversion\\n    toOpenAITool,\\n\\n    -- * Handler helpers\\n    defaultHandler,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Data.Aeson (Value)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport System.Agents.ToolSchema (ParamProperty (..), ParamType (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core Types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handler function type for executing OpenAPI tool calls.\\n-- This is a placeholder type - actual execution would be implemented\\n-- by the caller based on their HTTP client needs.\\ntype ToolHandler = Map Text Value -> IO (Either Text Text)\\n\\n-- | Internal representation of a tool derived from OpenAPI.\\ndata OpenAPITool = OpenAPITool\\n    { toolOperation :: Operation\\n    , toolPath :: Path\\n    , toolMethod :: Method\\n    , toolName :: Text\\n    , toolDescription :: Text\\n    , toolParameters :: ToolParameters\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parameters structure for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata ToolParameters = ToolParameters\\n    { paramsType :: Text\\n    -- ^ Always \\\"object\\\" for OpenAPI tools\\n    , paramsProperties :: Map Text PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ Map of parameter name to property schema\\n    , paramsRequired :: [Text]\\n    -- ^ List of required parameter names\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Property schema for LLM tool parameters.\\n-- Mirrors JSON Schema structure needed for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata PropertySchema = PropertySchema\\n    { propType :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ JSON Schema type (string, integer, boolean, etc.)\\n    , propDescription :: Text\\n    -- ^ Description for the LLM\\n    , propEnum :: Maybe [Text]\\n    -- ^ For enum values, stores the allowed values\\n    , propItems :: Maybe PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ For arrays, the schema of array items\\n    , propAnyOf :: Maybe [PropertySchema]\\n    -- ^ For anyOf schemas, the possible schemas\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Main Conversion Functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPI spec into a list of tools.\\n--\\n-- This function walks through all paths and methods in the spec,\\n-- converting each operation into an 'OpenAPITool'.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let spec = OpenAPISpec (Map.singleton \\\"/pets\\\" (Map.singleton \\\"GET\\\" op)) Nothing\\n-- >>> let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools spec\\n-- >>> length tools\\n-- 1\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools ::\\n    OpenAPISpec ->\\n    [OpenAPITool]\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools spec = do\\n    (path, methods) <- Map.toList (specPaths spec)\\n    (method, operation) <- Map.toList methods\\n    pure $ convertOperation path method operation\\n\\n-- | Converts a single OpenAPI operation to a tool.\\n--\\n-- This is the core conversion function that:\\n-- * Derives a tool name from operationId or path/method\\n-- * Builds the tool description from summary and description\\n-- * Converts parameters to property schemas\\n-- * Tracks required parameters\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nconvertOperation ::\\n    Path ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    OpenAPITool\\nconvertOperation path method op =\\n    OpenAPITool\\n        { toolOperation = op\\n        , toolPath = path\\n        , toolMethod = method\\n        , toolName = deriveToolName path method op\\n        , toolDescription = buildToolDescription op\\n        , toolParameters = buildToolParameters op\\n        }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Name Derivation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Derives a tool name from an OpenAPI operation.\\n--\\n-- Uses operationId if available, otherwise falls back to {method}_{path}.\\n-- All names are prefixed with \\\"openapi_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation (Just \\\"listPets\\\") Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_listPets\\\"\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_get_pets__id_\\\"\\nderiveToolName :: Path -> Method -> Operation -> Text\\nderiveToolName path method op =\\n    \\\"openapi_\\\" <> case opOperationId op of\\n        Just opId -> opId\\n        Nothing -> sanitizePathForName method path\\n\\n-- | Sanitizes a path for use in a tool name.\\n--\\n-- Converts the path/method combination to a valid identifier.\\n-- Replaces special characters with underscores.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/pets\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_pets\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"POST\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}/owner\\\"\\n-- \\\"post_pets__id__owner\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/api/v1/users\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_api_v1_users\\\"\\nsanitizePathForName :: Method -> Path -> Text\\nsanitizePathForName method path =\\n    let methodPart = Text.toLower method\\n        -- Remove leading slash and replace special chars with underscore\\n        pathPart =\\n            Text.replace \\\"/\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"{\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"}\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.dropWhile (== '/')\\n                $ path\\n        -- Clean up multiple consecutive underscores\\n        cleaned = Text.replace \\\"__\\\" \\\"_\\\" pathPart\\n     in if Text.null cleaned\\n            then methodPart\\n            else methodPart <> \\\"_\\\" <> cleaned\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Description Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builds a tool description from an operation's summary and description.\\n--\\n-- Combines both fields with a colon separator if both are present.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a single pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op\\n-- \\\"Get a pet:\\\\nReturns a single pet\\\"\\n-- >>> let op2 = Operation Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"Just description\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op2\\n-- \\\":\\\\nJust description\\\"\\nbuildToolDescription :: Operation -> Text\\nbuildToolDescription op =\\n    fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opSummary op) <> \\\":\\\\n\\\" <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opDescription op)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts a parameter to its tool property name.\\n--\\n-- Prefixes path and query parameters with \\\"p_\\\" to avoid conflicts.\\n-- Header parameters are also prefixed with \\\"p_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let param = Parameter \\\"petId\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param\\n-- \\\"p_petId\\\"\\n-- >>> let param2 = Parameter \\\"limit\\\" ParamInQuery (Just \\\"Max results\\\") False Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param2\\n-- \\\"p_limit\\\"\\nparamToToolName :: Parameter -> Text\\nparamToToolName param = \\\"p_\\\" <> paramName param\\n\\n-- | Builds tool parameters structure from an operation.\\n--\\n-- Collects all parameters (path, query, header) and body into a\\n-- unified parameter schema structure.\\nbuildToolParameters :: Operation -> ToolParameters\\nbuildToolParameters op =\\n    ToolParameters\\n        { paramsType = \\\"object\\\"\\n        , paramsProperties = paramProps <> bodyProps\\n        , paramsRequired = getRequiredParams op\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Convert operation parameters to properties\\n    paramProps = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe paramToProperty (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property if present\\n    bodyProps = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body -> bodyToProperty body\\n        Nothing -> Map.empty\\n\\n    -- Convert a single parameter to (name, schema) if it has a schema\\n    paramToProperty :: Parameter -> Maybe (Text, PropertySchema)\\n    paramToProperty param = do\\n        schema <- paramSchema param\\n        pure (paramToToolName param, buildToolProperty schema (paramDescription param))\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property map\\n    bodyToProperty :: RequestBody -> Map Text PropertySchema\\n    bodyToProperty body =\\n        -- Use \\\"b\\\" as the body parameter name\\n        -- For simplicity, we take the first schema from content types\\n        case Map.elems (reqBodyContent body) of\\n            [] -> Map.empty\\n            (schema : _) -> Map.singleton \\\"b\\\" (schemaToProperty schema (bodyDescription body))\\n\\n    bodyDescription :: RequestBody -> Text\\n    bodyDescription body = fromMaybe \\\"Request body\\\" (reqBodyDescription body)\\n\\n-- | Builds a property schema from an OpenAPI schema.\\n--\\n-- Handles:\\n-- * Enum values: copies type and augments description\\n-- * Arrays: copies items schema\\n-- * AnyOf: copies anyOf schemas\\n-- * Basic types: copies type and description\\n--\\n-- The parameter description is merged with the schema description.\\nbuildToolProperty ::\\n    Schema ->\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    PropertySchema\\nbuildToolProperty schema paramDesc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = buildDescription schema\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`buildToolProperty` Nothing) (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`buildToolProperty` Nothing)) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Build description from parameter description, schema description, and enum values\\n    buildDescription :: Schema -> Text\\n    buildDescription s =\\n        let baseDesc = fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (paramDesc <|> schemaDescription s)\\n            enumDesc = case schemaEnum s of\\n                Just vals -> \\\"\\\\nAllowed values: \\\" <> formatEnumValues vals\\n                Nothing -> \\\"\\\"\\n         in baseDesc <> enumDesc\\n\\n    -- Format enum values as a readable string\\n    formatEnumValues :: [Aeson.Value] -> Text\\n    formatEnumValues vals =\\n        let txtVals = mapMaybe extractString vals\\n         in Text.intercalate \\\", \\\" txtVals\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n    -- Extract enum values as Text list\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Converts a Schema to PropertySchema without parameter description.\\nschemaToProperty :: Schema -> Text -> PropertySchema\\nschemaToProperty schema desc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = desc <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (fmap (\\\"\\\\n\\\" <>) (schemaDescription schema))\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\") (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\")) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n-- Alternative operator for Maybe\\n(<|>) :: Maybe a -> Maybe a -> Maybe a\\n(<|>) (Just x) _ = Just x\\n(<|>) Nothing y = y\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Required Parameters\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Gets the list of required parameter names for a tool.\\n--\\n-- Includes:\\n-- * Parameters marked with required=true\\n-- * Body parameter \\\"b\\\" if request body is required\\ngetRequiredParams :: Operation -> [Text]\\ngetRequiredParams op =\\n    paramNames <> bodyName\\n  where\\n    -- Get required parameter names (prefixed)\\n    paramNames =\\n        map paramToToolName $\\n            filter paramRequired (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Check if body is required\\n    bodyName = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body | reqBodyRequired body -> [\\\"b\\\"]\\n        _ -> []\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Conversion to OpenAI Tool Format\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPITool to the OpenAI Tool format.\\n--\\n-- This allows OpenAPITools to be used directly with the LLM interface.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"test\\\") (Just \\\"Test op\\\") (Just \\\"A test\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let apiTool = convertOperation \\\"/test\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> let tool = toOpenAITool apiTool\\n-- >>> OpenAI.toolName tool\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_test\\\"}\\ntoOpenAITool :: OpenAPITool -> OpenAI.Tool\\ntoOpenAITool apiTool =\\n    OpenAI.Tool\\n        { OpenAI.toolName = OpenAI.ToolName (toolName apiTool)\\n        , OpenAI.toolDescription = toolDescription apiTool\\n        , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = map propertyToParamProperty $ Map.toList (paramsProperties (toolParameters apiTool))\\n        }\\n  where\\n    propertyToParamProperty :: (Text, PropertySchema) -> ParamProperty\\n    propertyToParamProperty (name, prop) =\\n        ParamProperty\\n            { propertyKey = name\\n            , propertyType = schemaTypeToParamType prop\\n            , propertyDescription = propDescription prop\\n            }\\n\\n    schemaTypeToParamType :: PropertySchema -> ParamType\\n    schemaTypeToParamType prop =\\n        case propEnum prop of\\n            Just vals -> EnumParamType vals\\n            Nothing -> case propType prop of\\n                Just \\\"string\\\" -> StringParamType\\n                Just \\\"integer\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"number\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"boolean\\\" -> BoolParamType\\n                Just \\\"array\\\" -> case propItems prop of\\n                    Just items -> OpaqueParamType (fromMaybe \\\"any\\\" (propType items))\\n                    Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"array\\\"\\n                Just \\\"object\\\" -> ObjectParamType []\\n                Just other -> OpaqueParamType other\\n                Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"any\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Handler Helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Default handler that always returns an error.\\n-- The actual handler should be provided by the caller.\\ndefaultHandler :: ToolHandler\\ndefaultHandler _ = pure $ Left \\\"Tool handler not implemented\\\"\\n\\n\""
```

```
"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 specification types and JSON parsers.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a subset of OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 and Swagger 2.0 types needed for\\n-- LLM tool generation. It focuses on parsing paths, operations,\\n-- parameters, request bodies, and schemas.\\n--\\n-- Note: This is not a full OpenAPI implementation - only the subset\\n-- needed for tool generation is supported.\\n--\\n-- The module automatically converts Swagger 2.0 specs to OpenAPI 3.0 structure:\\n-- * 'definitions' -> 'components/schemas'\\n-- * 'in: body' parameters -> 'requestBody'\\n-- * parameter-level 'type' -> nested 'schema.type'\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    -- * OpenAPI spec types\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Method,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n    Components (..),\\n    MediaType,\\n\\n    -- * Tool execution result\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n\\n    -- * JSON helpers\\n    Pair,\\n    object,\\n    parseParamLocation,\\n    paramLocationToText,\\n    (.=?),\\n    (.=),\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Applicative ((<|>))\\nimport Data.Aeson (FromJSON (..), ToJSON (..), Value (..), decode, withObject, (.:), (.:?))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Aeson.Key (fromText, toText, Key)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport Data.Aeson.Types (Parser)\\nimport qualified Data.HashMap.Strict as HashMap\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (catMaybes, fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\n-- | Aeson Pair type for building JSON objects.\\ntype Pair = (Key, Value)\\n\\n-- | Build JSON object from a list of optional pairs.\\nobject :: [Maybe Pair] -> Value\\nobject = Aeson.object . catMaybes\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Top-level OpenAPI spec\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Top-level OpenAPI specification document.\\n-- Supports both OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 formats.\\ndata OpenAPISpec = OpenAPISpec\\n    { specPaths :: Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n    , specComponents :: Maybe Components\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"OpenAPISpec\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        -- Check for Swagger 2.0 vs OpenAPI 3.x\\n        mSwagger <- o .:? \\\"swagger\\\"\\n        mOpenapi <- o .:? \\\"openapi\\\"\\n\\n        case (mSwagger :: Maybe Text, mOpenapi :: Maybe Text) of\\n            (Just \\\"2.0\\\", _) -> parseSwaggerV2 o\\n            (_, Just _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o\\n            (_, _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o  -- Default to OpenAPI 3.x parsing\\n\\n-- | Parse OpenAPI 3.x format.\\nparseOpenAPIV3 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseOpenAPIV3 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    paths <- parsePaths pathsObj\\n    components <- o .:? \\\"components\\\"\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 format and convert to OpenAPI 3.x structure.\\nparseSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseSwaggerV2 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    -- Parse paths with Swagger 2.0 conversion\\n    paths <- parsePathsSwaggerV2 pathsObj\\n    -- Convert definitions to components/schemas\\n    definitions <- o .:? \\\"definitions\\\"\\n    let components = fmap definitionsToComponents definitions\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 definitions to Components.\\ndefinitionsToComponents :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Components\\ndefinitionsToComponents defs =\\n    let schemas = HashMap.toList defs\\n        schemaMap = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe parseDefinition schemas\\n    in Components (if Map.null schemaMap then Nothing else Just schemaMap)\\n  where\\n    parseDefinition :: (Key, Value) -> Maybe (Text, Schema)\\n    parseDefinition (k, v) =\\n        case fromJSON v of\\n            Aeson.Success schema -> Just (toText k, schema)\\n            Aeson.Error _ -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert JSON Value to Schema.\\nfromJSON :: Value -> Aeson.Result Schema\\nfromJSON = Aeson.fromJSON\\n\\n-- | Parse the paths object where each path maps to a methods object.\\nparsePaths :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePaths = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItem o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item from the paths object.\\nparsePathItem :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItem o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperations val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse an operations object (the value of a path).\\n-- This contains the HTTP methods like \\\"get\\\", \\\"post\\\", etc.\\nparseOperations :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperations = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperation o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    -- Skip OpenAPI extension fields (starting with \\\"x-\\\") and other non-method fields\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"servers\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation.\\nparseMethodOperation :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperation o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    op <- o .: key\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse paths for Swagger 2.0, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItemSwaggerV2 o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item for Swagger 2.0.\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperationsSwaggerV2 val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse operations for Swagger 2.0, converting body params to requestBody.\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation for Swagger 2.0.\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    opVal <- o .: key\\n    op <- parseOperationSwaggerV2 opVal\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 operation, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Operation\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    operationId <- o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n    summary <- o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n    description <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    rawParams <- fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\"\\n\\n    -- Convert parameters: separate body params from regular params\\n    let (bodyParams, otherParams) = partitionBodyParams rawParams\\n\\n    -- Convert body params to requestBody if present\\n    let mRequestBody = case bodyParams of\\n            [] -> Nothing\\n            (bodyParam : _) -> Just $ swaggerBodyParamToRequestBody bodyParam\\n\\n    -- Parse other parameters\\n    regularParams <- traverse parseParameterSwaggerV2 otherParams\\n\\n    pure $ Operation operationId summary description regularParams mRequestBody\\n  where\\n    partitionBodyParams :: [Value] -> ([SwaggerBodyParam], [Value])\\n    partitionBodyParams = foldr classify ([], [])\\n      where\\n        classify v (bodies, others) =\\n            case parseMaybe parseSwaggerBodyParam v of\\n                Just bp -> (bp : bodies, others)\\n                Nothing -> (bodies, v : others)\\n\\n    parseMaybe :: (Value -> Parser a) -> Value -> Maybe a\\n    parseMaybe p v = case Aeson.parse p v of\\n        Aeson.Success r -> Just r\\n        Aeson.Error _ -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Temporary type for Swagger 2.0 body parameters.\\ndata SwaggerBodyParam = SwaggerBodyParam\\n    { sbpDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , sbpRequired :: Bool\\n    , sbpSchema :: Schema\\n    }\\n\\n-- | Parse a Swagger 2.0 body parameter.\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam :: Value -> Parser SwaggerBodyParam\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam = withObject \\\"body parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    paramIn <- o .: \\\"in\\\" :: Parser Text\\n    if paramIn == \\\"body\\\"\\n        then do\\n            desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n            schema <- o .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n            pure $ SwaggerBodyParam desc required schema\\n        else fail \\\"Not a body parameter\\\"\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 body parameter to RequestBody.\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody :: SwaggerBodyParam -> RequestBody\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody sbp =\\n    let content = Map.singleton \\\"application/json\\\" (sbpSchema sbp)\\n     in RequestBody (sbpDescription sbp) (sbpRequired sbp) content\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 parameter (supports both direct type and schema).\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Parameter\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 v = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" (\\\\o -> do\\n    paramInStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n    paramIn <- parseParamLocationSwaggerV2 paramInStr\\n    name <- o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n    desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\n    -- Swagger 2.0 can have type directly or in schema\\n    mDirectType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mSchema <- o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\n    -- Build schema from direct type if present\\n    let schema = case (mSchema, mDirectType) of\\n            (Just s, _) -> Just s\\n              }\\n            (Nothing, Nothing) -> Nothing\\n\\n    pure $ Parameter name paramIn desc required schema\\n    ) v\\n\\n-- | Parse parameter location for Swagger 2.0 (includes formData).\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"formData\\\" = pure ParamInFormData\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    toJSON spec =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"paths\\\" .= specPaths spec\\n            , \\\"components\\\" .=? specComponents spec\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path and method types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | URL path like \\\"/pets\\\" or \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\".\\ntype Path = Text\\n\\n-- | HTTP method like \\\"GET\\\", \\\"POST\\\", \\\"PUT\\\", \\\"DELETE\\\", etc.\\ntype Method = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation (an endpoint)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents an API operation (endpoint).\\n-- Corresponds to an Operation Object in OpenAPI.\\ndata Operation = Operation\\n    { opOperationId :: Maybe Text\\n    , opSummary :: Maybe Text\\n    , opDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , opParameters :: [Parameter]\\n    , opRequestBody :: Maybe RequestBody\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Operation where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        Operation\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"requestBody\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Operation where\\n    toJSON op =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"operationId\\\" .=? op.opOperationId\\n            , \\\"summary\\\" .=? op.opSummary\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? op.opDescription\\n            , \\\"parameters\\\" .=? if null op.opParameters then Nothing else Just op.opParameters\\n            , \\\"requestBody\\\" .=? op.opRequestBody\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter (path, query, header, formData)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parameter (path, query, header, or formData parameter).\\ndata Parameter = Parameter\\n    { paramName :: Text\\n    , paramIn :: ParamLocation\\n    , paramDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , paramRequired :: Bool\\n    , paramSchema :: Maybe Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Parameter where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        locationStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n        location <- parseParamLocation locationStr\\n        Parameter\\n            <$> o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n            <*> pure location\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Parameter where\\n    toJSON param =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"name\\\" .= param.paramName\\n            , \\\"in\\\" .= paramLocationToText param.paramIn\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? param.paramDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if param.paramRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"schema\\\" .=? param.paramSchema\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parameter location: path, query, header, or formData.\\ndata ParamLocation\\n    = ParamInPath\\n    | ParamInQuery\\n    | ParamInHeader\\n    | ParamInFormData\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a parameter location from text.\\nparseParamLocation :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocation \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocation \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocation \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocation other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\n-- | Convert a parameter location to text.\\nparamLocationToText :: ParamLocation -> Text\\nparamLocationToText ParamInPath = \\\"path\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInQuery = \\\"query\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInHeader = \\\"header\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInFormData = \\\"formData\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Request body\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a request body for an operation.\\ndata RequestBody = RequestBody\\n    { reqBodyDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , reqBodyRequired :: Bool\\n    , reqBodyContent :: Map MediaType Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON RequestBody where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"RequestBody\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        contentObj <- o .: \\\"content\\\"\\n        content <- parseContentMap contentObj\\n        RequestBody\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> pure content\\n\\ninstance ToJSON RequestBody where\\n    toJSON rb =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"description\\\" .=? rb.reqBodyDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if rb.reqBodyRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"content\\\" .= KeyMap.fromList (map mediaTypeSchemaToPair $ Map.toList rb.reqBodyContent)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parse a content map (media type -> media type object with schema).\\nparseContentMap :: Value -> Parser (Map MediaType Schema)\\nparseContentMap = withObject \\\"content\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMediaTypeSchema o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single media type entry.\\nparseMediaTypeSchema :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (MediaType, Schema)\\nparseMediaTypeSchema o key = do\\n    let mediaType = toText key\\n    mediaObj <- o .: key\\n    schema <- mediaObj .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n    pure (mediaType, schema)\\n\\n-- | Convert media type/schema pair to an Aeson pair.\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair :: (MediaType, Schema) -> Pair\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair (mediaType, schema) =\\n    (fromText mediaType, Aeson.object [\\\"schema\\\" Aeson..= schema])\\n\\n-- | Media type like \\\"application/json\\\" or \\\"application/xml\\\".\\ntype MediaType = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- JSON Schema (subset needed for OpenAPI)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema subset used in OpenAPI.\\n-- Supports common schema types needed for tool generation.\\ndata Schema = Schema\\n    { schemaType :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaEnum :: Maybe [Aeson.Value]\\n    , schemaProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    , schemaItems :: Maybe Schema\\n    , schemaAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema]\\n    , schemaRef :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaRequired :: Maybe [Text]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Schema where\\n    parseJSON v =\\n        parseRefSchema v\\n            <|> parseInlineSchema v\\n\\n-- | Parse a schema that uses $ref.\\nparseRefSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseRefSchema = withObject \\\"Schema ($ref)\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    ref <- o .: \\\"$ref\\\"\\n    pure $\\n        Schema\\n            { schemaType = Nothing\\n            , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n            , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n            , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n            , schemaItems = Nothing\\n            , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n            , schemaRef = Just ref\\n            , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Parse an inline schema definition.\\nparseInlineSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseInlineSchema = withObject \\\"Schema\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    mType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mProps <- o .:? \\\"properties\\\"\\n    -- Convert properties from HashMap to Map if present\\n    let props = fmap HashMap.toList mProps >>= \\\\pairs ->\\n            if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n    Schema\\n        <$> pure mType\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"enum\\\"\\n        <*> pure props\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"items\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"anyOf\\\"\\n        <*> pure Nothing\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Schema where\\n    toJSON schema =\\n        case schema.schemaRef of\\n            Just ref -> Aeson.object [\\\"$ref\\\" Aeson..= ref]\\n            Nothing ->\\n                object\\n                    [ \\\"type\\\" .=? schema.schemaType\\n                    , \\\"description\\\" .=? schema.schemaDescription\\n                    , \\\"enum\\\" .=? schema.schemaEnum\\n                    , \\\"properties\\\" .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaPropertyToPair . Map.toList) schema.schemaProperties)\\n                    , \\\"items\\\" .=? schema.schemaItems\\n                    , \\\"anyOf\\\" .=? schema.schemaAnyOf\\n                    , \\\"required\\\" .=? schema.schemaRequired\\n                    ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema property to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaPropertyToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaPropertyToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Components section for reusable schemas\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Components section containing reusable schemas and other definitions.\\ndata Components = Components\\n    { componentsSchemas :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Components where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Components\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        mSchemas <- o .:? \\\"schemas\\\"\\n        -- Convert from HashMap to Map\\n        let schemas = mSchemas >>= \\\\hm ->\\n                let pairs = HashMap.toList hm\\n                 in if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n        pure $ Components schemas\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Components where\\n    toJSON comps =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"schemas\\\"\\n                .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaDefinitionToPair . Map.toList) comps.componentsSchemas)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema definition to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaDefinitionToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaDefinitionToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool execution result\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Result of executing an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This type captures the structured result from executing an OpenAPI-based\\n-- tool call, including the HTTP response details.\\ndata ToolResult = ToolResult\\n    { resultPath :: Text\\n    -- ^ Path that was called\\n    , resultMethod :: Text\\n    -- ^ HTTP method used\\n    , resultStatus :: Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status code\\n    , resultPayload :: Value\\n    -- ^ Response payload as JSON\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON ToolResult where\\n    toJSON tr =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"path\\\" Aeson..= resultPath tr\\n            , \\\"method\\\" Aeson..= resultMethod tr\\n            , \\\"status\\\" Aeson..= resultStatus tr\\n            , \\\"payload\\\" Aeson..= resultPayload tr\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper functions for JSON construction\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Optional field helper - only include if value is present.\\n(.=?) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> Maybe v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=?) k v = fmap ((Aeson..=) k) v\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=?\\n\\n-- | Required field helper.\\n(.=) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=) k v = Just ((Aeson..=) k v)\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=\\n\\n\""
```

```
"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 specification types and JSON parsers.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a subset of OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 and Swagger 2.0 types needed for\\n-- LLM tool generation. It focuses on parsing paths, operations,\\n-- parameters, request bodies, and schemas.\\n--\\n-- Note: This is not a full OpenAPI implementation - only the subset\\n-- needed for tool generation is supported.\\n--\\n-- The module automatically converts Swagger 2.0 specs to OpenAPI 3.0 structure:\\n-- * 'definitions' -> 'components/schemas'\\n-- * 'in: body' parameters -> 'requestBody'\\n-- * parameter-level 'type' -> nested 'schema.type'\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    -- * OpenAPI spec types\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Method,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n    Components (..),\\n    MediaType,\\n\\n    -- * Tool execution result\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n\\n    -- * JSON helpers\\n    Pair,\\n    object,\\n    parseParamLocation,\\n    paramLocationToText,\\n    (.=?),\\n    (.=),\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Applicative ((<|>))\\nimport Data.Aeson (FromJSON (..), ToJSON (..), Value (..), decode, withObject, (.:), (.:?))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Aeson.Key (fromText, toText, Key)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport Data.Aeson.Types (Parser, parseEither)\\nimport qualified Data.HashMap.Strict as HashMap\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (catMaybes, fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\n-- | Aeson Pair type for building JSON objects.\\ntype Pair = (Key, Value)\\n\\n-- | Build JSON object from a list of optional pairs.\\nobject :: [Maybe Pair] -> Value\\nobject = Aeson.object . catMaybes\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Top-level OpenAPI spec\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Top-level OpenAPI specification document.\\n-- Supports both OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 formats.\\ndata OpenAPISpec = OpenAPISpec\\n    { specPaths :: Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n    , specComponents :: Maybe Components\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"OpenAPISpec\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        -- Check for Swagger 2.0 vs OpenAPI 3.x\\n        mSwagger <- o .:? \\\"swagger\\\"\\n        mOpenapi <- o .:? \\\"openapi\\\"\\n\\n        case (mSwagger :: Maybe Text, mOpenapi :: Maybe Text) of\\n            (Just \\\"2.0\\\", _) -> parseSwaggerV2 o\\n            (_, Just _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o\\n            (_, _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o  -- Default to OpenAPI 3.x parsing\\n\\n-- | Parse OpenAPI 3.x format.\\nparseOpenAPIV3 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseOpenAPIV3 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    paths <- parsePaths pathsObj\\n    components <- o .:? \\\"components\\\"\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 format and convert to OpenAPI 3.x structure.\\nparseSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseSwaggerV2 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    -- Parse paths with Swagger 2.0 conversion\\n    paths <- parsePathsSwaggerV2 pathsObj\\n    -- Convert definitions to components/schemas\\n    definitions <- o .:? \\\"definitions\\\"\\n    let components = fmap definitionsToComponents definitions\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 definitions to Components.\\ndefinitionsToComponents :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Components\\ndefinitionsToComponents defs =\\n    let schemas = HashMap.toList defs\\n        schemaMap = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe parseDefinition schemas\\n    in Components (if Map.null schemaMap then Nothing else Just schemaMap)\\n  where\\n    parseDefinition :: (Key, Value) -> Maybe (Text, Schema)\\n    parseDefinition (k, v) =\\n        case Aeson.fromJSON v of\\n            Aeson.Success schema -> Just (toText k, schema)\\n            Aeson.Error _ -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Parse the paths object where each path maps to a methods object.\\nparsePaths :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePaths = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItem o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item from the paths object.\\nparsePathItem :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItem o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperations val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse an operations object (the value of a path).\\n-- This contains the HTTP methods like \\\"get\\\", \\\"post\\\", etc.\\nparseOperations :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperations = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperation o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    -- Skip OpenAPI extension fields (starting with \\\"x-\\\") and other non-method fields\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"servers\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation.\\nparseMethodOperation :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperation o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    op <- o .: key\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse paths for Swagger 2.0, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItemSwaggerV2 o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item for Swagger 2.0.\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperationsSwaggerV2 val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse operations for Swagger 2.0, converting body params to requestBody.\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation for Swagger 2.0.\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    opVal <- o .: key\\n    op <- parseOperationSwaggerV2 opVal\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 operation, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Operation\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    operationId <- o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n    summary <- o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n    description <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    rawParams <- fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\"\\n\\n    -- Convert parameters: separate body params from regular params\\n    let (bodyParams, otherParams) = partitionBodyParams rawParams\\n\\n    -- Convert body params to requestBody if present\\n    let mRequestBody = case bodyParams of\\n            [] -> Nothing\\n            (bodyParam : _) -> Just $ swaggerBodyParamToRequestBody bodyParam\\n\\n    -- Parse other parameters\\n    regularParams <- traverse parseParameterSwaggerV2 otherParams\\n\\n    pure $ Operation operationId summary description regularParams mRequestBody\\n  where\\n    partitionBodyParams :: [Value] -> ([SwaggerBodyParam], [Value])\\n    partitionBodyParams = foldr classify ([], [])\\n      where\\n        classify v (bodies, others) =\\n            case parseMaybe parseSwaggerBodyParam v of\\n                Just bp -> (bp : bodies, others)\\n                Nothing -> (bodies, v : others)\\n\\n    parseMaybe :: (Value -> Parser a) -> Value -> Maybe a\\n    parseMaybe p v = either (const Nothing) Just (parseEither p v)\\n\\n-- | Temporary type for Swagger 2.0 body parameters.\\ndata SwaggerBodyParam = SwaggerBodyParam\\n    { sbpDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , sbpRequired :: Bool\\n    , sbpSchema :: Schema\\n    }\\n\\n-- | Parse a Swagger 2.0 body parameter.\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam :: Value -> Parser SwaggerBodyParam\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam = withObject \\\"body parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    paramIn <- o .: \\\"in\\\" :: Parser Text\\n    if paramIn == \\\"body\\\"\\n        then do\\n            desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n            schema <- o .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n            pure $ SwaggerBodyParam desc required schema\\n        else fail \\\"Not a body parameter\\\"\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 body parameter to RequestBody.\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody :: SwaggerBodyParam -> RequestBody\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody sbp =\\n    let content = Map.singleton \\\"application/json\\\" (sbpSchema sbp)\\n     in RequestBody (sbpDescription sbp) (sbpRequired sbp) content\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 parameter (supports both direct type and schema).\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Parameter\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 v = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" (\\\\o -> do\\n    paramInStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n    paramIn <- parseParamLocationSwaggerV2 paramInStr\\n    name <- o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n    desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\n    -- Swagger 2.0 can have type directly or in schema\\n    mDirectType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mSchema <- o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\n    -- Build schema from direct type if present\\n    let schema = case (mSchema, mDirectType) of\\n            (Just s, _) -> Just s\\n            (Nothing, Just t) -> Just $ Schema\\n                { schemaType = Just t\\n                , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n                , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n                , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n                , schemaItems = Nothing\\n                , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n                , schemaRef = Nothing\\n                , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n                }\\n            (Nothing, Nothing) -> Nothing\\n\\n    pure $ Parameter name paramIn desc required schema\\n    ) v\\n\\n-- | Parse parameter location for Swagger 2.0 (includes formData).\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"formData\\\" = pure ParamInFormData\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    toJSON spec =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"paths\\\" .= specPaths spec\\n            , \\\"components\\\" .=? specComponents spec\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path and method types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | URL path like \\\"/pets\\\" or \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\".\\ntype Path = Text\\n\\n-- | HTTP method like \\\"GET\\\", \\\"POST\\\", \\\"PUT\\\", \\\"DELETE\\\", etc.\\ntype Method = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation (an endpoint)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents an API operation (endpoint).\\n-- Corresponds to an Operation Object in OpenAPI.\\ndata Operation = Operation\\n    { opOperationId :: Maybe Text\\n    , opSummary :: Maybe Text\\n    , opDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , opParameters :: [Parameter]\\n    , opRequestBody :: Maybe RequestBody\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Operation where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        Operation\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"requestBody\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Operation where\\n    toJSON op =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"operationId\\\" .=? op.opOperationId\\n            , \\\"summary\\\" .=? op.opSummary\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? op.opDescription\\n            , \\\"parameters\\\" .=? if null op.opParameters then Nothing else Just op.opParameters\\n            , \\\"requestBody\\\" .=? op.opRequestBody\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter (path, query, header, formData)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parameter (path, query, header, or formData parameter).\\ndata Parameter = Parameter\\n    { paramName :: Text\\n    , paramIn :: ParamLocation\\n    , paramDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , paramRequired :: Bool\\n    , paramSchema :: Maybe Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Parameter where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        locationStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n        location <- parseParamLocation locationStr\\n        Parameter\\n            <$> o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n            <*> pure location\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Parameter where\\n    toJSON param =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"name\\\" .= param.paramName\\n            , \\\"in\\\" .= paramLocationToText param.paramIn\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? param.paramDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if param.paramRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"schema\\\" .=? param.paramSchema\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parameter location: path, query, header, or formData.\\ndata ParamLocation\\n    = ParamInPath\\n    | ParamInQuery\\n    | ParamInHeader\\n    | ParamInFormData\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a parameter location from text.\\nparseParamLocation :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocation \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocation \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocation \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocation other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\n-- | Convert a parameter location to text.\\nparamLocationToText :: ParamLocation -> Text\\nparamLocationToText ParamInPath = \\\"path\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInQuery = \\\"query\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInHeader = \\\"header\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInFormData = \\\"formData\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Request body\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a request body for an operation.\\ndata RequestBody = RequestBody\\n    { reqBodyDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , reqBodyRequired :: Bool\\n    , reqBodyContent :: Map MediaType Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON RequestBody where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"RequestBody\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        contentObj <- o .: \\\"content\\\"\\n        content <- parseContentMap contentObj\\n        RequestBody\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> pure content\\n\\ninstance ToJSON RequestBody where\\n    toJSON rb =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"description\\\" .=? rb.reqBodyDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if rb.reqBodyRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"content\\\" .= KeyMap.fromList (map mediaTypeSchemaToPair $ Map.toList rb.reqBodyContent)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parse a content map (media type -> media type object with schema).\\nparseContentMap :: Value -> Parser (Map MediaType Schema)\\nparseContentMap = withObject \\\"content\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMediaTypeSchema o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single media type entry.\\nparseMediaTypeSchema :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (MediaType, Schema)\\nparseMediaTypeSchema o key = do\\n    let mediaType = toText key\\n    mediaObj <- o .: key\\n    schema <- mediaObj .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n    pure (mediaType, schema)\\n\\n-- | Convert media type/schema pair to an Aeson pair.\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair :: (MediaType, Schema) -> Pair\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair (mediaType, schema) =\\n    (fromText mediaType, Aeson.object [\\\"schema\\\" Aeson..= schema])\\n\\n-- | Media type like \\\"application/json\\\" or \\\"application/xml\\\".\\ntype MediaType = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- JSON Schema (subset needed for OpenAPI)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema subset used in OpenAPI.\\n-- Supports common schema types needed for tool generation.\\ndata Schema = Schema\\n    { schemaType :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaEnum :: Maybe [Aeson.Value]\\n    , schemaProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    , schemaItems :: Maybe Schema\\n    , schemaAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema]\\n    , schemaRef :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaRequired :: Maybe [Text]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Schema where\\n    parseJSON v =\\n        parseRefSchema v\\n            <|> parseInlineSchema v\\n\\n-- | Parse a schema that uses $ref.\\nparseRefSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseRefSchema = withObject \\\"Schema ($ref)\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    ref <- o .: \\\"$ref\\\"\\n    pure $\\n        Schema\\n            { schemaType = Nothing\\n            , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n            , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n            , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n            , schemaItems = Nothing\\n            , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n            , schemaRef = Just ref\\n            , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Parse an inline schema definition.\\nparseInlineSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseInlineSchema = withObject \\\"Schema\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    mType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mProps <- o .:? \\\"properties\\\"\\n    -- Convert properties from HashMap to Map if present\\n    let props = fmap HashMap.toList mProps >>= \\\\pairs ->\\n            if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n    Schema\\n        <$> pure mType\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"enum\\\"\\n        <*> pure props\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"items\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"anyOf\\\"\\n        <*> pure Nothing\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Schema where\\n    toJSON schema =\\n        case schema.schemaRef of\\n            Just ref -> Aeson.object [\\\"$ref\\\" Aeson..= ref]\\n            Nothing ->\\n                object\\n                    [ \\\"type\\\" .=? schema.schemaType\\n                    , \\\"description\\\" .=? schema.schemaDescription\\n                    , \\\"enum\\\" .=? schema.schemaEnum\\n                    , \\\"properties\\\" .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaPropertyToPair . Map.toList) schema.schemaProperties)\\n                    , \\\"items\\\" .=? schema.schemaItems\\n                    , \\\"anyOf\\\" .=? schema.schemaAnyOf\\n                    , \\\"required\\\" .=? schema.schemaRequired\\n                    ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema property to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaPropertyToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaPropertyToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Components section for reusable schemas\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Components section containing reusable schemas and other definitions.\\ndata Components = Components\\n    { componentsSchemas :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Components where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Components\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        mSchemas <- o .:? \\\"schemas\\\"\\n        -- Convert from HashMap to Map\\n        let schemas = mSchemas >>= \\\\hm ->\\n                let pairs = HashMap.toList hm\\n                 in if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n        pure $ Components schemas\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Components where\\n    toJSON comps =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"schemas\\\"\\n                .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaDefinitionToPair . Map.toList) comps.componentsSchemas)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema definition to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaDefinitionToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaDefinitionToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool execution result\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Result of executing an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This type captures the structured result from executing an OpenAPI-based\\n-- tool call, including the HTTP response details.\\ndata ToolResult = ToolResult\\n    { resultPath :: Text\\n    -- ^ Path that was called\\n    , resultMethod :: Text\\n    -- ^ HTTP method used\\n    , resultStatus :: Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status code\\n    , resultPayload :: Value\\n    -- ^ Response payload as JSON\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON ToolResult where\\n    toJSON tr =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"path\\\" Aeson..= resultPath tr\\n            , \\\"method\\\" Aeson..= resultMethod tr\\n            , \\\"status\\\" Aeson..= resultStatus tr\\n            , \\\"payload\\\" Aeson..= resultPayload tr\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper functions for JSON construction\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Optional field helper - only include if value is present.\\n(.=?) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> Maybe v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=?) k v = fmap ((Aeson..=) k) v\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=?\\n\\n-- | Required field helper.\\n(.=) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=) k v = Just ((Aeson..=) k v)\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=\\n\\n\""
```

### Response

All 135 tests pass, including the new Swagger 2.0 tests. Let me provide a summary:

## Summary

I added support for parsing Swagger 2.0 (OpenAPI v2) specifications in the OpenAPI toolbox, along with tests verifying the fix.

### Changes Made:

1. **`src/System/Agents/Tools/OpenAPI/Types.hs`**:
   - Added support for detecting Swagger 2.0 specs (checks for `"swagger": "2.0"`)
   - Converts Swagger 2.0 `definitions` to OpenAPI 3.0 `components/schemas`
   - Converts Swagger 2.0 `in: body` parameters to `requestBody` structure
   - Converts Swagger 2.0 parameters with direct `type` field to nested `schema.type`
   - Added new `ParamInFormData` constructor for `formData` parameters

2. **`src/System/Agents/Tools/OpenAPI/Resolver.hs`**:
   - Updated `parseRef` to support both OpenAPI 3.x refs (`#/components/schemas/Name`) and Swagger 2.0 refs (`#/definitions/Name`)

3. **`test/OpenAPIToolboxTests.hs`**:
   - Added new test group "Swagger 2.0 Support" with 5 tests:
     - `test_parseSwaggerV2Spec`: Verifies the v2 spec can be parsed
     - `test_swaggerV2Definitions`: Checks that definitions are accessible as components
     - `test_swaggerV2BodyParam`: Verifies body params are converted to requestBody
     - `test_swaggerV2FormDataParam`: Ensures formData params are handled
     - `test_swaggerV2InitializeToolbox`: Tests that the full conversion pipeline works

### Test Results:
All 135 tests pass, including the new Swagger 2.0 tests that verify the petstore-v2.json spec can be correctly parsed and tools can be generated from it.

spec\\n    | CallingEndpointTrace !Text !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ method, url, path being called\\n    | EndpointResponseTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of endpoint response\\n    | SchemaResolutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during schema dereferencing\\n    | ToolExecutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during tool execution\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during toolbox initialization.\\ndata InitializationError\\n    = NetworkError !Text\\n    -- ^ Network error fetching spec\\n    | ParseError !Text\\n    -- ^ JSON parse error\\n    | SpecError !Text\\n    -- ^ OpenAPI spec validation error\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Configuration for initializing an OpenAPI toolbox.\\ndata Config = Config\\n    { configUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ URL to -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Initialize an OpenAPI toolbox from a configuration.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Converts operations to tools\\n--\\n-- Returns an 'InitializationError' if any step fails.\\ninitializeToolbox ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    Config ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError Toolbox)\\ninitializeToolbox tracer config = do\\n    -- Create HTTP runtime with auth\\n    let token = case config.configToken of\\n            Just t -> HttpClient.BearerToken t\\n            Nothing -> HttpClient.NoToken\\n    runtime <- HttpClient.newRuntime token\\n\\n    -- Fetch OpenAPI spec\\n    runTracer tracer (FetchingSpecTrace config.configUrl)\\n    fetchResult <- fetchSpec runtime config.configUrl\\n\\n    case fetchResult of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right (status, body) -> do\\n            runTracer tracer (SpecFetchedTrace status)\\n\\n convertOpenAPIToTools dereferencedSpec\\n                    runTracer tracer (ToolsConvertedTrace (length tools))\\n\\n                    -- Create toolbox\\n                    pure $ Right $ Toolbox\\n                        { toolboxName = Text.takeWhile (/= '/') (Text.drop 8 config.configUrl)\\n                            -- Use part of URL as name, removing protocol\\n (e :: HttpClient.HttpException) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show e)\\n        Right (Left err) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show err)\\n        Right (Right response) ->\\n            let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n                body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n             in pure $ Right (status, body)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Handler Creation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Creates a handler function for an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This handler integrates with the ToolExecutionContext system and\\n-- returns results in the standard CallResult format.\\ncreateToolHandler ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tracer IO ToolTrace ->\\n    ToolExecutionContext ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (CallResult ())\\ncreateToolHandler toolbox tool tracer _ctx args = do\\n    result <- handleToolCall toolbox tool args\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> do\\n            runTracer (contramapTrace tracer) (ToolExecutionErrorTrace err)\\n            pure $ IOToolError () (ScriptExecutionError (Text.unpack err))\\n        Right (textResult, _toolResult) -> do\\n            pure $ BlobToolSuccess () (ByteString.pack $ Text.unpack textResult)\\n  where\\n    contramapTrace :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> Tracer IO Trace\\n    contramapTrace _t = Tracer $ \\\\_traceEvent -> do\\n        -- Convert Trace to ToolTrace if needed\\n        -- For now, we just don't trace these events to the main tool tracer\\n        pure ()\\n\\n-- | Handle a tool call by executing the HTTP request.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Extracts path params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 2. Extracts query params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 3. Extracts body (param \\\"b\\\")\\n-- 4. Formats the path with path args\\n-- 5. Builds full URL\\n-- 6. Makes HTTP request\\n-- 7. Returns result\\nhandleToolCall ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleToolCall toolbox tool args = do\\n    case args of\\n (toolOperation tool)\\n\\n            -- Format the path with path parameters\\n            let formattedPath = formatPath (toolPath tool) pathParams\\n\\n            -- Build full URL\\n            let fullUrl = buildFullUrl (toolboxBaseUrl toolbox) formattedPath\\n\\n            -- Get headers\\n            headers <- case toolbox.headerFunc of\\n                Just hf -> hf\\n                Nothing -> pure Map.empty\\n            let allHeaders = Map.union headers (staticHeaders toolbox)\\n\\n            -- Make HTTP request\\n            let method = toolMethod tool\\n            runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ CallingEndpointTrace method fullUrl formattedPath\\n\\n            result <- executeRequest toolbox.httpRuntime method fullUrl queryParams bodyValue allHeaders\\n\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n                Right (textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                    runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ EndpointResponseTrace (resultStatus toolResult)\\n                    pure $ Right (textResult, toolResult)\\n        _ -> pure $ Left \\\"Tool arguments must be a JSON object\\\"\\n\\n-- | Extract parameters from the JSON object based on operation definition.\\nextractParams ::\\n    Map Text Value ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    (Map Text Text, Map Text Text, Maybe Value)\\nextractParams obj op =\\n    let params = opParameters op\\n        -- Extract path params\\n        pathParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractPathParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n extractPathParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract query params\\n        queryParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractQueryParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractQueryParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractQueryParam k v acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) (valueToText v) acc\\n            extractQueryParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract body\\n        bodyValue = Map.lookup \\\"b\\\" obj\\n     in (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue)\\n\\n-- | Find a parameter by name in the operation parameters list.\\nfindParam :: [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert a JSON value to Text for query params.\\nvalueToText :: Value -> Text\\nvalueToText (String s) = s\\nvalueToText (Number n) = Text.pack $ show n\\nvalueToText (Bool b) = if b then \\\"true\\\" else \\\"false\\\"\\nvalueToText Null = \\\"null\\\"\\nvalueToText _ = \\\"\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path Formatting\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Format a path template with path parameters.\\n--\\n-- Converts a path like @/pets/{petId}@ with @{p_petId: \\\"123\\\"}@ to @/pets/123@.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\nformatPath :: Path -> Map Text Text -> Path\\nformatPath pathTemplate pathArgs =\\n    Map.foldrWithKey replaceParam pathTemplate pathArgs\\n  where\\n    replaceParam :: Text -> Text -> Path -> Path\\n    replaceParam name value path =\\n        Text.replace (\\\"{\\\" <> name <> \\\"}\\\") value path\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- URL Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the full URL from base URL and path.\\nbuildFullUrl :: Text -> Path -> Text\\nbuildFullUrl baseUrl path =\\n    let base = Text.dropWhileEnd (== '/') baseUrl\\n        path' = if Text.isPrefixOf \\\"/\\\" path then path else \\\"/\\\" <> path\\n     in base <> path'\\n\\n-- | Build query string from query parameters.\\nbuildQueryString :: Map Text Text -> Text\\nbuildQueryString params\\n    | Map.null params = \\\"\\\"\\n    | otherwise =\\n        \\\"?\\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\"&\\\" (map encodeParam $ Map.toList params)\\n  where\\n    encodeParam (k, v) = urlEncode k <> \\\"=\\\" <> urlEncode v\\n    urlEncode = id -- Simplified - real implementation would URL-encode\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- HTTP Request Building and Execution\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build an HTTP request.\\nbuildRequest ::\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO HttpClient.Request\\nbuildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    let queryString = buildQueryString queryParams\\n    let urlWithQuery = fullUrl <> queryString\\n\\n    req <- HttpClient.parseRequest (Text.unpack urlWithQuery)\\n\\n    -- Set method\\n    let reqWithMethod = req{HttpClient.method = Text.encodeUtf8 method}\\n\\n    -- Add body if present\\n    let reqWithBody = case mbody of\\n            Just body ->\\n                reqWithMethod\\n                    { HttpClient.requestBody = HttpClient.RequestBodyLBS (Aeson.encode body)\\n                    , HttpClient.requestHeaders =\\n                        (\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n                            : HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithMethod\\n                    }\\n            Nothing -> reqWithMethod\\n\\n    -- Add custom headers\\n    let headerList = map (\\\\(k, v) -> (CI.mk (Text.encodeUtf8 k), Text.encodeUtf8 v)) $ Map.toList headers\\n    let finalReq = reqWithBody{HttpClient.requestHeaders = HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithBody ++ headerList}\\n\\n    pure finalReq\\n\\n-- | Execute an HTTP request.\\nexecuteRequest ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nexecuteRequest runtime method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    _ <- buildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers\\n\\n    -- Use the runtime to make the request\\n    -- Note: We're using a simplified approach here\\n    -- In a real implementation, we'd use the HttpClient.Runtime properly\\n    let tracer = Tracer (const (pure ()))\\n    result <- executeRequest' runtime tracer method fullUrl mbody\\n\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right response -> handleResponse toolInfo response\\n  where\\n    toolInfo = (method, fullUrl)\\n    executeRequest' rt tr m url mb\\n        | m == \\\"GET\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.get rt tr url\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | m == \\\"POST\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.post rt tr url mb\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | otherwise = do\\n            -- For other methods, we need to use the underlying manager\\n            pure $ Left $ \\\"Method not supported: \\\" <> m\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Response Handling\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handle an HTTP response, converting to ToolResult.\\n--\\n-- Returns both text for the LLM and a structured ToolResult.\\nhandleResponse ::\\n    (Method, Text) ->\\n    HttpClient.Response LByteString.ByteString ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleResponse (method, url) response = do\\n    let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n    let body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n\\n    -- Parse body as JSON if possible\\n    let payload = case Aeson.decode body of\\n            Just val -> val\\n            Nothing -> String (Text.decodeUtf8 $ LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    -- Create text representation for LLM\\n    let textResult =\\n            \\\"HTTP \\\" <> Text.pack (show status) <> \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n                <> Text.decodeUtf8 (LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    pure $ Right\\n        ( textResult\\n        , ToolResult\\n            { resultPath = url\\n            , resultMethod = method\\n            , resultStatus = status\\n            , resultPayload = payload\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get operation ID from operation, falling back to tool name.\\n--\\n-- This is used for tool registration when creating unique tool names.\\ngetOperationId :: Operation -> Maybe Text\\ngetOperationId = opOperationId\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Naming helper\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Convert an OpenAPI operation ID to an LLM tool name.\\n--\\n-- Names are prefixed with @openapi_@ and include the toolbox name\\n-- to avoid conflicts.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_getPet\\\"}\\nopenapi2LLMName :: Text -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nopenapi2LLMName tboxName operationId =\\n    OpenAI.ToolName (\\\"openapi_\\\" <> tboxName <> \\\"_\\\" <> operationId)\\n\\n\""
