"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI Toolbox runtime for executing tools from OpenAPI specifications.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a runtime that:\\n-- * Fetches OpenAPI specifications from URLs\\n-- * Converts operations to executable tools\\n-- * Handles HTTP execution with proper parameter substitution\\n-- * Integrates with the LLM tool registration system\\n--\\n-- The toolbox follows patterns similar to McpToolbox and BashToolbox for\\n-- consistent integration with the agents system.\\n--\\n-- Key feature: Name normalization for LLM compatibility.\\n-- OpenAPI operation IDs may contain invalid characters (dots, slashes, etc.)\\n-- which are normalized to LLM-safe names using 'normalizeForLLM'.\\n--\\n-- Example usage:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- import System.Agents\\n-- import qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox as OpenAPI\\n--\\n-- main :: IO ()\\n-- main = do\\n--     let config = OpenAPI.Config\\n--             { configUrl = \\\"https://api.example.com/openapi.json\\\"\\n--             , configBaseUrl = \\\"https://api.example.com\\\"\\n--             , configHeaders = Map.empty\\n--             , configToken = Just \\\"my-bearer-token\\\"\\n--             }\\n--     result <- OpenAPI.initializeToolbox tracer config\\n--     case result of\\n--         Right toolbox -> do\\n--             -- Register tools with LLM\\n--             regResult <- registerOpenAPITools toolbox\\n--             case regResult of\\n--                 Right registrations -> useWithAgent registrations\\n--                 Left err -> handleError err\\n--         Left err -> handleError err\\n-- @\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    Trace (..),\\n    Toolbox (..),\\n    Config (..),\\n    InitializationError (..),\\n    \\n    -- * Re-export ToolResult from Types\\n    ToolResult,\\n    \\n    -- * Initialization\\n    initializeToolbox,\\n    \\n    -- * Tool execution\\n    createToolHandler,\\n    handleToolCall,\\n    \\n    -- * Path formatting\\n    formatPath,\\n    \\n    -- * HTTP building\\n    buildRequest,\\n    buildFullUrl,\\n    \\n    -- * Response handling\\n    handleResponse,\\n    \\n    -- * Operation helpers\\n    getOperationId,\\n    getToolByNormalizedName,\\n    \\n    -- * Naming helpers\\n    openapi2LLMName,\\n    normalizeToolName,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Exception (try)\\nimport Data.Aeson (Value (..))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Char8 as ByteString\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LByteString\\nimport qualified Data.CaseInsensitive as CI\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Client as HttpClient\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Types as HttpTypes\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..), runTracer)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (\\n    OpenAPITool (..),\\n    NameMapping (..),\\n    buildToolNameMapping,\\n    findToolByNormalizedName,\\n    normalizeForLLM,\\n    convertOpenAPIToTools,\\n )\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver (dereferenceSpec)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n  )\\nimport qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Base (CallResult (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolExecutionContext)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.IO (RunError (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Trace (ToolTrace (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Trace types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Trace events for OpenAPI toolbox operations.\\n--\\n-- These events allow monitoring of:\\n-- * Spec fetching progress\\n-- * Tool conversion status\\n-- * Endpoint execution\\n-- * Error conditions\\ndata Trace\\n    = FetchingSpecTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ URL being fetched\\n    | SpecFetchedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of spec fetch\\n    | ToolsConvertedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ Number of tools converted from spec\\n    | CallingEndpointTrace !Text !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ method, url, path being called\\n    | EndpointResponseTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of endpoint response\\n    | SchemaResolutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during schema dereferencing\\n    | ToolExecutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during tool execution\\n    | ToolNameNormalizedTrace !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ Original name and normalized name (for debugging)\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during toolbox initialization.\\ndata InitializationError\\n    = NetworkError !Text\\n    -- ^ Network error fetching spec\\n    | ParseError !Text\\n    -- ^ JSON parse error\\n    | SpecError !Text\\n    -- ^ OpenAPI spec validation error\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Configuration for initializing an OpenAPI toolbox.\\ndata Config = Config\\n    { configUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ URL to fetch OpenAPI spec from\\n    , configBaseUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ Base URL for API calls\\n    , configHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers to include in all requests\\n    , configToken :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ Optional Bearer token for authentication\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Runtime state for an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- The toolbox maintains:\\n-- * A name for identification\\n-- * The base URL for API calls\\n-- * A list of available tools\\n-- * A name mapping for LLM-safe tool names\\n-- * The HTTP runtime for making requests\\n-- * An optional header function for dynamic auth headers\\ndata Toolbox = Toolbox\\n    { toolboxName :: Text\\n    , toolboxBaseUrl :: Text\\n    , toolboxTools :: [OpenAPITool]\\n    -- ^ Tools converted from the OpenAPI spec\\n    , toolboxNameMapping :: Map Text NameMapping\\n    -- ^ Mapping from normalized LLM names to original operation IDs\\n    , httpRuntime :: HttpClient.Runtime\\n    -- ^ HTTP client runtime\\n    , headerFunc :: Maybe (IO (Map Text Text))\\n    -- ^ Optional function to get dynamic headers (e.g., for auth)\\n    , staticHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers from config\\n    }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Initialization\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Initialize an OpenAPI toolbox from a configuration.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Creates an HTTP runtime with authentication\\n-- 2. Fetches the OpenAPI spec from the configured URL\\n-- 3. Parses the JSON to an OpenAPISpec\\n-- 4. Dereferences schema references ($ref)\\n-- 5. Converts operations to tools\\n-- 6. Builds the name mapping for LLM-safe names\\n--\\n-- Returns an 'InitializationError' if any step fails.\\ninitializeToolbox ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    Config ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError Toolbox)\\ninitializeToolbox tracer config = do\\n    -- Create HTTP runtime with auth\\n    let token = case config.configToken of\\n            Just t -> HttpClient.BearerToken t\\n            Nothing -> HttpClient.NoToken\\n    runtime <- HttpClient.newRuntime token\\n\\n    -- Fetch OpenAPI spec\\n    runTracer tracer (FetchingSpecTrace config.configUrl)\\n    fetchResult <- fetchSpec runtime config.configUrl\\n\\n    case fetchResult of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right (status, body) -> do\\n            runTracer tracer (SpecFetchedTrace status)\\n\\n            -- Parse JSON to OpenAPISpec\\n            case Aeson.decode body of\\n                Nothing -> pure $ Left $ ParseError \\\"Failed to parse OpenAPI spec JSON\\\"\\n                Just spec -> do\\n                    -- Dereference schemas\\n                    let dereferencedSpec = dereferenceSpec spec\\n\\n                    -- Convert to tools\\n                    let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools dereferencedSpec\\n                    runTracer tracer (ToolsConvertedTrace (length tools))\\n\\n                    -- Build name mapping for LLM-safe names\\n                    let nameMapping = buildToolNameMapping toolboxName tools\\n                    \\n                    -- Trace name normalizations (for debugging)\\n                    mapM_ (\\\\m -> runTracer tracer (ToolNameNormalizedTrace (nmOriginal m) (nmNormalized m))) \\n                          (Map.elems nameMapping)\\n\\n                    -- Create toolbox\\n                    pure $ Right $ Toolbox\\n                        { toolboxName = toolboxName\\n                        , toolboxBaseUrl = config.configBaseUrl\\n                        , toolboxTools = tools\\n                        , toolboxNameMapping = nameMapping\\n                        , httpRuntime = runtime\\n                        , headerFunc = Nothing\\n                        , staticHeaders = config.configHeaders\\n                        }\\n  where\\n    toolboxName = extractToolboxName config.configUrl\\n\\n-- | Extract a toolbox name from the spec URL.\\nextractToolboxName :: Text -> Text\\nextractToolboxName url = \\n    let withoutProtocol = Text.dropWhile (/= '/') $ Text.drop 8 url\\n        hostPart = Text.takeWhile (/= '/') withoutProtocol\\n    in if Text.null hostPart \\n       then \\\"openapi\\\" \\n       else normalizeForLLM hostPart\\n\\n-- | Fetch the OpenAPI spec from a URL.\\nfetchSpec ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Text ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError (Int, LByteString.ByteString))\\nfetchSpec runtime url = do\\n    result <- try $ HttpClient.get runtime (Tracer (const (pure ()))) url\\n    case result of\\n        Left (e :: HttpClient.HttpException) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show e)\\n        Right (Left err) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show err)\\n        Right (Right response) ->\\n            let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n                body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n             in pure $ Right (status, body)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Lookup\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get a tool by its normalized LLM name.\\n--\\n-- This is used during tool execution to find the original tool\\n-- when the LLM calls a tool by its normalized name.\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Nothing' if no tool with that normalized name exists.\\ngetToolByNormalizedName ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Maybe OpenAPITool\\ngetToolByNormalizedName toolbox normalizedName = do\\n    -- Look up the original operation ID from the mapping\\n    originalOpId <- findToolByNormalizedName (toolboxNameMapping toolbox) normalizedName\\n    -- Find the tool with that original operation ID\\n    find ((== Just originalOpId) . opOperationId . toolOperation) (toolboxTools toolbox)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Handler Creation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Creates a handler function for an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This handler integrates with the ToolExecutionContext system and\\n-- returns results in the standard CallResult format.\\ncreateToolHandler ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tracer IO ToolTrace ->\\n    ToolExecutionContext ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (CallResult ())\\ncreateToolHandler toolbox tool tracer _ctx args = do\\n    result <- handleToolCall toolbox tool args\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> do\\n            runTracer (contramapTrace tracer) (ToolExecutionErrorTrace err)\\n            pure $ IOToolError () (ScriptExecutionError (Text.unpack err))\\n        Right (textResult, _toolResult) -> do\\n            pure $ BlobToolSuccess () (ByteString.pack $ Text.unpack textResult)\\n  where\\n    contramapTrace :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> Tracer IO Trace\\n    contramapTrace _t = Tracer $ \\\\_traceEvent -> do\\n        -- Convert Trace to ToolTrace if needed\\n        -- For now, we just don't trace these events to the main tool tracer\\n        pure ()\\n\\n-- | Handle a tool call by executing the HTTP request.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Extracts path params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 2. Extracts query params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 3. Extracts body (param \\\"b\\\")\\n-- 4. Formats the path with path args\\n-- 5. Builds full URL\\n-- 6. Makes HTTP request\\n-- 7. Returns result\\nhandleToolCall ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleToolCall toolbox tool args = do\\n    case args of\\n        Object obj -> do\\n            -- Extract parameters from the JSON object\\n            let objMap = KeyMap.toMapText obj\\n            let (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue) = extractParams objMap (toolOperation tool)\\n\\n            -- Format the path with path parameters\\n            let formattedPath = formatPath (toolPath tool) pathParams\\n\\n            -- Build full URL\\n            let fullUrl = buildFullUrl (toolboxBaseUrl toolbox) formattedPath\\n\\n            -- Get headers\\n            headers <- case toolbox.headerFunc of\\n                Just hf -> hf\\n                Nothing -> pure Map.empty\\n            let allHeaders = Map.union headers (staticHeaders toolbox)\\n\\n            -- Make HTTP request\\n            let method = toolMethod tool\\n            runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ CallingEndpointTrace method fullUrl formattedPath\\n\\n            result <- executeRequest toolbox.httpRuntime method fullUrl queryParams bodyValue allHeaders\\n\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n                Right (textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                    runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ EndpointResponseTrace (resultStatus toolResult)\\n                    pure $ Right (textResult, toolResult)\\n        _ -> pure $ Left \\\"Tool arguments must be a JSON object\\\"\\n\\n-- | Extract parameters from the JSON object based on operation definition.\\nextractParams ::\\n    Map Text Value ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    (Map Text Text, Map Text Text, Maybe Value)\\nextractParams obj op =\\n    let params = opParameters op\\n        -- Extract path params\\n        pathParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractPathParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractPathParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInPath = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractPathParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract query params\\n        queryParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractQueryParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractQueryParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractQueryParam k v acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) (valueToText v) acc\\n            extractQueryParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract body\\n        bodyValue = Map.lookup \\\"b\\\" obj\\n     in (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue)\\n\\n-- | Find a parameter by name in the operation parameters list.\\nfindParam :: [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert a JSON value to Text for query params.\\nvalueToText :: Value -> Text\\nvalueToText (String s) = s\\nvalueToText (Number n) = Text.pack $ show n\\nvalueToText (Bool b) = if b then \\\"true\\\" else \\\"false\\\"\\nvalueToText Null = \\\"null\\\"\\nvalueToText _ = \\\"\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path Formatting\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Format a path template with path parameters.\\n--\\n-- Converts a path like @/pets/{petId}@ with @{p_petId: \\\"123\\\"}@ to @/pets/123@.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\nformatPath :: Path -> Map Text Text -> Path\\nformatPath pathTemplate pathArgs =\\n    Map.foldrWithKey replaceParam pathTemplate pathArgs\\n  where\\n    replaceParam :: Text -> Text -> Path -> Path\\n    replaceParam name value path =\\n        Text.replace (\\\"{\\\" <> name <> \\\"}\\\") value path\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- URL Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the full URL from base URL and path.\\nbuildFullUrl :: Text -> Path -> Text\\nbuildFullUrl baseUrl path =\\n    let base = Text.dropWhileEnd (== '/') baseUrl\\n        path' = if Text.isPrefixOf \\\"/\\\" path then path else \\\"/\\\" <> path\\n     in base <> path'\\n\\n-- | Build query string from query parameters.\\nbuildQueryString :: Map Text Text -> Text\\nbuildQueryString params\\n    | Map.null params = \\\"\\\"\\n    | otherwise =\\n        \\\"?\\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\"&\\\" (map encodeParam $ Map.toList params)\\n  where\\n    encodeParam (k, v) = urlEncode k <> \\\"=\\\" <> urlEncode v\\n    urlEncode = id -- Simplified - real implementation would URL-encode\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- HTTP Request Building and Execution\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build an HTTP request.\\nbuildRequest ::\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO HttpClient.Request\\nbuildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    let queryString = buildQueryString queryParams\\n    let urlWithQuery = fullUrl <> queryString\\n\\n    req <- HttpClient.parseRequest (Text.unpack urlWithQuery)\\n\\n    -- Set method\\n    let reqWithMethod = req{HttpClient.method = Text.encodeUtf8 method}\\n\\n    -- Add body if present\\n    let reqWithBody = case mbody of\\n            Just body ->\\n                reqWithMethod\\n                    { HttpClient.requestBody = HttpClient.RequestBodyLBS (Aeson.encode body)\\n                    , HttpClient.requestHeaders =\\n                        (\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n                            : HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithMethod\\n                    }\\n            Nothing -> reqWithMethod\\n\\n    -- Add custom headers\\n    let headerList = map (\\\\(k, v) -> (CI.mk (Text.encodeUtf8 k), Text.encodeUtf8 v)) $ Map.toList headers\\n    let finalReq = reqWithBody{HttpClient.requestHeaders = HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithBody ++ headerList}\\n\\n    pure finalReq\\n\\n-- | Execute an HTTP request.\\nexecuteRequest ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nexecuteRequest runtime method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    _ <- buildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers\\n\\n    -- Use the runtime to make the request\\n    -- Note: We're using a simplified approach here\\n    -- In a real implementation, we'd use the HttpClient.Runtime properly\\n    let tracer = Tracer (const (pure ()))\\n    result <- executeRequest' runtime tracer method fullUrl mbody\\n\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right response -> handleResponse toolInfo response\\n  where\\n    toolInfo = (method, fullUrl)\\n    executeRequest' rt tr m url mb\\n        | m == \\\"GET\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.get rt tr url\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | m == \\\"POST\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.post rt tr url mb\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | otherwise = do\\n            -- For other methods, we need to use the underlying manager\\n            pure $ Left $ \\\"Method not supported: \\\" <> m\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Response Handling\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handle an HTTP response, converting to ToolResult.\\n--\\n-- Returns both text for the LLM and a structured ToolResult.\\nhandleResponse ::\\n    (Method, Text) ->\\n    HttpClient.Response LByteString.ByteString ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleResponse (method, url) response = do\\n    let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n    let body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n\\n    -- Parse body as JSON if possible\\n    let payload = case Aeson.decode body of\\n            Just val -> val\\n            Nothing -> String (Text.decodeUtf8 $ LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    -- Create text representation for LLM\\n    let textResult =\\n            \\\"HTTP \\\" <> Text.pack (show status) <> \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n                <> Text.decodeUtf8 (LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    pure $ Right\\n        ( textResult\\n        , ToolResult\\n            { resultPath = url\\n            , resultMethod = method\\n            , resultStatus = status\\n            , resultPayload = payload\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get operation ID from operation, falling back to tool name.\\n--\\n-- This is used for tool registration when creating unique tool names.\\ngetOperationId :: Operation -> Maybe Text\\ngetOperationId = opOperationId\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Naming helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Normalize a tool name for LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- This is a re-export of 'normalizeForLLM' for convenience.\\nnormalizeToolName :: Text -> Text\\nnormalizeToolName = normalizeForLLM\\n\\n-- | Convert an OpenAPI operation ID to an LLM tool name.\\n--\\n-- Names are prefixed with @openapi_@ and include the normalized toolbox name\\n-- and normalized operation ID to avoid conflicts and ensure LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- The operation ID is normalized to replace invalid characters:\\n-- - Dots (.) become underscores (_)\\n-- - Slashes (/) become underscores (_)\\n-- - Other invalid characters become underscores\\n-- - Names starting with digits are prefixed with 't'\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_getPet\\\"}\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"pet.findByStatus\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_pet_findByStatus\\\"}\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"2.0/getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_t2_0_getPet\\\"}\\nopenapi2LLMName :: Text -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nopenapi2LLMName tboxName operationId =\\n    let normalizedToolbox = normalizeForLLM tboxName\\n        normalizedOpId = normalizeForLLM operationId\\n    in OpenAI.ToolName (\\\"openapi_\\\" <> normalizedToolbox <> \\\"_\\\" <> normalizedOpId)\\n\\n\""

specifications.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a runtime that:\\n-- * Fetches OpenAPI specifications from URLs\\n-- * Converts operations to executable tools\\n-- * Handles HTTP execution with proper parameter substitution\\n-- * Integrates with the LLM tool registration system\\n--\\n-- The toolbox follows patterns similar to McpToolbox and BashToolbox for\\n-- consistent integration with the agents system.\\n--\\n-- Key feature: Name normalization for LLM compatibility.\\n-- OpenAPI operation IDs may contain invalid characters (dots, slashes, etc.)\\n-- which are normalized to LLM-safe names using 'normalizeForLLM'.\\n--\\n-- Example usage:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- import System.Agents\\n-- import qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox as OpenAPI\\n--\\n-- main :: IO ()\\n-- main = registrations -> useWithAgent registrations\\n--                 Left err -> handleError err\\n--         Left err -> handleError err\\n-- @\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    Trace (..),\\n    Toolbox (..),\\n    Config (..),\\n    InitializationError (..),\\n    \\n    -- * Re-export ToolResult from Types\\n    ToolResult,\\n    \\n    -- * Initialization\\n    initializeToolbox,\\n    \\n    -- * Tool execution\\n    createToolHandler,\\n    handleToolCall,\\n    \\n    -- * Path formatting\\n    formatPath,\\n    \\n    -- * HTTP building\\n    buildRequest,\\n    buildFullUrl,\\n    \\n    -- * Response handling\\n    handleResponse,\\n    \\n    -- * Operation helpers\\n    getOperationId,\\n    getToolByNormalizedName,\\n qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Client as HttpClient\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Types as HttpTypes\\nimport Prod.Tracer normalizeForLLM,\\n    convertOpenAPIToTools,\\n )\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver (dereferenceSpec)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n  )\\nimport qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Base (CallResult (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolExecutionContext)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.IO (RunError (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Trace (ToolTrace (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Trace types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Trace events for OpenAPI toolbox operations.\\n--\\n-- These events allow monitoring of:\\n-- * Spec fetching progress\\n-- * Tool conversion status\\n-- * Endpoint execution\\n-- * Error conditions\\ndata Trace\\n    = FetchingSpecTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ URL being fetched\\n    | SpecFetchedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of spec fetch\\n    | ToolsConvertedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ Number of tools converted from spec\\n    | CallingEndpointTrace !Text !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ method, url, path being called\\n    | EndpointResponseTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of endpoint response\\n    | SchemaResolutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during schema dereferencing\\n    | ToolExecutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during tool execution\\n    | ToolNameNormalizedTrace !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ Original name and normalized name (for debugging)\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during toolbox initialization.\\ndata InitializationError\\n    = NetworkError !Text\\n    -- ^ Network error fetching spec\\n    | ParseError !Text\\n    -- ^ JSON parse error\\n    | SpecError !Text\\n    -- ^ OpenAPI spec validation error\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Configuration for initializing an OpenAPI toolbox.\\ndata Config = Config\\n    { configUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ URL to fetch OpenAPI spec from\\n    , configBaseUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ Base URL for API calls\\n    , configHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers to include in all requests\\n    , configToken :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ Optional Bearer token for authentication\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Runtime state for an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- The toolbox maintains:\\n-- * A name for identification\\n-- * The base URL for API calls\\n-- * A list of available tools\\n-- * A name mapping for LLM-safe tool names\\n-- * The HTTP runtime for making requests\\n-- * An optional header function for dynamic auth headers\\ndata Toolbox = Toolbox\\n    { toolboxName :: Text\\n    , toolboxBaseUrl :: Text\\n    , toolboxTools :: [OpenAPITool]\\n    -- ^ Tools converted from the OpenAPI spec\\n    , toolboxNameMapping :: Map Text NameMapping\\n    -- ^ Mapping from normalized LLM names to original operation IDs\\n    , httpRuntime :: HttpClient.Runtime\\n    -- ^ HTTP client runtime\\n    , headerFunc :: Maybe (IO (Map Text Text))\\n    -- ^ Optional function to get dynamic headers (e.g., for auth)\\n    , staticHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers from config\\n    }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Initialization\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Initialize an OpenAPI toolbox from a configuration.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Creates an HTTP runtime with authentication\\n-- 2. Fetches the OpenAPI spec from the configured URL\\n-- 3. Parses the JSON to an OpenAPISpec\\n-- 4. Dereferences schema references ($ref)\\n-- 5. Converts operations to tools\\n-- 6. Builds the name mapping for LLM-safe names\\n--\\n-- Returns an 'InitializationError' if any step fails.\\ninitializeToolbox ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    Config ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError Toolbox)\\ninitializeToolbox tracer config = do\\n    -- Create HTTP runtime with auth\\n    let token = case config.configToken of\\n            Just t -> HttpClient.BearerToken t\\n            Nothing -> HttpClient.NoToken\\n    runtime <- HttpClient.newRuntime token\\n\\n    -- Fetch OpenAPI spec\\n    runTracer tracer (FetchingSpecTrace config.configUrl)\\n    fetchResult <- fetchSpec runtime config.configUrl\\n\\n    case fetchResult of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right (status, body) -> do\\n            runTracer tracer (SpecFetchedTrace status)\\n\\n            -- Parse JSON to OpenAPISpec\\n            case Aeson.decode body of\\n                Nothing -> pure $ Left $ ParseError \\\"Failed to parse OpenAPI spec JSON\\\"\\n                Just spec -> do\\n                    -- Dereference schemas\\n                    let dereferencedSpec = dereferenceSpec spec\\n\\n                    -- Convert to tools\\n                    let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools dereferencedSpec\\n                    runTracer tracer (ToolsConvertedTrace (length tools))\\n\\n                    -- Build name mapping for LLM-safe names\\n                    let nameMapping = buildToolNameMapping toolboxName tools\\n                    \\n                    -- Trace name normalizations (for debugging)\\n                    mapM_ (\\\\m -> runTracer tracer (ToolNameNormalizedTrace (nmOriginal m) (nmNormalized m))) \\n                          (Map.elems nameMapping)\\n\\n                    -- Create toolbox\\n                    pure $ Right $ Toolbox\\n                        { toolboxName = toolboxName\\n                        , toolboxBaseUrl = config.configBaseUrl\\n                        , toolboxTools = tools\\n                        , toolboxNameMapping = nameMapping\\n                        , httpRuntime = runtime\\n                        , headerFunc = Nothing\\n                        , staticHeaders = config.configHeaders\\n                        }\\n  where\\n    toolboxName = extractToolboxName config.configUrl\\n\\n-- | Extract a toolbox name from the spec URL.\\nextractToolboxName :: Text -> Text\\nextractToolboxName url = \\n    let withoutProtocol = Text.dropWhile (/= '/') $ Text.drop 8 url\\n        hostPart = Text.takeWhile (/= '/') withoutProtocol\\n    in if Text.null hostPart \\n       then \\\"openapi\\\" \\n       else normalizeForLLM hostPart\\n\\n-- | Fetch the OpenAPI spec from a URL.\\nfetchSpec ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Text ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError (Int, LByteString.ByteString))\\nfetchSpec runtime url = do\\n    result <- try $ HttpClient.get runtime (Tracer (const (pure ()))) url\\n    case result of\\n        Left (e :: HttpClient.HttpException) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show e)\\n        Right (Left err) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show err)\\n        Right (Right response) ->\\n            let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n                body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n             in pure $ Right (status, body)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Lookup\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get a tool by its normalized LLM name.\\n--\\n-- This is used during tool execution to find the original tool\\n-- when the LLM calls a tool by its normalized name.\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Nothing' if no tool with that normalized name exists.\\ngetToolByNormalizedName ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Maybe OpenAPITool\\ngetToolByNormalizedName toolbox normalizedName = do\\n    -- Look up the original operation ID from the mapping\\n    originalOpId <- findToolByNormalizedName (toolboxNameMapping toolbox) normalizedName\\n    -- Find the tool with that original operation ID\\n    find ((== Just originalOpId) . opOperationId . toolOperation) (toolboxTools toolbox)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Handler Creation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Creates a handler function for an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This handler integrates with the ToolExecutionContext system and\\n-- returns results in the standard CallResult format.\\ncreateToolHandler ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tracer IO ToolTrace ->\\n    ToolExecutionContext ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (CallResult ())ncreateToolHandler toolbox tool tracer _ctx args = do\\n    result <- handleToolCall toolbox tool args\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> do\\n            runTracer (contramapTrace tracer) (ToolExecutionErrorTrace err)\\n            pure $ IOToolError () (ScriptExecutionError (Text.unpack err))\\n        Right (textResult, _toolResult) -> do\\n            pure $ BlobToolSuccess () (ByteString.pack $ Text.unpack textResult)\\n  where\\n    contramapTrace :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> Tracer IO Trace\\n    contramapTrace _t = Tracer $ \\\\_traceEvent -> do\\n        -- Convert Trace to ToolTrace if needed\\n        -- For now, we just don't trace these events to the main tool tracer\\n        pure ()\\n\\n-- | Handle a tool call by executing the HTTP request.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Extracts path params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 2. Extracts query params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 3. Extracts body (param \\\"b\\\")\\n-- 4. Formats the path with path args\\n-- 5. Builds full URL\\n-- 6. Makes HTTP request\\n-- 7. Returns result\\nhandleToolCall ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleToolCall toolbox tool args = do\\n    case args of\\n        Object obj -> do\\n            -- Extract parameters from the JSON object\\n            let objMap = KeyMap.toMapText obj\\n            let (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue) = extractParams objMap (toolOperation tool)\\n\\n            -- Format the path with path parameters\\n            let formattedPath = formatPath (toolPath tool) pathParams\\n\\n            -- Build full URL\\n            let fullUrl = buildFullUrl (toolboxBaseUrl toolbox) formattedPath\\n\\n            -- Get headers\\n            headers <- case toolbox.headerFunc of\\n                Just hf -> hf\\n                Nothing -> pure Map.empty\\n            let allHeaders = Map.union headers (staticHeaders toolbox)\\n\\n            -- Make HTTP request\\n            let method = toolMethod tool\\n            runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ CallingEndpointTrace method fullUrl formattedPath\\n\\n            result <- executeRequest toolbox.httpRuntime method fullUrl queryParams bodyValue allHeaders\\n\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n                Right (textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                    runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ EndpointResponseTrace (resultStatus toolResult)\\n                    pure $ Right (textResult, toolResult)\\n        _ -> pure $ Left \\\"Tool arguments must be a JSON object\\\"\\n\\n-- | Extract parameters from the JSON object based on operation definition.\\nextractParams ::\\n    Map Text Value ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    (Map Text Text, Map Text Text, Maybe Value)\\nextractParams obj op =\\n    let params = opParameters op\\n        -- Extract path params\\n        pathParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractPathParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractPathParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInPath = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractPathParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract query params\\n        queryParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractQueryParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractQueryParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractQueryParam k v acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) (valueToText v) acc\\n            extractQueryParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract body\\n        bodyValue = Map.lookup \\\"b\\\" obj\\n     in (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue)\\n\\n-- | Find a parameter by name in the operation parameters list.\\nfindParam :: [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert a JSON value to Text for query params.\\nvalueToText :: Value -> Text\\nvalueToText (String s) = s\\nvalueToText (Number n) = Text.pack $ show n\\nvalueToText (Bool b) = if b then \\\"true\\\" else \\\"false\\\"\\nvalueToText Null = \\\"null\\\"\\nvalueToText _ = \\\"\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path Formatting\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Format a path template with path parameters.\\n--\\n-- Converts a path like @/pets/{petId}@ with @{p_petId: \\\"123\\\"}@ to @/pets/123@.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\nformatPath :: Path -> Map Text Text -> Path\\nformatPath pathTemplate pathArgs =\\n    Map.foldrWithKey replaceParam pathTemplate pathArgs\\n  where\\n    replaceParam :: Text -> Text -> Path -> Path\\n    replaceParam name value path =\\n        Text.replace (\\\"{\\\" <> name <> \\\"}\\\") value path\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- URL Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the full URL from base URL and path.\\nbuildFullUrl :: Text -> Path -> Text\\nbuildFullUrl baseUrl path =\\n    let base = Text.dropWhileEnd (== '/') baseUrl\\n        path' = if Text.isPrefixOf \\\"/\\\" path then path else \\\"/\\\" <> path\\n     in base <> path'\\n\\n-- | Build query string from query parameters.\\nbuildQueryString :: Map Text Text -> Text\\nbuildQueryString params\\n    | Map.null params = \\\"\\\"\\n    | otherwise =\\n        \\\"?\\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\"&\\\" (map encodeParam $ Map.toList params)\\n  where\\n    encodeParam (k, v) = urlEncode k <> \\\"=\\\" <> urlEncode v\\n    urlEncode = id -- Simplified - real implementation would URL-encode\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- HTTP Request Building and Execution\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build an HTTP request.\\nbuildRequest ::\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO HttpClient.Request\\nbuildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    let queryString = buildQueryString queryParams\\n    let urlWithQuery = fullUrl <> queryString\\n\\n    req <- HttpClient.parseRequest (Text.unpack urlWithQuery)\\n\\n    -- Set method\\n    let reqWithMethod = req{HttpClient.method = Text.encodeUtf8 method}\\n\\n    -- Add body if present\\n    let reqWithBody = case mbody of\\n            Just body ->\\n                reqWithMethod\\n                    { HttpClient.requestBody = HttpClient.RequestBodyLBS (Aeson.encode body)\\n                    , HttpClient.requestHeaders =\\n                        (\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n                            : HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithMethod\\n                    }\\n            Nothing -> reqWithMethod\\n\\n    -- Add custom headers\\n    let headerList = map (\\\\(k, v) -> (CI.mk (Text.encodeUtf8 k), Text.encodeUtf8 v)) $ Map.toList headers\\n    let finalReq = reqWithBody{HttpClient.requestHeaders = HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithBody ++ headerList}\\n\\n    pure finalReq\\n\\n-- | Execute an HTTP request.\\nexecuteRequest ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nexecuteRequest runtime method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    _ <- buildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers\\n\\n    -- Use the runtime to make the request\\n    -- Note: We're using a simplified approach here\\n    -- In a real implementation, we'd use the HttpClient.Runtime properly\\n    let tracer = Tracer (const (pure ()))\\n    result <- executeRequest' runtime tracer method fullUrl mbody\\n\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right response -> handleResponse toolInfo response\\n  where\\n    toolInfo = (method, fullUrl)\\n    executeRequest' rt tr m url mb\\n        | m == \\\"GET\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.get rt tr url\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | m == \\\"POST\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.post rt tr url mb\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | otherwise = do\\n            -- For other methods, we need to use the underlying manager\\n            pure $ Left $ \\\"Method not supported: \\\" <> m\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Response Handling\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handle an HTTP response, converting to ToolResult.\\n--\\n-- Returns both text for the LLM and a structured ToolResult.\\nhandleResponse ::\\n    (Method, Text) ->\\n    HttpClient.Response LByteString.ByteString ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleResponse (method, url) response = do\\n    let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n    let body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n\\n    -- Parse body as JSON if possible\\n    let payload = case Aeson.decode body of\\n            Just val -> val\\n            Nothing -> String (Text.decodeUtf8 $ LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    -- Create text representation for LLM\\n    let textResult =\\n            \\\"HTTP \\\" <> Text.pack (show status) <> \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n                <> Text.decodeUtf8 (LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    pure $ Right\\n        ( textResult\\n        , ToolResult\\n            { resultPath = url\\n            , resultMethod = method\\n            , resultStatus = status\\n            , resultPayload = payload\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get operation ID from operation, falling back to tool name.\\n--\\n-- This is used for tool registration when creating unique tool names.\\ngetOperationId :: Operation -> Maybe Text\\ngetOperationId = opOperationId\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Naming helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Normalize a tool name for LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- This is a re-export of 'normalizeForLLM' for convenience.\\nnormalizeToolName :: Text -> Text\\nnormalizeToolName = normalizeForLLM\\n\\n-- | Convert an OpenAPI operation ID to an LLM tool name.\\n--\\n-- Names are prefixed with @openapi_@ and include the normalized toolbox name\\n-- and normalized operation ID to avoid conflicts and ensure LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- The operation ID is normalized to replace invalid characters:\\n-- - Dots (.) become underscores (_)\\n-- - Slashes (/) become underscores (_)\\n-- - Other invalid characters become underscores\\n-- - Names starting with digits are prefixed with 't'\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_getPet\\\"}\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"pet.findByStatus\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_pet_findByStatus\\\"}\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"2.0/getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_t2_0_getPet\\\"}\\nopenapi2LLMName :: Text -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nopenapi2LLMName tboxName operationId =\\n    let normalizedToolbox = normalizeForLLM tboxName\\n        normalizedOpId = normalizeForLLM operationId\\n    in OpenAI.ToolName (\\\"openapi_\\\" <> normalizedToolbox <> \\\"_\\\" <> normalizedOpId)\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}\\n\\n-- | Defines an LLM tool registration.\\n--\\n-- This module provides functionality for registering tools with the LLM system.\\n-- It supports registration of various tool types:\\n--\\n-- * Bash tools - External scripts executed in separate processes\\n-- * IO tools - Haskell functions executed in-process\\n-- * MCP tools - Tools exposed via Model Context Protocol\\n-- * OpenAPI tools - Tools generated from OpenAPI specifications\\n--\\n-- For OpenAPI tools, special handling is done for name normalization:\\n-- OpenAPI operation IDs may contain invalid characters (dots, slashes, etc.)\\n-- which are normalized to LLM-safe names. \\n    -- * Registration functions\\n    registerBashToolInLLM,\\n    registerIOScriptInLLM,\\n    registerMcpToolInLLM,\\n    registerOpenAPIToolInLLM,\\n    registerOpenAPITools,\\n (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.Bash as BashTools\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox (callTool)\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox as McpTools\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (\\n    OpenAPITool (..),\\n    NameMapping (..),\\n    toOpenAITool,\\n    normalizeForLLM,\\n )\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter as innerTool :: Tool ()\\n    , declareTool :: OpenAI.Tool\\n    , findTool :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n    }\\ninstance Show ToolRegistration where\\n    show (ToolRegistration d _ _) = Prelude.unwords [\\\"ToolRegistration(\\\", show d.toolDef, \\\")\\\"]\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- naming policy for IO tools\\nio2LLMName :: forall a b. OpenAI.ToolName\\nbash2LLMName bash = OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat [\\\"bash_\\\", bash.scriptInfo.scriptSlug])\\n\\n-- naming policy for MCP tools\\nmcp2LLMName :: McpTools.Toolbox -> McpTools.ToolDescription -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nmcp2LLMName box mcp =\\n    OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat [\\\"mcp_\\\", box.name, \\\"_\\\", mcp.getToolDescription.name])\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nregisterBashToolInLLM ::\\n    ScriptDescription ->\\n    ToolRegistration\\nregisterBashToolInLLM script =\\n    let\\n        matchName :: ScriptDescription -> OpenAI.ToolCall -> Bool\\n        matchName bash call = bash2LLMName bash == call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName\\n\\n        mapToolDescriptionBash2LLM :: ScriptDescription -> OpenAI.Tool\\n        mapToolDescriptionBash2LLM bash =\\n            OpenAI.Tool\\n bashTool script\\n\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call = if matchName script call then Just (mapToolResult (const call) tool) else Nothing\\n mapToolDescriptionMcp2LLM schema =\\n            OpenAI.Tool\\n                { OpenAI.toolName = llmName\\n                , OpenAI.toolDescription = llmDescription\\n                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = schema\\n                }\\n\\n        tool :: Tool ()\\n        tool = mcpTool box mcp\\n\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call = if matchName mcp call then Just (mapToolResult (const call) tool) else Nothing\\n     in\\n        case llmBasedSchema of\\n            Right schema ->\\n                Right $ ToolRegistration tool (mapToolDescriptionMcp2LLM schema) find\\n            Left err ->\\n                Left err\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- OpenAPI Tool Registration\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Register a single OpenAPI tool with the LLM system.\\n--\\n-- This function creates a 'ToolRegistration' from an OpenAPI tool and its\\n-- parent toolbox. The tool name is normalized for LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- The name normalization handles OpenAPI operation IDs that may contain\\n-- invalid characters (dots, slashes, etc.) by:\\n-- 1. Replacing invalid characters with underscores\\n-- 2. Ensuring the name starts with a letter\\n-- 3. Using the toolbox's name mapping for bidirectional lookup\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Left' if the tool cannot be registered.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- case registerOpenAPITool toolbox apiTool of\\n--     Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Failed to register: \\\" ++ err\\n--     Right registration -> useWithAgent registration\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITool ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPITool toolbox tool =\\n    let \\n        -- Get the original operation ID\\n        originalOpId = Maybe.fromMaybe (OpenAPI.toolName tool) \\n                       (OpenAPIToolbox.getOperationId (OpenAPI.toolOperation tool))\\n        \\n        -- Get the normalized name from the mapping\\n        mNameMapping = findNameMapping toolbox originalOpId\\n        \\n        -- Generate LLM name using the normalized operation ID\\n        llmName = case mNameMapping of\\n            Just nm -> openapi2LLMName (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxName toolbox) (nmNormalized nm)\\n            Nothing -> openapi2LLMName (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxName toolbox) originalOpId\\n     in case mNameMapping of\\n        Nothing -> Left $ \\\"Tool not found in name mapping: \\\" ++ Text.unpack originalOpId\\n        Just nameMapping ->\\n            let -- Convert to OpenAI Tool format with normalized name\\n                openaiTool = (toOpenAITool tool)\\n                    { OpenAI.toolName = llmName }\\n\\n                -- Create the tool handler that uses the mapping\\n                runFunc :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> ToolExecutionContext -> Aeson.Value -> IO (CallResult ())\\n                runFunc tr ctx argz = createToolHandler toolbox tool tr ctx argz\\n\\n                -- Create the Tool\\n                toolDef0 = IOTool $ IOScriptDescription\\n                    { ioSlug = OpenAI.getToolName llmName\\n                    , ioDescription = OpenAPI.toolDescription tool\\n                    }\\n\\n                tool' = Tool\\n                    { toolDef = toolDef0\\n                    , toolRun = runFunc\\n                    }\\n\\n                -- Find function - matches on the normalized LLM name\\n                find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n                find call =\\n                    if call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName == llmName\\n                        then Just $ mapToolResult (const call) tool'\\n                        else Nothing\\n             in Right $ ToolRegistration\\n                    { innerTool = mapToolResult (const ()) tool'\\n                    , declareTool = openaiTool\\n                    , findTool = find\\n                    }\\n\\n-- | Find the name mapping for a given original operation ID.\\nfindNameMapping :: OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox -> Text -> Maybe NameMapping\\nfindNameMapping toolbox originalOpId =\\n    -- Look through the name mapping to find one with matching original name\\n    case filter (\\\\nm -> nmOriginal nm == originalOpId) \\n                (Map.elems $ OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxNameMapping toolbox) of\\n        (nm:_) -> Just nm\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Register all tools from an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- This function iterates through all tools in the toolbox and attempts to\\n-- register each one with normalized names for LLM compatibility.\\n--\\n-- If any registration fails, the entire operation fails fast with the first\\n-- error encountered.\\n--\\n-- For partial success handling, use 'registerOpenAPITool' on individual tools.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- result <- OpenAPI.initializeToolbox tracer config\\n-- case result of\\n--     Left err -> print err\\n--     Right toolbox -> do\\n--         regResult <- registerOpenAPITools toolbox\\n--         case regResult of\\n--             Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Registration failed: \\\" ++ err\\n--             Right registrations -> do\\n--                 -- Use registrations with agent runtime\\n--                 mapM_ (addToAgent agent) registrations\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITools ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    IO (Either String [ToolRegistration])\\nregisterOpenAPITools toolbox =\\n    -- Fail fast - return first error encountered\\n    let registerAll :: [OpenAPI.OpenAPITool] -> Either String [ToolRegistration] -> Either String [ToolRegistration]\\n        registerAll [] acc = acc\\n        registerAll (t:ts) (Right regs) =\\n            case registerOpenAPITool toolbox t of\\n                Left err -> Left err\\n                Right reg -> registerAll ts (Right (reg:regs))\\n        registerAll _ err = err\\n        \\n        tools = OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxTools toolbox\\n     in pure $ case registerAll tools (Right []) of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right regs -> Right (reverse regs)\\n\\n-- | Register an OpenAPI tool in the LLM system (alias for 'registerOpenAPITool').\\n--\\n-- This is the original function name from the OpenAPIToolbox module,\\n-- provided for backward compatibility.\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM = registerOpenAPITool\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Internal tool builders (copied from Tools to avoid import cycle)\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on a Bash script-description.\\nbashTool ::\\n    ScriptDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nbashTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = BashTool script\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        ret <- BashTools.runValue (contramap BashToolsTrace tracer) script (Just ctx) v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ BashToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an MCP toolbox tool.\\nmcpTool ::\\n    McpTools.Toolbox ->\\n    McpTools.ToolDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nmcpTool toolbox desc =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = MCPTool desc\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run _tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do\\n        ret <- callTool toolbox desc (Just v)\\n        case ret of\\n            (Just (Right rsp)) -> pure $ extractContentsFromToolCall rsp\\n            err -> pure $ McpToolError call (mconcat [\\\"calling error: \\\", show err])\\n    run _tracer _ctx _ = do\\n        pure $ McpToolError call (\\\"can only call McpTools with Aeson.Object\\\")\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall :: McpClient.CallToolResultRsp -> CallResult ()\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall rsp =\\n        McpToolResult call rsp.getCallToolResult\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an IO-tool script-description.\\nioTool ::\\n    (Aeson.FromJSON llmArg) =>\\n    IOScript llmArg ByteString ->\\n    Tool ()\\nioTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = IOTool script.description\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        let adaptTrace = IOToolsTrace . IOTools.adaptTraceInput (const v)\\n        ret <- IOTools.runValue (contramap adaptTrace tracer) script ctx v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ IOToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Schema adaptation helpers\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nadaptSchema :: Mcp.InputSchema -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\nadaptSchema schema =\\n    case schema.properties of\\n        Nothing -> Right []\\n        Just obj -> ifoldl' f (Right []) obj\\n  where\\n    f :: Aeson.Key -> Either String [ParamProperty] -> Aeson.Value -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\n    f _ err@(Left _) _ = err\\n    f k (Right xs) v =\\n        case adaptProperty k v of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right x -> Right (x : xs)\\n\\nadaptProperty :: Aeson.Key -> Aeson.Value -> Either String ParamProperty\\nadaptProperty k val =\\n    case propMappingResult of\\n        Aeson.Success prop ->\\n            Right $\\n                ParamProperty\\n                    (AesonKey.toText k)\\n                    (OpaqueParamType prop._type)\\n                    prop._description\\n        Aeson.Error err -> Left err\\n  where\\n    propMappingResult :: Aeson.Result PropertyHelper\\n    propMappingResult = Aeson.fromJSON val\\n\\ndata PropertyHelper\\n    = PropertyHelper {_type :: Text, _description :: Text}\\n\\ninstance Aeson.FromJSON PropertyHelper where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"PropertyHelper\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        PropertyHelper <$> o Aeson..: \\\"type\\\" <*> o Aeson..: \\\"description\\\"\\n\\n\""

integrationTests\\n        , propertyTests\\n        , swaggerV2Tests\\n        ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Test Data Loading\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Load a test spec from the test/data directory.\\nloadTestSpec :: FilePath -> IO OpenAPISpec\\nloadTestSpec filename = do\\n    let path = \\\"test/data/\\\" ++ filename\\n    content <- LBS.readFile path\\n    case decode content of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure $ \\\"Failed to parse JSON from \\\" ++ path\\n        Just spec -> pure spec\\n\\n-- | Load test spec as raw JSON value.\\nloadTestSpecRaw :: FilePath -> IO Value\\nloadTestSpecRaw filename = do\\n    let path = \\\"test/data/\\\" ++ filename\\n    content <- LBS.readFile path\\n    case decode content of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure $ \\\"Failed to parse JSON from \\\" ++ path\\n        Just val -> pure val\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 1. test_parseOperation\\n        , test_parseParameter\\n        , test_parseSchema\\n        , test_parseRequestBody\\n        , test_parseComponents\\n        , test_roundtripSpec\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | \\\"petstore.json\\\"\\n    assertBool \\\"Has paths\\\" (not $ Map.null $ specPaths spec)\\n    assertBool \\\"Has components\\\" (isJust $ specComponents spec)\\n\\n-- | Test minimal spec parsing.\\ntest_parseMinimalSpec :: TestTree\\ntest_parseMinimalSpec = testCase \\\"Can parse minimal spec\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"minimal.json\\\"\\n    Map.size (specPaths spec) @?= 1\\n    case Map.lookup \\\"/health\\\" (specPaths spec) of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Path /health not found\\\"\\n        Just methods -> case Map.lookup \\\"GET\\\" methods of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"GET method not found\\\"\\n            Just op -> opOperationId op @?= Just \\\"healthCheck\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test spec with references parsing.\\ntest_parseWithRefsSpec :: TestTree\\ntest_parseWithRefsSpec = testCase \\\"Can parse spec with $refs\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"with_refs.json\\\"\\n    assertBool \\\"Has paths\\\" (Map.size (specPaths spec) > 0)\\n    case specComponents spec of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected components\\\"\\n        Just comps -> case componentsSchemas comps of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected schemas\\\"\\n            Just schemas -> Map.size schemas @?= 3\\n\\n-- | Test spec with enums parsing.\\ntest_parseWithEnumsSpec :: TestTree\\ntest_parseWithEnumsSpec = testCase \\\"Can parse spec with enums\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"with_enums.json\\\"\\n    -- Find an operation with enum parameters\\n    case Map.lookup \\\"/orders\\\" (specPaths spec) of\\n \\\"GET\\\" methods <|> Map.lookup \\\"get\\\" methods\\n            case methodLookup of\\n                Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"GET method not found\\\"\\n                Just op -> do\\n \\\"Failed to parse operation\\\"\\n        Just (op :: Operation) -> do\\n            opOperationId op @?= Just \\\"testOp\\\"\\n            opSummary op @?= Just \\\"Test operation\\\"\\n parse parameter\\\"\\n        Just (param :: Parameter) -> do\\n            paramName param @?= \\\"userId\\\"\\n            paramIn param @?= ParamInPath\\n            paramRequired param @?= True\\n
