             Aeson..= Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"Pet\\\"\\n                            Aeson..= Aeson.object\\n                                [ \\\"type\\\" Aeson..= (\\\"object\\\" :: Text)\\n                                , \\\"properties\\\"\\n                                    Aeson..= Aeson.object\\n                                        [\\\"name\\\" Aeson..= Aeson.object [\\\"type\\\" Aeson..= (\\\"string\\\" :: Text)]]\\n                                ]\\n                        ]\\n                ]\\n    case decode (encode json) of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Failed to parse components\\\"\\n        Just (comps :: Components) -> do\\n            case componentsSchemas comps of\\n                Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected schemas\\\"\\n                Just schemas -> do\\n                    Map.size schemas @?= 1\\n                    assertBool \\\"Has Pet schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"Pet\\\" schemas)\\n\\n-- | Test JSON round-trip for spec.\\ntest_roundtripSpec :: TestTree\\ntest_roundtripSpec = testCase \\\"OpenAPISpec round-trips through JSON\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore.json\\\"\\n    let json = encode spec\\n    case decode json of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Failed to round-trip spec\\\"\\n        Just spec' -> do\\n            -- Check key properties match\\n            Map.size (specPaths spec) @?= Map.size (specPaths spec')\\n            (isJust $ specComponents spec) @?= (isJust $ specComponents spec')\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 2. Resolver Tests\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Tests for reference resolution.\\nresolverTests :: TestTree\\nresolverTests =\\n    testGroup\\n        \\\"Resolver\\\"\\n        [ test_resolveSimpleRef\\n        , test_resolveNestedRef\\n        , test_resolveArrayItemRef\\n        , test_resolveAnyOfRef\\n        , test_resolveCircularRef\\n        , test_resolveMissingRef\\n        , test_resolveInvalidRefPath\\n        , test_dereferenceSpec\\n        , test_parseRefValid\\n        , test_parseRefInvalid\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | Test simple reference resolution.\\ntest_resolveSimpleRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveSimpleRef = testCase \\\"Resolves simple $ref\\\" $ do\\n    let petSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"A pet\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n    let refSchema = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n\\n    let resolved = resolveSchema refSchema components\\n    schemaType resolved @?= Just \\\"object\\\"\\n    schemaDescription resolved @?= Just \\\"A pet\\\"\\n    schemaRef resolved @?= Nothing\\n\\n-- | Test nested reference resolution.\\ntest_resolveNestedRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveNestedRef = testCase \\\"Resolves nested $refs\\\" $ do\\n    -- Address -> User -> UserRef\\n    let addressSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"An address\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let addressRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Address\\\") Nothing\\n    let userSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") Nothing Nothing (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"address\\\", addressRef)])) Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let userRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/User\\\") Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Address\\\", addressSchema), (\\\"User\\\", userSchema)]))\\n\\n    let resolved = resolveSchema userRef components\\n    schemaType resolved @?= Just \\\"object\\\"\\n    case schemaProperties resolved of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected properties\\\"\\n        Just props -> case Map.lookup \\\"address\\\" props of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected address property\\\"\\n            Just addr -> schemaDescription addr @?= Just \\\"An address\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test array item reference resolution.\\ntest_resolveArrayItemRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveArrayItemRef = testCase \\\"Resolves array item $ref\\\" $ do\\n    let itemSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"string\\\") (Just \\\"A tag\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let itemRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Tag\\\") Nothing\\n    let arraySchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"array\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just itemRef) Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Tag\\\", itemSchema), (\\\"Tags\\\", arraySchema)]))\\n    let tagsRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Tags\\\") Nothing\\n\\n    let resolved = resolveSchema tagsRef components\\n    schemaType resolved @?= Just \\\"array\\\"\\n    case schemaItems resolved of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected items\\\"\\n        Just items -> schemaDescription items @?= Just \\\"A tag\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test anyOf reference resolution.\\ntest_resolveAnyOfRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveAnyOfRef = testCase \\\"Resolves anyOf $refs\\\" $ do\\n    let catSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"A cat\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let dogSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"A dog\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let catRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Cat\\\") Nothing\\n    let dogRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Dog\\\") Nothing\\n    let anyOfSchema = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just [catRef, dogRef]) Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Cat\\\", catSchema), (\\\"Dog\\\", dogSchema), (\\\"Pet\\\", anyOfSchema)]))\\n    let petRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n\\n    let resolved = resolveSchema petRef components\\n    case schemaAnyOf resolved of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected anyOf\\\"\\n        Just schemas -> do\\n            length schemas @?= 2\\n            let descs = map schemaDescription schemas\\n            assertBool \\\"Contains cat description\\\" (Just \\\"A cat\\\" `elem` descs)\\n            assertBool \\\"Contains dog description\\\" (Just \\\"A dog\\\" `elem` descs)\\n\\n-- | Test circular reference detection.\\ntest_resolveCircularRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveCircularRef = testCase \\\"Detects circular references\\\" $ do\\n    -- A refs B, B refs A\\n    let aRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/A\\\") Nothing\\n    let bRef = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/B\\\") Nothing\\n    let schemaA = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") Nothing Nothing (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"b\\\", bRef)])) Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let schemaB = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") Nothing Nothing (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"a\\\", aRef)])) Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"A\\\", schemaA), (\\\"B\\\", schemaB)]))\\n\\n    let result = resolveSchemaWithDepth aRef components 0 Set.empty\\n    case result of\\n        Left (CircularReference _) -> pure ()\\n        Left err -> assertFailure $ \\\"Expected CircularReference, got: \\\" ++ show err\\n        Right _ -> assertFailure \\\"Expected error for circular reference\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test missing reference handling.\\ntest_resolveMissingRef :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveMissingRef = testCase \\\"Handles missing reference gracefully\\\" $ do\\n    let refSchema = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Missing\\\") Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just Map.empty)\\n\\n    let resolved = resolveSchema refSchema components\\n    -- Should return schema with error description\\n    case schemaDescription resolved of\\n        Just desc -> assertBool \\\"Expected error message\\\" (\\\"Reference resolution failed\\\" `Text.isInfixOf` desc)\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected error description\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test invalid ref path handling.\\ntest_resolveInvalidRefPath :: TestTree\\ntest_resolveInvalidRefPath = testCase \\\"Handles invalid ref path\\\" $ do\\n    let components = Components Nothing\\n    case resolveRef \\\"invalid-ref\\\" components of\\n        Left (InvalidRefPath _) -> pure ()\\n        Left err -> assertFailure $ \\\"Expected InvalidRefPath, got: \\\" ++ show err\\n        Right _ -> assertFailure \\\"Expected error for invalid ref\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test full spec dereferencing.\\ntest_dereferenceSpec :: TestTree\\ntest_dereferenceSpec = testCase \\\"Dereferences all schemas in spec\\\" $ do\\n    let petSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"A pet\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n    let refSchema = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n    let param = Parameter \\\"petId\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True (Just refSchema)\\n    let operation = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") Nothing Nothing [param] Nothing\\n    let spec = OpenAPISpec (Map.singleton \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.singleton \\\"GET\\\" operation)) (Just components)\\n\\n    let dereferenced = dereferenceSpec spec\\n    case Map.lookup \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (specPaths dereferenced) of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Path not found\\\"\\n        Just methods -> case Map.lookup \\\"GET\\\" methods of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"GET method not found\\\"\\n            Just op -> case opParameters op of\\n                [] -> assertFailure \\\"Expected parameters\\\"\\n                (p : _) -> case paramSchema p of\\n                    Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected schema\\\"\\n                    Just schema -> do\\n                        schemaType schema @?= Just \\\"object\\\"\\n                        schemaRef schema @?= Nothing\\n\\n-- | Test parsing valid refs.\\ntest_parseRefValid :: TestTree\\ntest_parseRefValid = testCase \\\"Parses valid internal refs\\\" $ do\\n    case parseRef \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\" of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Failed to parse valid ref\\\"\\n        Just refPath -> do\\n            refComponents refPath @?= \\\"components\\\"\\n            refSection refPath @?= \\\"schemas\\\"\\n            refName refPath @?= \\\"Pet\\\"\\n\\n    case parseRef \\\"#/components/schemas/Nested/Deep\\\" of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Failed to parse nested ref\\\"\\n        Just refPath -> refName refPath @?= \\\"Nested\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test parsing invalid refs.\\ntest_parseRefInvalid :: TestTree\\ntest_parseRefInvalid = testCase \\\"Rejects invalid refs\\\" $ do\\n    parseRef \\\"http://example.com/schema.json\\\" @?= Nothing\\n    parseRef \\\"https://example.com/schema.json\\\" @?= Nothing\\n    parseRef \\\"invalid\\\" @?= Nothing\\n    parseRef \\\"\\\" @?= Nothing\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 3. Converter Tests\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Tests for tool conversion.\\nconverterTests :: TestTree\\nconverterTests =\\n    testGroup\\n        \\\"Converter\\\"\\n        [ test_convertOperation\\n        , test_deriveToolName\\n        , test_sanitizePathForName\\n        , test_buildToolDescription\\n        , test_parameterPrefixing\\n        , test_bodyParameter\\n        , test_enumDescription\\n        , test_requiredParams\\n        , test_convertOpenAPIToTools\\n        , test_toOpenAITool\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | Test operation conversion.\\ntest_convertOperation :: TestTree\\ntest_convertOperation = testCase \\\"Converts operation to tool\\\" $ do\\n    let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n    let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n\\n    toolName tool @?= \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\n    toolPath tool @?= \\\"/pets/{id}\\\"\\n    toolMethod tool @?= \\\"GET\\\"\\n    toolDescription tool @?= \\\"Get pet:\\\\nReturns a pet\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test tool name derivation.\\ntest_deriveToolName :: TestTree\\ntest_deriveToolName = testCase \\\"Derives tool names correctly\\\" $ do\\n    -- With operationId\\n    let op1 = Operation (Just \\\"listPets\\\") Nothing Nothing [] Nothing\\n    deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op1 @?= \\\"openapi_listPets\\\"\\n\\n    -- Without operationId (falls back to path)\\n    let op2 = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"List pets\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n    deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op2 @?= \\\"openapi_get_pets\\\"\\n\\n    -- With path parameters\\n    let op3 = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] Nothing\\n    deriveToolName \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op3 @?= \\\"openapi_get_pets_id_\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test path sanitization.\\ntest_sanitizePathForName :: TestTree\\ntest_sanitizePathForName = testCase \\\"Sanitizes paths for names\\\" $ do\\n    sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/pets\\\" @?= \\\"get_pets\\\"\\n    sanitizePathForName \\\"POST\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" @?= \\\"post_pets_id_\\\"\\n    sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/api/v1/users\\\" @?= \\\"get_api_v1_users\\\"\\n    sanitizePathForName \\\"DELETE\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" @?= \\\"delete_pets_id_\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test description building.\\ntest_buildToolDescription :: TestTree\\ntest_buildToolDescription = testCase \\\"Builds tool descriptions\\\" $ do\\n    let op1 = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n    buildToolDescription op1 @?= \\\"Get a pet:\\\\nReturns a pet\\\"\\n\\n    let op2 = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"Summary only\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n    buildToolDescription op2 @?= \\\"Summary only:\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n    let op3 = Operation Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"Description only\\\") [] Nothing\\n    buildToolDescription op3 @?= \\\":\\\\nDescription only\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test parameter prefixing with p_.\\ntest_parameterPrefixing :: TestTree\\ntest_parameterPrefixing = testCase \\\"Prefixes parameters with p_\\\" $ do\\n    let schema = Schema (Just \\\"string\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let param = Parameter \\\"petId\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True (Just schema)\\n    let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") Nothing Nothing [param] Nothing\\n    let params = buildToolParameters op\\n\\n    -- Check that parameter name is prefixed\\n    assertBool \\\"Has p_petId property\\\" (Map.member \\\"p_petId\\\" (paramsProperties params))\\n    paramsRequired params @?= [\\\"p_petId\\\"]\\n\\n    -- Check optional query parameter\\n    let queryParam = Parameter \\\"limit\\\" ParamInQuery (Just \\\"Max results\\\") False (Just schema)\\n    let op2 = Operation (Just \\\"listPets\\\") Nothing Nothing [queryParam] Nothing\\n    let params2 = buildToolParameters op2\\n\\n    assertBool \\\"Has p_limit property\\\" (Map.member \\\"p_limit\\\" (paramsProperties params2))\\n    paramsRequired params2 @?= []  -- Not required\\n\\n-- | Test body parameter with \\\"b\\\" name.\\ntest_bodyParameter :: TestTree\\ntest_bodyParameter = testCase \\\"Creates body parameter named 'b'\\\" $ do\\n    let bodySchema = Schema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"Pet object\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let content = Map.singleton \\\"application/json\\\" bodySchema\\n    let body = RequestBody (Just \\\"Pet to create\\\") True content\\n    let op = Operation (Just \\\"createPet\\\") Nothing Nothing [] (Just body)\\n    let params = buildToolParameters op\\n\\n    assertBool \\\"Has 'b' property\\\" (Map.member \\\"b\\\" (paramsProperties params))\\n    paramsRequired params @?= [\\\"b\\\"]  -- Body is required\\n\\n    -- Check optional body\\n    let body2 = RequestBody Nothing False content\\n    let op2 = Operation (Just \\\"createPet\\\") Nothing Nothing [] (Just body2)\\n    let params2 = buildToolParameters op2\\n\\n    paramsRequired params2 @?= []  -- Not required\\n\\n-- | Test enum values in description.\\ntest_enumDescription :: TestTree\\ntest_enumDescription = testCase \\\"Includes enum values in description\\\" $ do\\n    let enumVals = [String \\\"cat\\\", String \\\"dog\\\", String \\\"bird\\\"]\\n    let schema = Schema (Just \\\"string\\\") Nothing (Just enumVals) Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let prop = buildToolProperty schema (Just \\\"Pet type\\\")\\n\\n    propType prop @?= Just \\\"string\\\"\\n    propEnum prop @?= Just [\\\"cat\\\", \\\"dog\\\", \\\"bird\\\"]\\n    assertBool \\\"Description contains enum info\\\" (\\\"Allowed values\\\" `Text.isInfixOf` propDescription prop)\\n    assertBool \\\"Description contains values\\\" (\\\"cat\\\" `Text.isInfixOf` propDescription prop)\\n\\n-- | Test required parameters tracking.\\ntest_requiredParams :: TestTree\\ntest_requiredParams = testCase \\\"Tracks required parameters correctly\\\" $ do\\n    let schema = Schema (Just \\\"string\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n\\n    -- Required path param\\n    let pathParam = Parameter \\\"id\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True (Just schema)\\n    let op1 = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [pathParam] Nothing\\n    getRequiredParams op1 @?= [\\\"p_id\\\"]\\n\\n    -- Optional query param\\n    let queryParam = Parameter \\\"filter\\\" ParamInQuery Nothing False (Just schema)\\n    let op2 = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [queryParam] Nothing\\n    getRequiredParams op2 @?= []\\n\\n    -- Required query param\\n    let reqQueryParam = Parameter \\\"filter\\\" ParamInQuery Nothing True (Just schema)\\n    let op3 = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [reqQueryParam] Nothing\\n    getRequiredParams op3 @?= [\\\"p_filter\\\"]\\n\\n    -- Required body\\n    let body = RequestBody Nothing True Map.empty\\n    let op4 = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] (Just body)\\n    getRequiredParams op4 @?= [\\\"b\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Test converting entire spec.\\ntest_convertOpenAPIToTools :: TestTree\\ntest_convertOpenAPIToTools = testCase \\\"Converts entire spec to tools\\\" $ do\\n    let getOp = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n    let postOp = Operation (Just \\\"createPet\\\") (Just \\\"Create\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n    let paths =\\n            Map.fromList\\n                [ (\\\"/pets\\\", Map.fromList [(\\\"GET\\\", getOp), (\\\"POST\\\", postOp)])\\n                , (\\\"/pets/{id}\\\", Map.singleton \\\"GET\\\" getOp)\\n                ]\\n    let spec = OpenAPISpec paths Nothing\\n\\n    let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools spec\\n    length tools @?= 3\\n\\n    let names = map toolName tools\\n    assertBool \\\"Has getPet tool\\\" (\\\"openapi_getPet\\\" `elem` names)\\n    assertBool \\\"Has createPet tool\\\" (\\\"openapi_createPet\\\" `elem` names)\\n\\n-- | Test OpenAI tool conversion.\\ntest_toOpenAITool :: TestTree\\ntest_toOpenAITool = testCase \\\"Converts to OpenAI tool format\\\" $ do\\n    let schema = Schema (Just \\\"string\\\") (Just \\\"Pet ID\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let param = Parameter \\\"id\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True (Just schema)\\n    let op = Operation (Just \\\"testOp\\\") (Just \\\"Test\\\") (Just \\\"A test op\\\") [param] Nothing\\n    let apiTool = convertOperation \\\"/test\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n    let tool = toOpenAITool apiTool\\n\\n    -- Verify OpenAI.Tool fields\\n    OpenAI.toolName tool @?= OpenAI.ToolName \\\"openapi_testOp\\\"\\n    OpenAI.toolDescription tool @?= \\\"Test:\\\\nA test op\\\"\\n    -- Check that parameters are converted\\n    assertBool \\\"Has parameter properties\\\" (not $ null $ OpenAI.toolParamProperties tool)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 4. Integration Tests\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Integration tests for path formatting and URL building.\\nintegrationTests :: TestTree\\nintegrationTests =\\n    testGroup\\n        \\\"Integration\\\"\\n        [ test_formatPath\\n        , test_formatPathWithMultipleParams\\n        , test_buildFullUrl\\n        , test_extractParams\\n        , test_findParam\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | Test path formatting.\\ntest_formatPath :: TestTree\\ntest_formatPath = testCase \\\"Formats path with parameters\\\" $ do\\n    let pathTemplate = \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\"\\n    let pathArgs = Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")]\\n    formatPath pathTemplate pathArgs @?= \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test path formatting with multiple parameters.\\ntest_formatPathWithMultipleParams :: TestTree\\ntest_formatPathWithMultipleParams = testCase \\\"Formats path with multiple parameters\\\" $ do\\n    let pathTemplate = \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\"\\n    let pathArgs = Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")]\\n    let result = formatPath pathTemplate pathArgs\\n    result @?= \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test URL building.\\ntest_buildFullUrl :: TestTree\\ntest_buildFullUrl = testCase \\\"Builds full URL from base and path\\\" $ do\\n    buildFullUrl \\\"https://api.example.com\\\" \\\"/pets\\\" @?= \\\"https://api.example.com/pets\\\"\\n    buildFullUrl \\\"https://api.example.com/\\\" \\\"/pets\\\" @?= \\\"https://api.example.com/pets\\\"\\n    buildFullUrl \\\"https://api.example.com\\\" \\\"pets\\\" @?= \\\"https://api.example.com/pets\\\"\\n    buildFullUrl \\\"https://api.example.com/v1\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" @?= \\\"https://api.example.com/v1/pets/{id}\\\"\\n\\n-- | Test parameter extraction.\\ntest_extractParams :: TestTree\\ntest_extractParams = testCase \\\"Extracts parameters from JSON object\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore.json\\\"\\n    let getPetOp = case Map.lookup \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (specPaths spec) of\\n            Just methods -> case Map.lookup \\\"GET\\\" methods of\\n                Just op -> op\\n                Nothing -> error \\\"GET not found\\\"\\n            Nothing -> error \\\"/pets/{petId} not found\\\"\\n\\n    -- Create a mock JSON object with parameters\\n    let objMap =\\n            Map.fromList\\n                [ (\\\"p_petId\\\", String \\\"123\\\")\\n                ]\\n\\n    -- Test that we can find the petId parameter\\n    let params = opParameters getPetOp\\n    case findParam params \\\"petId\\\" of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Should find petId parameter\\\"\\n        Just param -> do\\n            paramName param @?= \\\"petId\\\"\\n            paramIn param @?= ParamInPath\\n            paramRequired param @?= True\\n\\n-- | Test finding parameter.\\ntest_findParam :: TestTree\\ntest_findParam = testCase \\\"Finds parameter by name\\\" $ do\\n    let schema = Schema (Just \\\"string\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let param1 = Parameter \\\"id\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True (Just schema)\\n    let param2 = Parameter \\\"limit\\\" ParamInQuery Nothing False (Just schema)\\n    let params = [param1, param2]\\n\\n    case findParam params \\\"id\\\" of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Should find id parameter\\\"\\n        Just p -> paramIn p @?= ParamInPath\\n\\n    case findParam params \\\"limit\\\" of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Should find limit parameter\\\"\\n        Just p -> paramIn p @?= ParamInQuery\\n\\n    case findParam params \\\"nonexistent\\\" of\\n        Just _ -> assertFailure \\\"Should not find nonexistent parameter\\\"\\n        Nothing -> pure ()\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 5. Swagger V2 Tests\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Tests for Swagger 2.0 format support.\\nswaggerV2Tests :: TestTree\\nswaggerV2Tests =\\n    testGroup\\n        \\\"Swagger 2.0 Support\\\"\\n        [ test_parseSwaggerV2Spec\\n        , test_swaggerV2Definitions\\n        , test_swaggerV2BodyParam\\n        , test_swaggerV2FormDataParam\\n        , test_swaggerV2InitializeToolbox\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | Test that Swagger 2.0 petstore spec can be parsed.\\ntest_parseSwaggerV2Spec :: TestTree\\ntest_parseSwaggerV2Spec = testCase \\\"Can parse Swagger 2.0 (OpenAPI v2) Petstore spec\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore-v2.json\\\"\\n    assertBool \\\"Has paths\\\" (not $ Map.null $ specPaths spec)\\n    -- Swagger 2.0 specs should be converted to have components\\n    assertBool \\\"Has components\\\" (isJust $ specComponents spec)\\n\\n-- | Test that Swagger 2.0 definitions are accessible.\\ntest_swaggerV2Definitions :: TestTree\\ntest_swaggerV2Definitions = testCase \\\"Swagger 2.0 definitions are accessible as components\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore-v2.json\\\"\\n    case specComponents spec of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected components\\\"\\n        Just comps -> case componentsSchemas comps of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Expected schemas in components\\\"\\n            Just schemas -> do\\n                -- Check that Pet, Order, User definitions are present\\n                assertBool \\\"Has Pet schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"Pet\\\" schemas)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has Order schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"Order\\\" schemas)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has User schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"User\\\" schemas)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has Category schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"Category\\\" schemas)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has Tag schema\\\" (Map.member \\\"Tag\\\" schemas)\\n\\n-- | Test that Swagger 2.0 body parameters are converted to requestBody.\\ntest_swaggerV2BodyParam :: TestTree\\ntest_swaggerV2BodyParam = testCase \\\"Swagger 2.0 body params converted to requestBody\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore-v2.json\\\"\\n    -- Find the POST /pet operation which has a body parameter\\n    case Map.lookup \\\"/pet\\\" (specPaths spec) of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Path /pet not found\\\"\\n        Just methods -> case Map.lookup \\\"POST\\\" methods of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"POST method not found\\\"\\n            Just op -> do\\n                -- Should have requestBody converted from body parameter\\n                assertBool \\\"Has requestBody\\\" (isJust $ opRequestBody op)\\n                case opRequestBody op of\\n                    Just rb -> do\\n                        assertBool \\\"Request body is required\\\" (reqBodyRequired rb)\\n                        assertBool \\\"Has content\\\" (not $ Map.null $ reqBodyContent rb)\\n                    Nothing -> pure ()\\n\\n-- | Test that Swagger 2.0 formData parameters are handled.\\ntest_swaggerV2FormDataParam :: TestTree\\ntest_swaggerV2FormDataParam = testCase \\\"Swagger 2.0 formData params are handled\\\" $ do\\n    spec <- loadTestSpec \\\"petstore-v2.json\\\"\\n    -- Find the POST /pet/{petId}/uploadImage operation which has formData params\\n    case Map.lookup \\\"/pet/{petId}/uploadImage\\\" (specPaths spec) of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Path /pet/{petId}/uploadImage not found\\\"\\n        Just methods -> case Map.lookup \\\"POST\\\" methods of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"POST method not found\\\"\\n            Just op -> do\\n                -- Should have parameters (including the formData ones)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has parameters\\\" (not $ null $ opParameters op)\\n                -- Check that the file parameter exists\\n                let hasFileParam = any (\\\\p -> paramName p == \\\"file\\\") (opParameters op)\\n                assertBool \\\"Has file parameter\\\" hasFileParam\\n\\n-- | Test that initializeToolbox can handle Swagger 2.0 specs.\\ntest_swaggerV2InitializeToolbox :: TestTree\\ntest_swaggerV2InitializeToolbox = testCase \\\"initializeToolbox works with Swagger 2.0 spec\\\" $ do\\n    -- Read the spec file directly\\n    content <- LBS.readFile \\\"test/data/petstore-v2.json\\\"\\n    case decode content of\\n        Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Failed to parse Swagger 2.0 spec\\\"\\n        Just spec -> do\\n            -- Verify it has paths\\n            assertBool \\\"Spec has paths\\\" (not $ Map.null $ specPaths spec)\\n            -- Verify tools can be converted\\n            let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools spec\\n            assertBool \\\"Tools were converted\\\" (not $ null tools)\\n            -- Should have tools for pet operations\\n            let toolNames = map toolName tools\\n            assertBool \\\"Has addPet tool\\\" (any (\\\"addPet\\\" `Text.isInfixOf`) toolNames)\\n            assertBool \\\"Has getPetById tool\\\" (any (\\\"getPetById\\\" `Text.isInfixOf`) toolNames)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- 6. Property Tests (Manual Properties)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Property-like tests using standard assertions.\\npropertyTests :: TestTree\\npropertyTests =\\n    testGroup\\n        \\\"Property-like Tests\\\"\\n        [ test_roundtripJsonProperty\\n        , test_refResolutionIdempotent\\n        , test_nameSanitizationPreservesUniqueness\\n        ]\\n\\n-- | Test JSON round-trip property.\\ntest_roundtripJsonProperty :: TestTree\\ntest_roundtripJsonProperty = testCase \\\"JSON round-trip preserves structure\\\" $ do\\n    -- Test with multiple spec types\\n    specs <- mapM loadTestSpec [\\\"minimal.json\\\", \\\"petstore.json\\\", \\\"with_refs.json\\\"]\\n    mapM_ checkRoundtrip specs\\n  where\\n    checkRoundtrip :: OpenAPISpec -> IO ()\\n    checkRoundtrip spec = do\\n        let json = encode spec\\n        case decode json of\\n            Nothing -> assertFailure \\\"Round-trip failed\\\"\\n            Just spec' -> do\\n                -- Path count should match\\n                Map.size (specPaths spec) @?= Map.size (specPaths spec')\\n                -- Components presence should match\\n                (isJust $ specComponents spec) @?= (isJust $ specComponents spec')\\n\\n-- | Test that resolution is idempotent on resolved schemas.\\ntest_refResolutionIdempotent :: TestTree\\ntest_refResolutionIdempotent = testCase \\\"Ref resolution is idempotent\\\" $ do\\n    let petSchema = mkSchema (Just \\\"object\\\") (Just \\\"A pet\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n    let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n    let refSchema = mkSchema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n\\n    -- First resolution\\n    let resolved1 = resolveSchema refSchema components\\n    -- Second resolution on already-resolved schema\\n    let resolved2 = resolveSchema resolved1 components\\n\\n    -- Should be the same\\n    schemaType resolved1 @?= schemaType resolved2\\n    schemaDescription resolved1 @?= schemaDescription resolved2\\n\\n-- | Test that name sanitization creates unique names for different paths.\\ntest_nameSanitizationPreservesUniqueness :: TestTree\\ntest_nameSanitizationPreservesUniqueness = testCase \\\"Name sanitization creates unique names\\\" $ do\\n    let op = Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] Nothing\\n\\n    -- Different paths should produce different names\\n    let name1 = deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n    let name2 = deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"POST\\\" op\\n    let name3 = deriveToolName \\\"/users\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n\\n    assertBool \\\"GET and POST should differ\\\" (name1 /= name2)\\n    assertBool \\\"Different paths should differ\\\" (name1 /= name3)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper Functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Find a parameter by name in a list.\\nfindParam :: [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Helper to construct Schema with correct field order.\\nmkSchema ::\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    Maybe [Value] ->\\n    Maybe (Map Text Schema) ->\\n    Maybe Schema ->\\n    Maybe [Schema] ->\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    Maybe [Text] ->\\n    Schema\\nmkSchema t d e p i a r req = Schema t d e p i a r req\\n\\n\""

= FetchingSpecTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ URL being fetched\\n    | SpecFetchedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of spec fetch\\n    | ToolsConvertedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ Number of tools converted from spec\\n    | CallingEndpointTrace !Text !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ method, url, path being called\\n    | EndpointResponseTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of endpoint response\\n    | -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur Toolbox)\\ninitializeToolbox tracer config = do\\n    -- Create HTTP runtime with auth\\n    let token = case config.configToken of\\n            Just t -> HttpClient.BearerToken t\\n            Nothing -> HttpClient.NoToken\\n    runtime <- HttpClient.newRuntime token\\n\\n    -- Fetch OpenAPI spec\\n    runTracer tracer (FetchingSpecTrace config.configUrl)\\n    fetchResult <- fetchSpec convertOpenAPIToTools dereferencedSpec\\n                    runTracer tracer (ToolsConvertedTrace (length tools))\\n\\n                    -- Create toolbox\\n                    pure $ Right $ Toolbox\\n                        { toolboxName = Text.takeWhile (/= '/') (Text.drop 8 config.configUrl)\\n                            -- Use part of URL as name, removing protocol\\n                    , staticHeaders = config.configHeaders\\n                        }\\n\\n-- | Fetch the OpenAPI spec from a URL.\\nfetchSpec ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Text ->\\n    IO (e :: HttpClient.HttpException) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show e)\\n        Right (Left err) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show err)\\n        Right (Right response) ->\\n            let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n                body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n             in pure $ Right (status, body)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Handler Creation\\n-- system and\\n-- returns results in the standard CallResult format.\\ncreateToolHandler ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tracer IO ToolTrace ->\\n    ToolExecutionContext ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (CallResult ())\\ncreateToolHandler toolbox tool tracer _ctx args = do\\n    result <- handleToolCall toolbox tool args\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> do\\n            runTracer (contramapTrace tracer) (ToolExecutionErrorTrace err)\\n            pure $ IOToolError () (ScriptExecutionError (Text.unpack err))\\n        Right (textResult, _toolResult) -> do\\n request.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Builds full URL\\n-- 6. Makes HTTP request\\n-- 7. Returns result\\nhandleToolCall ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleToolCall toolbox tool args = do\\n    case args of\\n (toolOperation tool)\\n\\n            -- Format the path with path parameters\\n            let formattedPath = formatPath (toolPath tool) pathParams\\n\\n            -- Build full URL\\n            let fullUrl = buildFullUrl (toolboxBaseUrl toolbox) formattedPath\\n\\n            -- Get headers\\n            headers <- case toolbox.headerFunc of\\n                Just hf -> hf\\n                Nothing -> pure Map.empty\\n            let allHeaders = Map.union headers (staticHeaders toolbox)\\n\\n            -- Make HTTP request\\n            let method = toolMethod tool\\n            runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ CallingEndpointTrace method fullUrl formattedPath\\n\\n            result <- executeRequest toolbox.httpRuntime method fullUrl queryParams bodyValue allHeaders\\n\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n                Right (textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                    runTracer (Tracer (const object\\\"\\n\\n-- | Extract parameters from the JSON object based on operation definition.\\nextractParams ::\\n    Map Text Value ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    (Map Text Text, Map Text Text, Maybe Value)\\nextractParams obj op =\\n    let params = opParameters op\\n        -- Extract path params\\n        pathParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractPathParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractQueryParam k v acc\\n [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert a JSON value to Text for query params.\\nvalueToText :: Value -> Text\\nvalueToText (String s) = s\\nvalueToText (Number n) = Text.pack $ show n\\nvalueToText (Bool b) = if b then \\\"true\\\" else \\\"false\\\"\\nvalueToText Null = \\\"null\\\"\\nvalueToText _ = \\\"\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path Formatting\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Format a path template with path parameters.\\n--\\n-- Converts a path like @/pets/{petId}@ with @{p_petId: \\\"123\\\"}@ to @/pets/123@.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\nformatPath :: Path -> Map Text Text -> Path\\nformatPath pathTemplate pathArgs =\\n    Map.foldrWithKey replaceParam pathTemplate pathArgs\\n  where\\n    replaceParam :: Text -> Text -> Path -> Path\\n    replaceParam name value path =\\n        Text.replace (\\\"{\\\" <> name <> \\\"}\\\") value path\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- URL Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the full URL from base URL and path.\\nbuildFullUrl :: Text -> Path -> Text\\nbuildFullUrl baseUrl path =\\n    let base = Text.dropWhileEnd (== '/') baseUrl\\n        path' = if Text.isPrefixOf \\\"/\\\" path then path else \\\"/\\\" <> path\\n     in base <> path'\\n\\n-- | Build query string from query parameters.\\nbuildQueryString :: Map Text Text -> Text\\nbuildQueryString params\\n    | Map.null params = \\\"\\\"\\n    | otherwise =\\n        \\\"?\\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\"&\\\" (map encodeParam $ Map.toList params)\\n  where\\n    encodeParam (k, v) = urlEncode k <> \\\"=\\\" <> urlEncode v\\n    urlEncode = id -- Simplified - real implementation would URL-encode\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- HTTP Request Building and Execution\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build an HTTP request.\\nbuildRequest ::\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO HttpClient.Request\\nbuildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    let queryString = buildQueryString queryParams\\n    let urlWithQuery = fullUrl <> queryString\\n\\n    req <- HttpClient.parseRequest (Text.unpack urlWithQuery)\\n\\n    -- Set method\\n    let reqWithMethod = req{HttpClient.method = Text.encodeUtf8 method}\\n\\n    -- Add body if present\\n    let reqWithBody = case mbody of\\n            Just body ->\\n                reqWithMethod\\n                    { HttpClient.requestBody = HttpClient.RequestBodyLBS (Aeson.encode body)\\n                    , HttpClient.requestHeaders =\\n                        (\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n                            : HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithMethod\\n                    }\\n            Nothing -> reqWithMethod\\n\\n    -- Add custom headers\\n    let headerList = map (\\\\(k, v) -> (CI.mk (Text.encodeUtf8 k), Text.encodeUtf8 v)) $ Map.toList headers\\n    let finalReq = reqWithBody{HttpClient.requestHeaders = HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithBody ++ headerList}\\n\\n    pure finalReq\\n\\n-- | Execute an HTTP request.\\nexecuteRequest ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nexecuteRequest runtime method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    _ <- buildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers\\n\\n    -- Use the runtime to make the request\\n    -- Note: We're using a simplified approach here\\n    -- In a real implementation, we'd use the HttpClient.Runtime properly\\n    let tracer = Tracer (const (pure ()))\\n    result <- executeRequest' runtime tracer method fullUrl mbody\\n\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right response -> handleResponse toolInfo response\\n  where\\n    toolInfo = (method, fullUrl)\\n    executeRequest' rt tr m url mb\\n        | m == \\\"GET\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.get rt tr url\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | m == \\\"POST\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.post rt tr url mb\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | otherwise = do\\n            -- For other methods, we need to use the underlying manager\\n            pure $ Left $ \\\"Method not supported: \\\" <> m\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Response Handling\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handle an HTTP response, converting to ToolResult.\\n--\\n-- Returns both text for the LLM and a structured ToolResult.\\nhandleResponse ::\\n    (Method, Text) ->\\n    HttpClient.Response LByteString.ByteString ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleResponse (method, url) response = do\\n    let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n    let body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n\\n    -- Parse body as JSON if possible\\n    let payload = case Aeson.decode body of\\n            Just val -> val\\n            Nothing -> String (Text.decodeUtf8 $ LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    -- Create text representation for LLM\\n    let textResult =\\n            \\\"HTTP \\\" <> Text.pack (show status) <> \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n                <> Text.decodeUtf8 (LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    pure $ Right\\n        ( textResult\\n        , ToolResult\\n            { resultPath = url\\n            , resultMethod = method\\n            , resultStatus = status\\n            , resultPayload = payload\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get operation ID from operation, falling back to tool name.\\n--\\n-- This is used for tool registration when creating unique tool names.\\ngetOperationId :: Operation -> Maybe Text\\ngetOperationId = opOperationId\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Naming helper\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Convert an OpenAPI operation ID to an LLM tool name.\\n--\\n-- Names are prefixed with @openapi_@ and include the toolbox name\\n-- to avoid conflicts.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_getPet\\\"}\\nopenapi2LLMName :: Text -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nopenapi2LLMName tboxName operationId =\\n    OpenAI.ToolName (\\\"openapi_\\\" <> tboxName <> \\\"_\\\" <> operationId)\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 specification types and JSON parsers.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a subset of OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 and Swagger 2.0 types needed for\\n-- LLM tool generation. It focuses on parsing paths, operations,\\n-- parameters, request bodies, and schemas.\\n--\\n-- Note: This is not a full OpenAPI implementation - only the subset\\n-- needed for tool generation is supported.\\n--\\n-- The module automatically converts Swagger 2.0 specs to OpenAPI 3.0 structure:\\n-- * 'definitions' -> 'components/schemas'\\n-- * 'in: body' parameters -> 'requestBody'\\n-- * parameter-level 'type' -> nested 'schema.type'\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    -- * OpenAPI spec types\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Method,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n    Components (..),\\n    MediaType,\\n\\n    -- * Tool execution result\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n\\n    -- * JSON helpers\\n    Pair,\\n    object,\\n    parseParamLocation,\\n    paramLocationToText,\\n    (.=?),\\n    (.=),\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Applicative ((<|>))\\nimport Data.Aeson (FromJSON (..), ToJSON (..), Value (..), decode, withObject, (.:), (.:?))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Aeson.Key (fromText, toText, Key)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport Data.Aeson.Types (Parser, parseEither)\\nimport qualified Data.HashMap.Strict as HashMap\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (catMaybes, fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\n-- | Aeson Pair type for building JSON objects.\\ntype Pair = (Key, Value)\\n\\n-- | Build JSON object from a list of optional pairs.\\nobject :: [Maybe Pair] -> Value\\nobject = Aeson.object . catMaybes\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Top-level OpenAPI spec\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Top-level OpenAPI specification document.\\n-- Supports both OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.0 formats.\\ndata OpenAPISpec = OpenAPISpec\\n    { specPaths :: Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n    , specComponents :: Maybe Components\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"OpenAPISpec\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        -- Check for Swagger 2.0 vs OpenAPI 3.x\\n        mSwagger <- o .:? \\\"swagger\\\"\\n        mOpenapi <- o .:? \\\"openapi\\\"\\n\\n        case (mSwagger :: Maybe Text, mOpenapi :: Maybe Text) of\\n            (Just \\\"2.0\\\", _) -> parseSwaggerV2 o\\n            (_, Just _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o\\n            (_, _) -> parseOpenAPIV3 o  -- Default to OpenAPI 3.x parsing\\n\\n-- | Parse OpenAPI 3.x format.\\nparseOpenAPIV3 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseOpenAPIV3 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    paths <- parsePaths pathsObj\\n    components <- o .:? \\\"components\\\"\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 format and convert to OpenAPI 3.x structure.\\nparseSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Parser OpenAPISpec\\nparseSwaggerV2 o = do\\n    pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n    -- Parse paths with Swagger 2.0 conversion\\n    paths <- parsePathsSwaggerV2 pathsObj\\n    -- Convert definitions to components/schemas\\n    definitions <- o .:? \\\"definitions\\\"\\n    let components = fmap definitionsToComponents definitions\\n    pure $ OpenAPISpec paths components\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 definitions to Components.\\ndefinitionsToComponents :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Components\\ndefinitionsToComponents defs =\\n    let schemas = KeyMap.toList defs\\n        schemaMap = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe parseDefinition schemas\\n    in Components (if Map.null schemaMap then Nothing else Just schemaMap)\\n  where\\n    parseDefinition :: (Key, Value) -> Maybe (Text, Schema)\\n    parseDefinition (k, v) =\\n        case Aeson.fromJSON v of\\n            Aeson.Success schema -> Just (toText k, schema)\\n            Aeson.Error _ -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Parse the paths object where each path maps to a methods object.\\nparsePaths :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePaths = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItem o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item from the paths object.\\nparsePathItem :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItem o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperations val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse an operations object (the value of a path).\\n-- This contains the HTTP methods like \\\"get\\\", \\\"post\\\", etc.\\nparseOperations :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperations = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperation o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    -- Skip OpenAPI extension fields (starting with \\\"x-\\\") and other non-method fields\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"servers\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation.\\nparseMethodOperation :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperation o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    op <- o .: key\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse paths for Swagger 2.0, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePathsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItemSwaggerV2 o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item for Swagger 2.0.\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItemSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperationsSwaggerV2 val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse operations for Swagger 2.0, converting body params to requestBody.\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperationsSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation for Swagger 2.0.\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperationSwaggerV2 o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    opVal <- o .: key\\n    op <- parseOperationSwaggerV2 opVal\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 operation, converting body parameters to requestBody.\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Operation\\nparseOperationSwaggerV2 = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    operationId <- o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n    summary <- o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n    description <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    rawParams <- fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\"\\n\\n    -- Convert parameters: separate body params from regular params\\n    let (bodyParams, otherParams) = partitionBodyParams rawParams\\n\\n    -- Convert body params to requestBody if present\\n    let mRequestBody = case bodyParams of\\n            [] -> Nothing\\n            (bodyParam : _) -> Just $ swaggerBodyParamToRequestBody bodyParam\\n\\n    -- Parse other parameters\\n    regularParams <- traverse parseParameterSwaggerV2 otherParams\\n\\n    pure $ Operation operationId summary description regularParams mRequestBody\\n  where\\n    partitionBodyParams :: [Value] -> ([SwaggerBodyParam], [Value])\\n    partitionBodyParams = foldr classify ([], [])\\n      where\\n        classify v (bodies, others) =\\n            case parseMaybe parseSwaggerBodyParam v of\\n                Just bp -> (bp : bodies, others)\\n                Nothing -> (bodies, v : others)\\n\\n    parseMaybe :: (Value -> Parser a) -> Value -> Maybe a\\n    parseMaybe p v = either (const Nothing) Just (parseEither p v)\\n\\n-- | Temporary type for Swagger 2.0 body parameters.\\ndata SwaggerBodyParam = SwaggerBodyParam\\n    { sbpDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , sbpRequired :: Bool\\n    , sbpSchema :: Schema\\n    }\\n\\n-- | Parse a Swagger 2.0 body parameter.\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam :: Value -> Parser SwaggerBodyParam\\nparseSwaggerBodyParam = withObject \\\"body parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    paramIn <- o .: \\\"in\\\" :: Parser Text\\n    if paramIn == \\\"body\\\"\\n        then do\\n            desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n            schema <- o .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n            pure $ SwaggerBodyParam desc required schema\\n        else fail \\\"Not a body parameter\\\"\\n\\n-- | Convert Swagger 2.0 body parameter to RequestBody.\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody :: SwaggerBodyParam -> RequestBody\\nswaggerBodyParamToRequestBody sbp =\\n    let content = Map.singleton \\\"application/json\\\" (sbpSchema sbp)\\n     in RequestBody (sbpDescription sbp) (sbpRequired sbp) content\\n\\n-- | Parse Swagger 2.0 parameter (supports both direct type and schema).\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 :: Value -> Parser Parameter\\nparseParameterSwaggerV2 v = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" (\\\\o -> do\\n    paramInStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n    paramIn <- parseParamLocationSwaggerV2 paramInStr\\n    name <- o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n    desc <- o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n    required <- fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\n    -- Swagger 2.0 can have type directly or in schema\\n    mDirectType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mSchema <- o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\n    -- Build schema from direct type if present\\n    let schema = case (mSchema, mDirectType) of\\n            (Just s, _) -> Just s\\n            (Nothing, Just t) -> Just $ Schema\\n                { schemaType = Just t\\n                , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n                , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n                , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n                , schemaItems = Nothing\\n                , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n                , schemaRef = Nothing\\n                , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n                }\\n            (Nothing, Nothing) -> Nothing\\n\\n    pure $ Parameter name paramIn desc required schema\\n    ) v\\n\\n-- | Parse parameter location for Swagger 2.0 (includes formData).\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 \\\"formData\\\" = pure ParamInFormData\\nparseParamLocationSwaggerV2 other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    toJSON spec =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"paths\\\" .= specPaths spec\\n            , \\\"components\\\" .=? specComponents spec\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path and method types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | URL path like \\\"/pets\\\" or \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\".\\ntype Path = Text\\n\\n-- | HTTP method like \\\"GET\\\", \\\"POST\\\", \\\"PUT\\\", \\\"DELETE\\\", etc.\\ntype Method = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation (an endpoint)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents an API operation (endpoint).\\n-- Corresponds to an Operation Object in OpenAPI.\\ndata Operation = Operation\\n    { opOperationId :: Maybe Text\\n    , opSummary :: Maybe Text\\n    , opDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , opParameters :: [Parameter]\\n    , opRequestBody :: Maybe RequestBody\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Operation where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        Operation\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"requestBody\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Operation where\\n    toJSON op =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"operationId\\\" .=? op.opOperationId\\n            , \\\"summary\\\" .=? op.opSummary\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? op.opDescription\\n            , \\\"parameters\\\" .=? if null op.opParameters then Nothing else Just op.opParameters\\n            , \\\"requestBody\\\" .=? op.opRequestBody\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter (path, query, header, formData)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parameter (path, query, header, or formData parameter).\\ndata Parameter = Parameter\\n    { paramName :: Text\\n    , paramIn :: ParamLocation\\n    , paramDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , paramRequired :: Bool\\n    , paramSchema :: Maybe Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Parameter where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        locationStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n        location <- parseParamLocation locationStr\\n        Parameter\\n            <$> o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n            <*> pure location\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Parameter where\\n    toJSON param =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"name\\\" .= param.paramName\\n            , \\\"in\\\" .= paramLocationToText param.paramIn\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? param.paramDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if param.paramRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"schema\\\" .=? param.paramSchema\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parameter location: path, query, header, or formData.\\ndata ParamLocation\\n    = ParamInPath\\n    | ParamInQuery\\n    | ParamInHeader\\n    | ParamInFormData\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a parameter location from text.\\nparseParamLocation :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocation \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocation \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocation \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocation other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\n-- | Convert a parameter location to text.\\nparamLocationToText :: ParamLocation -> Text\\nparamLocationToText ParamInPath = \\\"path\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInQuery = \\\"query\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInHeader = \\\"header\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInFormData = \\\"formData\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Request body\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a request body for an operation.\\ndata RequestBody = RequestBody\\n    { reqBodyDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , reqBodyRequired :: Bool\\n    , reqBodyContent :: Map MediaType Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON RequestBody where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"RequestBody\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        contentObj <- o .: \\\"content\\\"\\n        content <- parseContentMap contentObj\\n        RequestBody\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> pure content\\n\\ninstance ToJSON RequestBody where\\n    toJSON rb =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"description\\\" .=? rb.reqBodyDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if rb.reqBodyRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"content\\\" .= KeyMap.fromList (map mediaTypeSchemaToPair $ Map.toList rb.reqBodyContent)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parse a content map (media type -> media type object with schema).\\nparseContentMap :: Value -> Parser (Map MediaType Schema)\\nparseContentMap = withObject \\\"content\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMediaTypeSchema o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single media type entry.\\nparseMediaTypeSchema :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (MediaType, Schema)\\nparseMediaTypeSchema o key = do\\n    let mediaType = toText key\\n    mediaObj <- o .: key\\n    schema <- mediaObj .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n    pure (mediaType, schema)\\n\\n-- | Convert media type/schema pair to an Aeson pair.\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair :: (MediaType, Schema) -> Pair\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair (mediaType, schema) =\\n    (fromText mediaType, Aeson.object [\\\"schema\\\" Aeson..= schema])\\n\\n-- | Media type like \\\"application/json\\\" or \\\"application/xml\\\".\\ntype MediaType = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- JSON Schema (subset needed for OpenAPI)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema subset used in OpenAPI.\\n-- Supports common schema types needed for tool generation.\\ndata Schema = Schema\\n    { schemaType :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaEnum :: Maybe [Aeson.Value]\\n    , schemaProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    , schemaItems :: Maybe Schema\\n    , schemaAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema]\\n    , schemaRef :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaRequired :: Maybe [Text]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Schema where\\n    parseJSON v =\\n        parseRefSchema v\\n            <|> parseInlineSchema v\\n\\n-- | Parse a schema that uses $ref.\\nparseRefSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseRefSchema = withObject \\\"Schema ($ref)\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    ref <- o .: \\\"$ref\\\"\\n    pure $\\n        Schema\\n            { schemaType = Nothing\\n            , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n            , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n            , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n            , schemaItems = Nothing\\n            , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n            , schemaRef = Just ref\\n            , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Parse an inline schema definition.\\nparseInlineSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseInlineSchema = withObject \\\"Schema\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    mType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mProps <- o .:? \\\"properties\\\"\\n    -- Convert properties from HashMap to Map if present\\n    let props = fmap HashMap.toList mProps >>= \\\\pairs ->\\n            if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n    Schema\\n        <$> pure mType\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"enum\\\"\\n        <*> pure props\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"items\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"anyOf\\\"\\n        <*> pure Nothing\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Schema where\\n    toJSON schema =\\n        case schema.schemaRef of\\n            Just ref -> Aeson.object [\\\"$ref\\\" Aeson..= ref]\\n            Nothing ->\\n                object\\n                    [ \\\"type\\\" .=? schema.schemaType\\n                    , \\\"description\\\" .=? schema.schemaDescription\\n                    , \\\"enum\\\" .=? schema.schemaEnum\\n                    , \\\"properties\\\" .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaPropertyToPair . Map.toList) schema.schemaProperties)\\n                    , \\\"items\\\" .=? schema.schemaItems\\n                    , \\\"anyOf\\\" .=? schema.schemaAnyOf\\n                    , \\\"required\\\" .=? schema.schemaRequired\\n                    ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema property to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaPropertyToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaPropertyToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Components section for reusable schemas\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Components section containing reusable schemas and other definitions.\\ndata Components = Components\\n    { componentsSchemas :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Components where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Components\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        mSchemas <- o .:? \\\"schemas\\\"\\n        -- Convert from HashMap to Map\\n        let schemas = mSchemas >>= \\\\hm ->\\n                let pairs = HashMap.toList hm\\n                 in if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n        pure $ Components schemas\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Components where\\n    toJSON comps =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"schemas\\\"\\n                .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaDefinitionToPair . Map.toList) comps.componentsSchemas)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema definition to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaDefinitionToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaDefinitionToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool execution result\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Result of executing an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This type captures the structured result from executing an OpenAPI-based\\n-- tool call, including the HTTP response details.\\ndata ToolResult = ToolResult\\n    { resultPath :: Text\\n    -- ^ Path that was called\\n    , resultMethod :: Text\\n    -- ^ HTTP method used\\n    , resultStatus :: Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status code\\n    , resultPayload :: Value\\n    -- ^ Response payload as JSON\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON ToolResult where\\n    toJSON tr =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"path\\\" Aeson..= resultPath tr\\n            , \\\"method\\\" Aeson..= resultMethod tr\\n            , \\\"status\\\" Aeson..= resultStatus tr\\n            , \\\"payload\\\" Aeson..= resultPayload tr\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper functions for JSON construction\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Optional field helper - only include if value is present.\\n(.=?) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> Maybe v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=?) k v = fmap ((Aeson..=) k) v\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=?\\n\\n-- | Required field helper.\\n(.=) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=) k v = Just ((Aeson..=) k v)\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=\\n\\n\""
```
