"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI to Tool conversion logic.\\n--\\n-- This module converts parsed and dereferenced OpenAPI operations into the\\n-- internal Tool representation used by the agents system. It handles:\\n--\\n-- * Converting OpenAPI specs to lists of tools\\n-- * Building tool parameter schemas from OpenAPI parameters\\n-- * Naming conventions (operationId or path/method based)\\n-- * Parameter naming (p_ prefix for path/query, b for body)\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    OpenAPITool (..),\\n    ToolParameters (..),\\n    PropertySchema (..),\\n    ToolHandler,\\n\\n    -- * Main conversion functions\\n    convertOpenAPIToTools,\\n    convertOperation,\\n\\n    -- * Parameter building\\n    buildToolProperty,\\n    paramToToolName,\\n    buildToolParameters,\\n\\n    -- * Description building\\n    buildToolDescription,\\n\\n    -- * Required parameters\\n    getRequiredParams,\\n\\n    -- * Naming\\n    deriveToolName,\\n    sanitizePathForName,\\n\\n    -- * OpenAI conversion\\n    toOpenAITool,\\n\\n    -- * Handler helpers\\n    defaultHandler,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Data.Aeson (Value)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport System.Agents.ToolSchema (ParamProperty (..), ParamType (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core Types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handler function type for executing OpenAPI tool calls.\\n-- This is a placeholder type - actual execution would be implemented\\n-- by the caller based on their HTTP client needs.\\ntype ToolHandler = Map Text Value -> IO (Either Text Text)\\n\\n-- | Internal representation of a tool derived from OpenAPI.\\ndata OpenAPITool = OpenAPITool\\n    { toolOperation :: Operation\\n    , toolPath :: Path\\n    , toolMethod :: Method\\n    , toolName :: Text\\n    , toolDescription :: Text\\n    , toolParameters :: ToolParameters\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parameters structure for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata ToolParameters = ToolParameters\\n    { paramsType :: Text\\n    -- ^ Always \\\"object\\\" for OpenAPI tools\\n    , paramsProperties :: Map Text PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ Map of parameter name to property schema\\n    , paramsRequired :: [Text]\\n    -- ^ List of required parameter names\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Property schema for LLM tool parameters.\\n-- Mirrors JSON Schema structure needed for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata PropertySchema = PropertySchema\\n    { propType :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ JSON Schema type (string, integer, boolean, etc.)\\n    , propDescription :: Text\\n    -- ^ Description for the LLM\\n    , propEnum :: Maybe [Text]\\n    -- ^ For enum values, stores the allowed values\\n    , propItems :: Maybe PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ For arrays, the schema of array items\\n    , propAnyOf :: Maybe [PropertySchema]\\n    -- ^ For anyOf schemas, the possible schemas\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Main Conversion Functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPI spec into a list of tools.\\n--\\n-- This function walks through all paths and methods in the spec,\\n-- converting each operation into an 'OpenAPITool'.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let spec = OpenAPISpec (Map.singleton \\\"/pets\\\" (Map.singleton \\\"GET\\\" op)) Nothing\\n-- >>> let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools spec\\n-- >>> length tools\\n-- 1\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools ::\\n    OpenAPISpec ->\\n    [OpenAPITool]\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools spec = do\\n    (path, methods) <- Map.toList (specPaths spec)\\n    (method, operation) <- Map.toList methods\\n    pure $ convertOperation path method operation\\n\\n-- | Converts a single OpenAPI operation to a tool.\\n--\\n-- This is the core conversion function that:\\n-- * Derives a tool name from operationId or path/method\\n-- * Builds the tool description from summary and description\\n-- * Converts parameters to property schemas\\n-- * Tracks required parameters\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nconvertOperation ::\\n    Path ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    OpenAPITool\\nconvertOperation path method op =\\n    OpenAPITool\\n        { toolOperation = op\\n        , toolPath = path\\n        , toolMethod = method\\n        , toolName = deriveToolName path method op\\n        , toolDescription = buildToolDescription op\\n        , toolParameters = buildToolParameters op\\n        }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Name Derivation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Derives a tool name from an OpenAPI operation.\\n--\\n-- Uses operationId if available, otherwise falls back to {method}_{path}.\\n-- All names are prefixed with \\\"openapi_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation (Just \\\"listPets\\\") Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_listPets\\\"\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_get_pets__id_\\\"\\nderiveToolName :: Path -> Method -> Operation -> Text\\nderiveToolName path method op =\\n    \\\"openapi_\\\" <> case opOperationId op of\\n        Just opId -> opId\\n        Nothing -> sanitizePathForName method path\\n\\n-- | Sanitizes a path for use in a tool name.\\n--\\n-- Converts the path/method combination to a valid identifier.\\n-- Replaces special characters with underscores.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/pets\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_pets\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"POST\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}/owner\\\"\\n-- \\\"post_pets__id__owner\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/api/v1/users\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_api_v1_users\\\"\\nsanitizePathForName :: Method -> Path -> Text\\nsanitizePathForName method path =\\n    let methodPart = Text.toLower method\\n        -- Remove leading slash and replace special chars with underscore\\n        pathPart =\\n            Text.replace \\\"/\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"{\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"}\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.dropWhile (== '/')\\n                $ path\\n        -- Clean up multiple consecutive underscores\\n        cleaned = Text.replace \\\"__\\\" \\\"_\\\" pathPart\\n     in if Text.null cleaned\\n            then methodPart\\n            else methodPart <> \\\"_\\\" <> cleaned\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Description Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builds a tool description from an operation's summary and description.\\n--\\n-- Combines both fields with a colon separator if both are present.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a single pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op\\n-- \\\"Get a pet:\\\\nReturns a single pet\\\"\\n-- >>> let op2 = Operation Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"Just description\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op2\\n-- \\\":\\\\nJust description\\\"\\nbuildToolDescription :: Operation -> Text\\nbuildToolDescription op =\\n    fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opSummary op) <> \\\":\\\\n\\\" <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opDescription op)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts a parameter to its tool property name.\\n--\\n-- Prefixes path and query parameters with \\\"p_\\\" to avoid conflicts.\\n-- Header parameters are also prefixed with \\\"p_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let param = Parameter \\\"petId\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param\\n-- \\\"p_petId\\\"\\n-- >>> let param2 = Parameter \\\"limit\\\" ParamInQuery (Just \\\"Max results\\\") False Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param2\\n-- \\\"p_limit\\\"\\nparamToToolName :: Parameter -> Text\\nparamToToolName param = \\\"p_\\\" <> paramName param\\n\\n-- | Builds tool parameters structure from an operation.\\n--\\n-- Collects all parameters (path, query, header) and body into a\\n-- unified parameter schema structure.\\nbuildToolParameters :: Operation -> ToolParameters\\nbuildToolParameters op =\\n    ToolParameters\\n        { paramsType = \\\"object\\\"\\n        , paramsProperties = paramProps <> bodyProps\\n        , paramsRequired = getRequiredParams op\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Convert operation parameters to properties\\n    paramProps = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe paramToProperty (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property if present\\n    bodyProps = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body -> bodyToProperty body\\n        Nothing -> Map.empty\\n\\n    -- Convert a single parameter to (name, schema) if it has a schema\\n    paramToProperty :: Parameter -> Maybe (Text, PropertySchema)\\n    paramToProperty param = do\\n        schema <- paramSchema param\\n        pure (paramToToolName param, buildToolProperty schema (paramDescription param))\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property map\\n    bodyToProperty :: RequestBody -> Map Text PropertySchema\\n    bodyToProperty body =\\n        -- Use \\\"b\\\" as the body parameter name\\n        -- For simplicity, we take the first schema from content types\\n        case Map.elems (reqBodyContent body) of\\n            [] -> Map.empty\\n            (schema : _) -> Map.singleton \\\"b\\\" (schemaToProperty schema (bodyDescription body))\\n\\n    bodyDescription :: RequestBody -> Text\\n    bodyDescription body = fromMaybe \\\"Request body\\\" (reqBodyDescription body)\\n\\n-- | Builds a property schema from an OpenAPI schema.\\n--\\n-- Handles:\\n-- * Enum values: copies type and augments description\\n-- * Arrays: copies items schema\\n-- * AnyOf: copies anyOf schemas\\n-- * Basic types: copies type and description\\n--\\n-- The parameter description is merged with the schema description.\\nbuildToolProperty ::\\n    Schema ->\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    PropertySchema\\nbuildToolProperty schema paramDesc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = buildDescription schema\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`buildToolProperty` Nothing) (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`buildToolProperty` Nothing)) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Build description from parameter description, schema description, and enum values\\n    buildDescription :: Schema -> Text\\n    buildDescription s =\\n        let baseDesc = fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (paramDesc <|> schemaDescription s)\\n            enumDesc = case schemaEnum s of\\n                Just vals -> \\\"\\\\nAllowed values: \\\" <> formatEnumValues vals\\n                Nothing -> \\\"\\\"\\n         in baseDesc <> enumDesc\\n\\n    -- Format enum values as a readable string\\n    formatEnumValues :: [Aeson.Value] -> Text\\n    formatEnumValues vals =\\n        let txtVals = mapMaybe extractString vals\\n         in Text.intercalate \\\", \\\" txtVals\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n    -- Extract enum values as Text list\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Converts a Schema to PropertySchema without parameter description.\\nschemaToProperty :: Schema -> Text -> PropertySchema\\nschemaToProperty schema desc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = desc <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (fmap (\\\"\\\\n\\\" <>) (schemaDescription schema))\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\") (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\")) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n-- Alternative operator for Maybe\\n(<|>) :: Maybe a -> Maybe a -> Maybe a\\n(<|>) (Just x) _ = Just x\\n(<|>) Nothing y = y\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Required Parameters\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Gets the list of required parameter names for a tool.\\n--\\n-- Includes:\\n-- * Parameters marked with required=true\\n-- * Body parameter \\\"b\\\" if request body is required\\ngetRequiredParams :: Operation -> [Text]\\ngetRequiredParams op =\\n    paramNames <> bodyName\\n  where\\n    -- Get required parameter names (prefixed)\\n    paramNames =\\n        map paramToToolName $\\n            filter paramRequired (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Check if body is required\\n    bodyName = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body | reqBodyRequired body -> [\\\"b\\\"]\\n        _ -> []\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Conversion to OpenAI Tool Format\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPITool to the OpenAI Tool format.\\n--\\n-- This allows OpenAPITools to be used directly with the LLM interface.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"test\\\") (Just \\\"Test op\\\") (Just \\\"A test\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let apiTool = convertOperation \\\"/test\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> let tool = toOpenAITool apiTool\\n-- >>> OpenAI.toolName tool\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_test\\\"}\\ntoOpenAITool :: OpenAPITool -> OpenAI.Tool\\ntoOpenAITool apiTool =\\n    OpenAI.Tool\\n        { OpenAI.toolName = OpenAI.ToolName (toolName apiTool)\\n        , OpenAI.toolDescription = toolDescription apiTool\\n        , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = map propertyToParamProperty $ Map.toList (paramsProperties (toolParameters apiTool))\\n        }\\n  where\\n    propertyToParamProperty :: (Text, PropertySchema) -> ParamProperty\\n    propertyToParamProperty (name, prop) =\\n        ParamProperty\\n            { propertyKey = name\\n            , propertyType = schemaTypeToParamType prop\\n            , propertyDescription = propDescription prop\\n            }\\n\\n    schemaTypeToParamType :: PropertySchema -> ParamType\\n    schemaTypeToParamType prop =\\n        case propEnum prop of\\n            Just vals -> EnumParamType vals\\n            Nothing -> case propType prop of\\n                Just \\\"string\\\" -> StringParamType\\n                Just \\\"integer\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"number\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"boolean\\\" -> BoolParamType\\n                Just \\\"array\\\" -> case propItems prop of\\n                    Just items -> OpaqueParamType (fromMaybe \\\"any\\\" (propType items))\\n                    Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"array\\\"\\n                Just \\\"object\\\" -> ObjectParamType []\\n                Just other -> OpaqueParamType other\\n                Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"any\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Handler Helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Default handler that always returns an error.\\n-- The actual handler should be provided by the caller.\\ndefaultHandler :: ToolHandler\\ndefaultHandler _ = pure $ Left \\\"Tool handler not implemented\\\"\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE GeneralizedNewtypeDeriving #-}\\n\\nmodule System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI (\\n  Tool(..),Trace(..),ToolCall(..),ToolCallFunction(..),ToolName(..), Model(..), ModelFlavor(..)\\n , ApiKey(..)\\n , parseFlavor\\n , ApiBaseUrl(..)\\n , SystemPrompt(..)\\n , Response(..)\\n , callLLMPayload\\n , parseLLMResponse\\n , waitRateLimit\\n , parseRateLimitDelay\\n , ApiLimits(..)\\n , WaitAction(..)\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Concurrent (threadDelay)\\nimport Data.Aeson (FromJSON, ToJSON, Value (..), (.:), (.:?), (.=))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.Types as Aeson\\nimport Data.ByteString (ByteString)\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Char8 as C8\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LByteString\\nimport Data.List.NonEmpty (NonEmpty)\\nimport qualified Data.List.NonEmpty as NonEmpty\\nimport Data.String (IsString)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport Data.Text.Read as Text\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Client as NetHttpClient\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..), contramap, runTracer)\\nimport Text.Read (readMaybe)\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient\\nimport System.Agents.ToolSchema\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\ndata Trace\\n    = CallChatCompletion !Aeson.Value\\n    | GotChatCompletion !Aeson.Value\\n    | HttpClientTrace !HttpClient.Trace\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nnewtype ApiBaseUrl\\n    = ApiBaseUrl {getBaseUrl :: Text}\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ntype Remaining = Int\\ntype Reset = Text\\n\\nextractRequestRateLimitFromResponse :: NetHttpClient.Response body -> (Maybe Remaining, Maybe Reset)\\nextractRequestRateLimitFromResponse rsp =\\n    let\\n        remaining = Prelude.lookup \\\"x-ratelimit-remaining-requests\\\" (NetHttpClient.responseHeaders rsp) >>= readMaybe . C8.unpack :: Maybe Int\\n        resetStr = Prelude.lookup \\\"x-ratelimit-reset-requests\\\" (NetHttpClient.responseHeaders rsp) >>= pure . Text.decodeUtf8\\n     in\\n        (remaining, resetStr)\\n\\nextractTokenRateLimitFromResponse :: NetHttpClient.Response body -> (Maybe Remaining, Maybe Reset)\\nextractTokenRateLimitFromResponse rsp =\\n    let\\n        remaining = Prelude.lookup \\\"x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens\\\" (NetHttpClient.responseHeaders rsp) >>= readMaybe . C8.unpack :: Maybe Int\\n        resetStr = Prelude.lookup \\\"x-ratelimit-reset-tokens\\\" (NetHttpClient.responseHeaders rsp) >>= pure . Text.decodeUtf8\\n     in\\n        (remaining, resetStr)\\n\\ndata ApiLimits = ApiLimits\\n    { requestCushion :: Remaining\\n    , tokenCushion :: Remaining\\n    }\\n\\ndata WaitAction\\n    = WaitOnTokens Int\\n    | WaitOnRequests Int\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq)\\n\\nevalWaitAction :: ApiLimits -> NetHttpClient.Response a -> Maybe WaitAction\\nevalWaitAction lims rsp =\\n    let\\n        (r0, rst0) = extractRequestRateLimitFromResponse rsp\\n        (r1, rst1) = extractTokenRateLimitFromResponse rsp\\n        p0 = maybe False (< lims.requestCushion) r0\\n        p1 = maybe False (< lims.tokenCushion) r1\\n        delay0 = maybe (-1) parseRateLimitDelay rst0\\n        delay1 = maybe (-1) parseRateLimitDelay rst1\\n     in\\n        doWait p0 delay0 p1 delay1\\n  where\\n    doWait True d0 True d1\\n        | d1 > d0 = Just $ WaitOnTokens d1\\n        | otherwise = Just $ WaitOnRequests d0\\n    doWait True d0 False _ = Just $ WaitOnRequests d0\\n    doWait False _ True d1 = Just $ WaitOnTokens d1\\n    doWait False _ False _ = Nothing\\n\\n{- | Parses the ratelimit reset header syntaxes.\\nhttps://community.openai.com/t/what-is-new-field-in-rate-limits-x-ratelimit-reset-tokens-usage-based/541210\\nI've not found a grammar for the format so I made up examples found, data seen, and hallucinated.\\n-}\\nparseRateLimitDelay :: Text -> Int\\nparseRateLimitDelay t0 =\\n    let\\n        (d, t1) = getDays t0\\n        (h, t2) = getHours t1\\n        (m, t3) = getMin t2\\n        (ms, _) = getMsec t3\\n        (s, _) = getSec t3\\n        secs = if ms > 0 then 1 else s\\n     in\\n        secs + 60 * (m + 60 * (h + 24 * d))\\n  where\\n    getDays, getHours, getMin, getSec :: Text -> (Int, Text)\\n    getDays = breakInt \\\"d\\\"\\n    getHours = breakInt \\\"h\\\"\\n    -- special handling of `ms` confusing minutes\\n    getMin t =\\n        let (val, rest) = breakInt \\\"m\\\" t\\n         in if \\\"s\\\" `Text.isPrefixOf` rest then (0, t) else (val, rest)\\n    getSec t =\\n        let (val, rest) = breakFloat \\\"s\\\" t\\n         in (ceiling val, rest)\\n    getMsec = breakInt \\\"ms\\\"\\n\\n    -- special handling of sub-seconds: we'll wait for a whole second anyway,\\n    -- so we round-up the value during parsing\\n    breakInt :: Text -> Text -> (Int, Text)\\n    breakInt char txt =\\n        let (val, rest) = Text.breakOn char txt\\n         in if rest == \\\"\\\"\\n                then (0, txt)\\n                else (readDecimalOrZero val, Text.drop (Text.length char) rest)\\n\\n    breakFloat :: Text -> Text -> (Float, Text)\\n    breakFloat char txt =\\n        let (val, rest) = Text.breakOn char txt\\n         in if rest == \\\"\\\"\\n                then (0, txt)\\n                else (readFloatOrZero val, Text.drop (Text.length char) rest)\\n\\n    readDecimalOrZero :: Text -> Int\\n    readDecimalOrZero txt =\\n        case Text.decimal txt of\\n            Right (v, _) -> v\\n            Left _ -> 0\\n\\n    readFloatOrZero :: Text -> Float\\n    readFloatOrZero txt =\\n        case Text.rational txt of\\n            Right (v, _) -> v\\n            Left _ -> 0\\n\\nwaitRateLimit :: ApiLimits -> (WaitAction -> IO ()) -> Tracer IO Trace\\nwaitRateLimit lims onWait = Tracer go\\n  where\\n    go :: Trace -> IO ()\\n    go (HttpClientTrace (HttpClient.RunRequest _ rsp)) = do\\n        case evalWaitAction lims rsp of\\n            Nothing -> pure ()\\n            (Just w@(WaitOnTokens n)) -> do\\n                onWait w\\n                threadDelay (1000000 * n)\\n            (Just w@(WaitOnRequests n)) -> do\\n                onWait w\\n                threadDelay (1000000 * n)\\n    go _ = pure ()\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nnewtype ToolName = ToolName {getToolName :: Text}\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)\\n\\ndata Tool = Tool\\n    { toolName :: ToolName\\n    , toolDescription :: Text\\n    , toolParamProperties :: [ParamProperty]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Tool where\\n    toJSON t =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"type\\\" .= (\\\"function\\\" :: Text)\\n            , \\\"function\\\"\\n                .= Aeson.object\\n                    [ \\\"name\\\" .= t.toolName.getToolName\\n                    , \\\"description\\\" .= t.toolDescription\\n                    , \\\"parameters\\\" .= Aeson.object (jsonSchema toplevelProperty)\\n                    ]\\n            ]\\n      where\\n        toplevelProperty :: ParamProperty\\n        toplevelProperty =\\n            ParamProperty\\n                t.toolName.getToolName\\n                (ObjectParamType t.toolParamProperties)\\n                t.toolDescription\\n\\ndata ToolResponse\\n    = ToolNotFound\\n    | TextToolResponse (NonEmpty Text)\\n    | ToolFailure Text\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)\\n\\nnewtype SystemPrompt = SystemPrompt {getSystemPrompt :: Text}\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord, IsString)\\n\\nnewtype ApiKey = ApiKey {revealApiKey :: ByteString}\\n\\ndata ModelFlavor\\n    = OpenAIv1\\n    | MistralV1\\n    | KimiV1\\n    | ClaudeV1\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)\\n\\ndata Model = Model\\n    { modelFlavor :: ModelFlavor\\n    , modelBaseUrl :: ApiBaseUrl\\n    , modelName :: Text\\n    , modelSystemPrompt :: SystemPrompt\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq, Ord)\\n\\nparseFlavor :: Text -> Maybe ModelFlavor\\nparseFlavor \\\"kimiv1\\\" = Just KimiV1\\nparseFlavor \\\"KimiV1\\\" = Just KimiV1\\nparseFlavor \\\"OpenAIv1\\\" = Just OpenAIv1\\nparseFlavor \\\"MistralV1\\\" = Just MistralV1\\nparseFlavor \\\"openai-v1\\\" = Just OpenAIv1\\nparseFlavor \\\"mistral-v1\\\" = Just MistralV1\\nparseFlavor \\\"claude-v1\\\" = Just ClaudeV1\\nparseFlavor \\\"ClaudeV1\\\" = Just ClaudeV1\\nparseFlavor _ = Nothing\\n\\n\\ncallLLMPayload ::\\n    (ToJSON payload) =>\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    ApiBaseUrl ->\\n    payload ->\\n    IO (Either String Value)\\ncallLLMPayload tracer rt (ApiBaseUrl baseUrl) payload = do\\n    let payloadVal = Aeson.toJSON payload\\n    runTracer tracer (CallChatCompletion payloadVal)\\n    httpRsp <- rt.post (contramap HttpClientTrace tracer) (baseUrl <> \\\"/chat/completions\\\") (Just payloadVal)\\n    case httpRsp of\\n        Left (HttpClient.SomeError err) -> do\\n            pure $ Left err\\n        Right rsp -> do\\n            case HttpClient.decodeBody (HttpClient.J rsp) of\\n                Nothing -> pure $ Left \\\"json decode body error\\\"\\n                Just body -> do\\n                    runTracer tracer (GotChatCompletion body)\\n                    pure $ Right body\\n\\ndata ToolCallFunction\\n    = ToolCallFunction\\n    { toolCallFunctionName :: ToolName\\n    , toolCallFunctionArgsUnparsed :: Text\\n    , toolCallFunctionArgs :: Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON ToolCallFunction where\\n    parseJSON =\\n        Aeson.withObject \\\"ToolCallFunction\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n            argsStr <- v .: \\\"arguments\\\"\\n            let argVal = Aeson.decode' (LByteString.fromStrict $ Text.encodeUtf8 argsStr) :: Maybe Aeson.Value\\n            ToolCallFunction\\n                <$> (ToolName <$> v .: \\\"name\\\")\\n                <*> pure argsStr\\n                <*> pure argVal\\n\\ndata ToolCall\\n    = ToolCall\\n    { rawToolCall :: Aeson.Object\\n    , toolCallId :: Text\\n    , toolCallType :: Maybe Text\\n    , toolCallFunction :: ToolCallFunction\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON ToolCall where\\n    parseJSON =\\n        Aeson.withObject \\\"ToolCall\\\" $ \\\\v ->\\n            ToolCall\\n                <$> pure v\\n                <*> v .: \\\"id\\\"\\n                <*> v .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n                <*> v .: \\\"function\\\"\\n\\ndata Response\\n    = Response\\n    { rawResponse :: Aeson.Object\\n    , chosenMessage :: Aeson.Object\\n    , finishReason :: Maybe Text\\n    , rspContent :: Maybe Text\\n    , rspToolCalls :: Maybe [ToolCall]\\n    , rspReasoningContent :: Maybe Text\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Response where\\n    parseJSON =\\n        Aeson.withObject \\\"Response\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n            choices <- v .: \\\"choices\\\" :: Aeson.Parser (NonEmpty Aeson.Object)\\n            firstChoice <- (NonEmpty.head choices) .: \\\"message\\\"\\n            Response\\n                <$> pure v\\n                <*> pure firstChoice\\n                <*> firstChoice .:? \\\"finish_reason\\\"\\n                <*> firstChoice .: \\\"content\\\"\\n                <*> firstChoice .:? \\\"tool_calls\\\"\\n                <*> firstChoice .:? \\\"reasoning_content\\\"\\n\\nparseLLMResponse :: Value -> Aeson.Parser Response\\nparseLLMResponse v = Aeson.parseJSON v\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{- | Provides a runtime value capable to load and reload a mcp list of tools.\\nNote that reloads are asynchronous.\\n-}\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox where\\n\\nimport Control.Concurrent.Async (Async, async)\\nimport Control.Concurrent.MVar (newEmptyMVar, putMVar, takeMVar)\\nimport Control.Concurrent.STM (atomically)\\nimport Control.Concurrent.STM.TBMChan (newTBMChanIO, readTBMChan, writeTBMChan)\\nimport Control.Concurrent.STM.TVar (TVar, newTVarIO, writeTVar)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Network.JSONRPC as Rpc\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..), contramap, traceBoth)\\nimport System.Process (CreateProcess)\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Base as Mcp\\nimport System.Agents.MCP.Client (LoopProps (..), LoopTrace (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Client as McpClient\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Client.Runtime as McpClient\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nnewtype ToolDescription = ToolDescription {getToolDescription :: Mcp.Tool}\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ndata Trace\\n    = McpClientRunTrace !McpClient.RunTrace\\n    | McpClientClientTrace !McpClient.ClientTrace\\n    | McpClientLoopTrace !McpClient.LoopTrace\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ndata Toolbox = Toolbox\\n    { name :: Text\\n    , job :: Async ()\\n    , toolsList :: TVar [ToolDescription]\\n    , callTool :: ToolDescription -> Maybe Aeson.Object -> IO (McpClient.ToolCallResponse)\\n    }\\n\\ninitializeMcpToolbox ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    Text ->\\n    CreateProcess ->\\n    IO Toolbox\\ninitializeMcpToolbox ttracer tname proc = do\\n    -- tool calls\\n    chan <- newTBMChanIO 30\\n    let nextToolCall = atomically $ readTBMChan chan\\n    let doCallTool :: ToolDescription -> Maybe Aeson.Object -> IO McpClient.ToolCallResponse\\n        doCallTool td param = do\\n            let tc = McpClient.ToolCall td.getToolDescription.name param\\n            mbox <- newEmptyMVar\\n            let done res = do\\n                    putMVar mbox res\\n            let fullcall = McpClient.FullToolCall tc done\\n            atomically $ writeTBMChan chan fullcall\\n            takeMVar mbox\\n\\n    -- tool discovery\\n    discoveredTools <- newTVarIO []\\n    let toolsListTracer = storeToolsInDiscoveredValues discoveredTools\\n    -- tracers\\n    let rtTracer = contramap McpClientRunTrace ttracer\\n    let clientTracer = contramap McpClientClientTrace ttracer\\n    let loopTracer = traceBoth toolsListTracer (contramap McpClientLoopTrace ttracer)\\n\\n    -- wire things together\\n    mcpRt <- McpClient.initRuntime rtTracer proc\\n    let props = LoopProps loopTracer nextToolCall\\n    ajob <- async (McpClient.runClient clientTracer mcpRt (McpClient.defaultLoop props))\\n    pure $ Toolbox tname ajob discoveredTools doCallTool\\n\\nstoreToolsInDiscoveredValues :: TVar [ToolDescription] -> Tracer IO LoopTrace\\nstoreToolsInDiscoveredValues list = Tracer f\\n  where\\n    f :: LoopTrace -> IO ()\\n    f (StartToolCall _ _) = pure ()\\n    f (EndToolCall _ _ _) = pure ()\\n    f (ExitingToolCallLoop) =\\n        atomically (writeTVar list [])\\n    f (ToolsRefreshed mitems) =\\n        let\\n            g :: Maybe (Either Rpc.ErrorObj McpClient.ListToolsResultRsp) -> [ToolDescription]\\n            g (Just (Right rsp)) = fmap ToolDescription rsp.getListToolsResult.tools\\n            g _ = []\\n\\n            items = mconcat $ map g mitems\\n         in\\n            atomically (writeTVar list items)\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}\\n\\n{- | Provides some abstraction of tools that agents can use.\\n\\nTo date, there are two ways to provide tools:\\n* bash tools (arbitrary code running in a separate process, and defined with an external command)\\n* IO tools (arbitrary code running in the same process, and defined with Haskell code)\\n* MCP tools (tools exposed via Model Context Protocol)\\n* OpenAPI tools (tools generated from OpenAPI specifications)\\n\\nTechnically, we could implement bash-tools as a sepecific implementation of\\nIO-tools however having a separate constructor enables to surface a bit more\\ninformation (when announcing a tool to an LLM, when logging etc.).\\nThus, a merge may happen at some point, but not just yet.\\n-}\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools (\\n    -- * Re-exports from Base\\n    Tool (..),\\n    ToolDef (..),\\n    CallResult (..),\\n    mapToolResult,\\n    mapCallResult,\\n    extractCall,\\n    \\n    -- * Re-exports from Trace\\n    module System.Agents.Tools.Trace,\\n    \\n    -- * Tool builders\\n    ioTool,\\n    bashTool,\\n    mcpTool,\\n    openapiTool,\\n) where\\n\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.Types as Aeson\\nimport Data.ByteString.Char8 as CByteString\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport Prod.Tracer (contramap)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Client as McpClient\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Base\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.Bash as BashTools\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.IO as IOTools\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox as McpTools\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter as OpenAPI\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox as OpenAPIToolbox\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Trace\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on a Bash script-description.\\n--\\n-- When executed, the script will have access to session context via environment\\n-- variables (see 'System.Agents.Tools.Bash.runValue' for details):\\n--\\n-- * @AGENT_SESSION_ID@ - The current session UUID\\n-- * @AGENT_CONVERSATION_ID@ - The conversation UUID\\n-- * @AGENT_TURN_ID@ - The current turn UUID\\n-- * @AGENT_AGENT_ID@ - The agent UUID (if available)\\n-- * @AGENT_SESSION_JSON@ - Full session as JSON (if available)\\n--\\n-- Scripts can use these variables for logging, audit trails, or context-aware\\n-- processing without requiring explicit parameters from the LLM.\\nbashTool ::\\n    BashTools.ScriptDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nbashTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = BashTool script\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        ret <- BashTools.runValue (contramap BashToolsTrace tracer) script (Just ctx) v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ BashToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an MCP toolbox tool.\\n--\\n-- MCP tools are exposed via the Model Context Protocol and provide\\n-- standardized tool definitions with structured schemas.\\nmcpTool ::\\n    McpTools.Toolbox ->\\n    McpTools.ToolDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nmcpTool toolbox desc =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = MCPTool desc\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run _tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do\\n        ret <- McpTools.callTool toolbox desc (Just v)\\n        case ret of\\n            (Just (Right rsp)) -> pure $ extractContentsFromToolCall rsp\\n            err -> pure $ McpToolError call (mconcat [\\\"calling error: \\\", show err])\\n    run _tracer _ctx _ = do\\n        pure $ McpToolError call (\\\"can only call McpTools with Aeson.Object\\\")\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall :: McpClient.CallToolResultRsp -> CallResult ()\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall rsp =\\n        McpToolResult call rsp.getCallToolResult\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for an OpenAPI-based tool.\\n--\\n-- This creates a tool from an OpenAPI operation that can be executed\\n-- against an API endpoint. The tool handles:\\n--\\n-- * Path parameter substitution (parameters prefixed with 'p_')\\n-- * Query parameter construction (parameters prefixed with 'p_')\\n-- * Request body serialization (parameter 'b')\\n-- * HTTP method execution\\n-- * Response parsing\\n--\\n-- Example usage:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- let toolbox = ... -- initialized OpenAPI toolbox\\n-- let apiTool = head (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxTools toolbox)\\n-- let tool = openapiTool toolbox apiTool\\n-- -- Use tool with agent runtime...\\n-- @\\n--\\n-- The tool name and description are derived from the OpenAPI operation,\\n-- and the execution uses the HTTP runtime from the toolbox.\\nopenapiTool ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tool ()\\nopenapiTool toolbox apiTool =\\n    let opId = fromMaybe (OpenAPI.toolName apiTool) (OpenAPIToolbox.getOperationId (OpenAPI.toolOperation apiTool))\\n    in Tool\\n        { toolDef = OpenAPITool (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxName toolbox) opId\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run _tracer _ctx args = do\\n        result <- OpenAPIToolbox.handleToolCall toolbox apiTool args\\n        case result of\\n            Left err -> do\\n                pure $ OpenAPIToolError call (Text.unpack err)\\n            Right (_textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                pure $ OpenAPIToolResult call toolResult\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an IO-tool script-description.\\n--\\n-- The IO action receives the full 'ToolExecutionContext', giving it direct access\\n-- to session metadata including:\\n--\\n-- * 'ctxSessionId' - The current session identifier\\n-- * 'ctxConversationId' - The conversation identifier\\n-- * 'ctxTurnId' - The current turn identifier\\n-- * 'ctxAgentId' - Optional agent identifier\\n-- * 'ctxFullSession' - Optional complete session data\\n--\\n-- This is a breaking change from the previous API where IO scripts received a\\n-- 'ConversationId'. To migrate existing tools:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- -- Before:\\n-- ioRun :: ConversationId -> MyArg -> IO ByteString\\n-- ioRun _convId arg = ...\\n--\\n-- -- After:\\n-- ioRun :: ToolExecutionContext -> MyArg -> IO ByteString\\n-- ioRun ctx arg = ...\\n-- -- Use ctx when needed, or ignore with _ctx if not needed\\n-- @\\n--\\n-- Example tool using context:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- logTool :: IOScript LogArg ByteString\\n-- logTool = IOScript\\n--     { description = IOScriptDescription \\\"log-tool\\\" \\\"Logs with session context\\\"\\n--     , ioRun = \\\\ctx arg -> do\\n--         let sessionId = ctxSessionId ctx\\n--         logWithSession sessionId arg.message\\n--         pure \\\"logged\\\"\\n--     }\\n-- @\\nioTool ::\\n    (Aeson.FromJSON llmArg) =>\\n    IOTools.IOScript llmArg ByteString ->\\n    Tool ()\\nioTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = IOTool script.description\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        -- we trace the original input object\\n        let adaptTrace = IOToolsTrace . IOTools.adaptTraceInput (const v)\\n        ret <- IOTools.runValue (contramap adaptTrace tracer) script ctx v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ IOToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema reference resolution ($ref dereferencing) for OpenAPI specs.\\n--\\n-- This module provides functions to recursively resolve '$ref' pointers in\\n-- OpenAPI and Swagger 2.0 specs. This is essential because OpenAPI specs use\\n-- references to avoid duplication and improve maintainability.\\n--\\n-- Supports both OpenAPI 3.x refs (@#/components/schemas/Name@) and\\n-- Swagger 2.0 refs (@#/definitions/Name@).\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\n-- >>> let petSchema = Schema (Just \\\"object\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n-- >>> let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n-- >>> let refSchema = Schema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n-- >>> resolveSchema refSchema components\\n-- Schema {schemaType = Just \\\"object\\\", ...}\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver (\\n    -- * Core resolution functions\\n    resolveSchema,\\n    dereferenceSpec,\\n\\n    -- * Reference path handling\\n    RefPath (..),\\n    parseRef,\\n\\n    -- * Resolution errors\\n    ResolutionError (..),\\n\\n    -- * Low-level resolution\\n    resolveRef,\\n    resolveSchemaWithDepth,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Monad (when)\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)\\nimport Data.Set (Set)\\nimport qualified Data.Set as Set\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Components (..),\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Reference path representation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parsed JSON reference path.\\n--\\n-- A reference like @#/components/schemas/Pet@ is parsed into:\\n-- @\\n-- RefPath { refComponents = \\\"components\\\"\\n--         , refSection = \\\"schemas\\\"\\n--         , refName = \\\"Pet\\\"\\n--         }\\n-- @\\n--\\n-- For Swagger 2.0 refs like @#/definitions/Pet@:\\n-- @\\n-- RefPath { refComponents = \\\"definitions\\\"\\n--         , refSection = \\\"\\\"\\n--         , refName = \\\"Pet\\\"\\n--         }\\n-- @\\ndata RefPath = RefPath\\n    { refComponents :: Text\\n    -- ^ The first path segment (usually \\\"components\\\" or \\\"definitions\\\")\\n    , refSection :: Text\\n    -- ^ The second path segment (e.g., \\\"schemas\\\", \\\"parameters\\\") or empty for Swagger 2.0\\n    , refName :: Text\\n    -- ^ The name of the referenced entity\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a reference string into a 'RefPath'.\\n--\\n-- Supports internal references of the form:\\n-- * @#/components/schemas/Name@ (OpenAPI 3.x)\\n-- * @#/definitions/Name@ (Swagger 2.0)\\n--\\n-- External references (URLs) are not supported and will return 'Nothing'.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> parseRef \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\"\\n-- Just (RefPath {refComponents = \\\"components\\\", refSection = \\\"schemas\\\", refName = \\\"Pet\\\"})\\n--\\n-- >>> parseRef \\\"#/definitions/Pet\\\"\\n-- Just (RefPath {refComponents = \\\"definitions\\\", refSection = \\\"\\\", refName = \\\"Pet\\\"})\\n--\\n-- >>> parseRef \\\"http://example.com/schema.json\\\"\\n-- Nothing\\nparseRef :: Text -> Maybe RefPath\\nparseRef ref\\n    -- External refs (http/https) are not supported\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"http://\\\" ref = Nothing\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"https://\\\" ref = Nothing\\n    -- Internal refs starting with #/\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"#/\\\" ref =\\n        let parts = Text.splitOn \\\"/\\\" (Text.drop 2 ref)\\n         in case parts of\\n                -- OpenAPI 3.x format: #/components/schemas/Name\\n                (\\\"components\\\" : \\\"schemas\\\" : name : _) ->\\n                    Just $ RefPath \\\"components\\\" \\\"schemas\\\" name\\n                -- Swagger 2.0 format: #/definitions/Name\\n                (\\\"definitions\\\" : name : _) ->\\n                    Just $ RefPath \\\"definitions\\\" \\\"\\\" name\\n                -- Generic format: try to extract name from the last segment\\n                parts' | not (null parts') ->\\n                    Just $ RefPath (head parts') (if length parts' > 2 then parts' !! 1 else \\\"\\\") (last parts')\\n                _ -> Nothing\\n    -- Relative refs not starting with #/ are not supported\\n    | otherwise = Nothing\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Resolution errors\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during reference resolution.\\ndata ResolutionError\\n    = -- | The reference path is malformed or unsupported\\n      InvalidRefPath Text\\n    | -- | The referenced schema was not found in components\\n      SchemaNotFound Text\\n    | -- | A circular reference was detected\\n      CircularReference [Text]\\n    | -- | Maximum recursion depth exceeded (safety limit)\\n      MaxDepthExceeded Int\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core resolution functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Default maximum recursion depth to prevent infinite loops.\\ndefaultMaxDepth :: Int\\ndefaultMaxDepth = 100\\n\\n-- | Recursively resolves all '$ref' pointers in a schema.\\n--\\n-- This function will follow references transitively until no more\\n-- references remain, or an error condition is encountered.\\n--\\n-- For simple resolution without error handling, use 'resolveSchema'.\\nresolveSchemaWithDepth ::\\n    -- | Schema to resolve\\n    Schema ->\\n    -- | Components containing schema definitions\\n    Components ->\\n    -- | Current resolution depth (for tracking)\\n    Int ->\\n    -- | Set of already-visited refs (for cycle detection)\\n    Set Text ->\\n    -- | Either an error or the fully-resolved schema\\n    Either ResolutionError Schema\\nresolveSchemaWithDepth schema components depth visited = do\\n    -- Check recursion depth\\n    when (depth > defaultMaxDepth) $\\n        Left (MaxDepthExceeded defaultMaxDepth)\\n\\n    case schema.schemaRef of\\n        -- If this schema has a $ref, resolve it\\n        Just ref -> do\\n            -- Check for circular reference\\n            when (Set.member ref visited) $\\n                Left (CircularReference (Set.toList $ Set.insert ref visited))\\n\\n            -- Parse and resolve the reference\\n            resolved <- resolveRef ref components\\n            -- Recursively resolve the referenced schema\\n            resolveSchemaWithDepth\\n                resolved\\n                components\\n                (depth + 1)\\n                (Set.insert ref visited)\\n        -- No $ref, recursively resolve nested schemas\\n        Nothing -> do\\n            resolvedProps <- resolveProperties schema.schemaProperties\\n            resolvedItems <- resolveItems schema.schemaItems\\n            resolvedAnyOf <- resolveAnyOf schema.schemaAnyOf\\n            pure $\\n                schema\\n                    { schemaProperties = resolvedProps\\n                    , schemaItems = resolvedItems\\n                    , schemaAnyOf = resolvedAnyOf\\n                    }\\n  where\\n    resolveProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema) -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe (Map Text Schema))\\n    resolveProperties Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveProperties (Just props) =\\n        Just <$> traverse (\\\\s -> resolveSchemaWithDepth s components depth visited) props\\n\\n    resolveItems :: Maybe Schema -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe Schema)\\n    resolveItems Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveItems (Just items) =\\n        Just <$> resolveSchemaWithDepth items components depth visited\\n\\n    resolveAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema] -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe [Schema])\\n    resolveAnyOf Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveAnyOf (Just schemas) =\\n        Just <$> traverse (\\\\s -> resolveSchemaWithDepth s components depth visited) schemas\\n\\n-- | Resolve a reference path to a schema from components.\\n--\\n-- Looks up the schema in the components/schemas section.\\n-- Supports both OpenAPI 3.x refs (#/components/schemas/Name) and\\n-- Swagger 2.0 refs (#/definitions/Name).\\nresolveRef :: Text -> Components -> Either ResolutionError Schema\\nresolveRef ref components = do\\n    refPath <- maybe (Left $ InvalidRefPath ref) Right (parseRef ref)\\n\\n    schemas <-\\n        maybe (Left $ SchemaNotFound ref) Right (components.componentsSchemas)\\n\\n    schema <-\\n        maybe (Left $ SchemaNotFound ref) Right (Map.lookup refPath.refName schemas)\\n\\n    pure schema\\n\\n-- | Recursively resolves all '$ref' pointers in a schema.\\n--\\n-- This is the main entry point for schema resolution. It handles:\\n--\\n-- * Simple references like @#/components/schemas/Pet@ or @#/definitions/Pet@\\n-- * Nested references (schema A refs B refs C)\\n-- * Arrays with item references\\n-- * Object properties with references\\n-- * anyOf schemas with references\\n--\\n-- For circular references, the original $ref is returned to prevent infinite loops.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n-- >>> resolveSchema refSchema components\\n-- Schema {schemaType = Just \\\"object\\\", ...}\\nresolveSchema :: Schema -> Components -> Schema\\nresolveSchema schema components =\\n    case resolveSchemaWithDepth schema components 0 Set.empty of\\n        Left err ->\\n            -- On error, return a schema with an error description\\n            -- but preserve the original to avoid losing information\\n            schema\\n                { schemaDescription =\\n                    Just $ \\\"Reference resolution failed: \\\" <> Text.pack (show err)\\n                }\\n        Right resolved -> resolved\\n\\n-- | Resolves all schemas within an OpenAPI spec.\\n--\\n-- This function walks through the entire spec and resolves all\\n-- schema references in:\\n--\\n-- * Path parameters\\n-- * Operation parameters\\n-- * Request bodies\\n-- * Response schemas (when implemented)\\n--\\n-- The returned spec has all $ref pointers fully resolved.\\ndereferenceSpec :: OpenAPISpec -> OpenAPISpec\\ndereferenceSpec spec =\\n    let comps = fromMaybe emptyComponents spec.specComponents\\n        resolvedPaths = Map.map (Map.map (resolveOperation comps)) spec.specPaths\\n     in spec{specPaths = resolvedPaths}\\n  where\\n    emptyComponents :: Components\\n    emptyComponents = Components Nothing\\n\\n    resolveOperation :: Components -> Operation -> Operation\\n    resolveOperation comps op =\\n        op\\n            { opParameters = map (resolveParameter comps) op.opParameters\\n            , opRequestBody = fmap (resolveRequestBody comps) op.opRequestBody\\n            }\\n\\n    resolveParameter :: Components -> Parameter -> Parameter\\n    resolveParameter comps param =\\n        param{paramSchema = fmap (\\\\s -> resolveSchema s comps) param.paramSchema}\\n\\n    resolveRequestBody :: Components -> RequestBody -> RequestBody\\n    resolveRequestBody comps body =\\n        body{reqBodyContent = Map.map (\\\\s -> resolveSchema s comps) body.reqBodyContent}\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}\\n\\n-- | Defines an LLM tool registration.\\n--\\n-- This module provides functionality for registering tools with the LLM system.\\n-- It supports registration of various tool types:\\n--\\n-- * Bash tools - External scripts executed in separate processes\\n-- * IO tools - Haskell functions executed in-process\\n-- * MCP tools - Tools exposed via Model Context Protocol\\n-- * OpenAPI tools - Tools generated from OpenAPI specifications\\n--\\n-- Each tool type has specific registration functions that handle the\\n-- conversion to LLM-compatible format.\\nmodule System.Agents.ToolRegistration (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    ToolRegistration (..),\\n    \\n    -- * Registration functions\\n    registerBashToolInLLM,\\n    registerIOScriptInLLM,\\n    registerMcpToolInLLM,\\n    registerOpenAPIToolInLLM,\\n    registerOpenAPITools,\\n    registerOpenAPITool,\\n    \\n    -- * Naming policies\\n    io2LLMName,\\n    bash2LLMName,\\n    mcp2LLMName,\\n    openapi2LLMName,\\n) where\\n\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey\\nimport Data.ByteString (ByteString)\\nimport Data.Foldable.WithIndex (ifoldl')\\nimport qualified Data.Maybe as Maybe\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Base as Mcp\\nimport qualified System.Agents.MCP.Client as McpClient\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Base (\\n    CallResult (..),\\n    Tool (..),\\n    ToolDef (..),\\n    mapToolResult,\\n )\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolExecutionContext)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.IO (IOScript (..), IOScriptDescription (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.IO as IOTools\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Bash (ScriptDescription (..), ScriptArg (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.Bash as BashTools\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox (callTool)\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox as McpTools\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (toOpenAITool)\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter as OpenAPI\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox (\\n    createToolHandler,\\n    openapi2LLMName,\\n  )\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox as OpenAPIToolbox\\nimport System.Agents.ToolSchema\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Trace (ToolTrace (..))\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer, contramap)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | We register tools that will take a ToolExecutionContext for execution.\\n--\\n-- The 'innerTool' field uses 'Tool ()' since the tool execution context\\n-- is passed at runtime via 'toolRun', not stored in the tool itself.\\ndata ToolRegistration\\n    = ToolRegistration\\n    { innerTool :: Tool ()\\n    , declareTool :: OpenAI.Tool\\n    , findTool :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n    }\\ninstance Show ToolRegistration where\\n    show (ToolRegistration d _ _) = Prelude.unwords [\\\"ToolRegistration(\\\", show d.toolDef, \\\")\\\"]\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- naming policy for IO tools\\nio2LLMName :: forall a b. IOScript a b -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nio2LLMName io = OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat [\\\"io_\\\", io.description.ioSlug])\\n\\n-- naming policy for Bash tools\\nbash2LLMName :: ScriptDescription -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nbash2LLMName bash = OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat [\\\"bash_\\\", bash.scriptInfo.scriptSlug])\\n\\n-- naming policy for MCP tools\\nmcp2LLMName :: McpTools.Toolbox -> McpTools.ToolDescription -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nmcp2LLMName box mcp =\\n    OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat [\\\"mcp_\\\", box.name, \\\"_\\\", mcp.getToolDescription.name])\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nregisterBashToolInLLM ::\\n    ScriptDescription ->\\n    ToolRegistration\\nregisterBashToolInLLM script =\\n    let\\n        matchName :: ScriptDescription -> OpenAI.ToolCall -> Bool\\n        matchName bash call = bash2LLMName bash == call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName\\n\\n        mapToolDescriptionBash2LLM :: ScriptDescription -> OpenAI.Tool\\n        mapToolDescriptionBash2LLM bash =\\n            OpenAI.Tool\\n                { OpenAI.toolName = bash2LLMName bash\\n                , OpenAI.toolDescription = bash.scriptInfo.scriptDescription\\n                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = fmap mapArg bash.scriptInfo.scriptArgs\\n                }\\n\\n        mapArg :: ScriptArg -> ParamProperty\\n        mapArg arg =\\n            ParamProperty\\n                { propertyKey = arg.argName\\n                , propertyType = OpaqueParamType arg.argBackingTypeString\\n                , propertyDescription = arg.argDescription\\n                }\\n\\n        tool :: Tool ()\\n        tool = bashTool script\\n\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call = if matchName script call then Just (mapToolResult (const call) tool) else Nothing\\n     in\\n        ToolRegistration tool (mapToolDescriptionBash2LLM script) find\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n{- | registers an IO Script, since we have not yet decided on a way to capture the\\nshape of the tool for IOScript (ideally some generics or something like Data.Aeson.Encoding) we take the whole Tool definition\\n-}\\nregisterIOScriptInLLM ::\\n    (Aeson.FromJSON a) =>\\n    IOScript a ByteString ->\\n    [ParamProperty] ->\\n    ToolRegistration\\nregisterIOScriptInLLM script llmProps =\\n    let\\n        matchName :: IOScript a b -> OpenAI.ToolCall -> Bool\\n        matchName io call = io2LLMName io == call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName\\n\\n        tool :: Tool ()\\n        tool = ioTool script\\n\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call = if matchName script call then Just (mapToolResult (const call) tool) else Nothing\\n\\n        llmTool :: OpenAI.Tool\\n        llmTool =\\n            OpenAI.Tool\\n                { OpenAI.toolName = io2LLMName script\\n                , OpenAI.toolDescription = script.description.ioDescription\\n                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = llmProps\\n                }\\n     in\\n        ToolRegistration tool llmTool find\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Register an MCP tool with the LLM system.\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Left' if the tool's schema cannot be adapted to the LLM format.\\nregisterMcpToolInLLM ::\\n    McpTools.Toolbox ->\\n    McpTools.ToolDescription ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterMcpToolInLLM box mcp =\\n    let\\n        matchName :: McpTools.ToolDescription -> OpenAI.ToolCall -> Bool\\n        matchName td call = mcp2LLMName box td == call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName\\n\\n        llmBasedSchema :: Either String [ParamProperty]\\n        llmBasedSchema = adaptSchema mcp.getToolDescription.inputSchema\\n\\n        llmName :: OpenAI.ToolName\\n        llmName = mcp2LLMName box mcp\\n\\n        llmDescription :: Text\\n        llmDescription = Maybe.fromMaybe \\\"\\\" mcp.getToolDescription.description\\n\\n        mapToolDescriptionMcp2LLM :: [ParamProperty] -> OpenAI.Tool\\n        mapToolDescriptionMcp2LLM schema =\\n            OpenAI.Tool\\n                { OpenAI.toolName = llmName\\n                , OpenAI.toolDescription = llmDescription\\n                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = schema\\n                }\\n\\n        tool :: Tool ()\\n        tool = mcpTool box mcp\\n\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call = if matchName mcp call then Just (mapToolResult (const call) tool) else Nothing\\n     in\\n        case llmBasedSchema of\\n            Right schema ->\\n                Right $ ToolRegistration tool (mapToolDescriptionMcp2LLM schema) find\\n            Left err ->\\n                Left err\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- OpenAPI Tool Registration\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Register a single OpenAPI tool with the LLM system.\\n--\\n-- This function creates a 'ToolRegistration' from an OpenAPI tool and its\\n-- parent toolbox. The tool must have a valid operation ID.\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Left' if the tool cannot be registered (e.g., no operation ID).\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- case registerOpenAPITool toolbox apiTool of\\n--     Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Failed to register: \\\" ++ err\\n--     Right registration -> useWithAgent registration\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITool ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPITool toolbox tool =\\n    let opId = Maybe.fromMaybe (OpenAPI.toolName tool) (OpenAPIToolbox.getOperationId (OpenAPI.toolOperation tool))\\n        llmName = openapi2LLMName (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxName toolbox) opId\\n\\n        -- Convert to OpenAI Tool format\\n        openaiTool = toOpenAITool tool\\n\\n        -- Create the tool handler\\n        toolDef0 = IOTool $ IOScriptDescription\\n            { ioSlug = OpenAI.getToolName llmName\\n            , ioDescription = OpenAPI.toolDescription tool\\n            }\\n\\n        -- Create the run function\\n        runFunc :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> ToolExecutionContext -> Aeson.Value -> IO (CallResult ())\\n        runFunc tr ctx argz = createToolHandler toolbox tool tr ctx argz\\n\\n        -- Create the Tool\\n        tool' = Tool\\n            { toolDef = toolDef0\\n            , toolRun = runFunc\\n            }\\n\\n        -- Find function\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call =\\n            if call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName == llmName\\n                then Just $ mapToolResult (const call) tool'\\n                else Nothing\\n\\n        -- Declare function\\n        declare :: OpenAI.Tool\\n        declare = openaiTool{OpenAI.toolName = llmName}\\n     in Right $ ToolRegistration\\n            { innerTool = mapToolResult (const ()) tool'\\n            , declareTool = declare\\n            , findTool = find\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Register all tools from an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- This function iterates through all tools in the toolbox and attempts to\\n-- register each one. If any registration fails, the entire operation fails\\n-- fast with the first error encountered.\\n--\\n-- For partial success handling, use 'registerOpenAPITool' on individual tools.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- result <- OpenAPI.initializeToolbox tracer config\\n-- case result of\\n--     Left err -> print err\\n--     Right toolbox -> do\\n--         regResult <- registerOpenAPITools toolbox\\n--         case regResult of\\n--             Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Registration failed: \\\" ++ err\\n--             Right registrations -> do\\n--                 -- Use registrations with agent runtime\\n--                 mapM_ (addToAgent agent) registrations\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITools ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    IO (Either String [ToolRegistration])\\nregisterOpenAPITools toolbox =\\n    -- Fail fast - return first error encountered\\n    let registerAll :: [OpenAPI.OpenAPITool] -> Either String [ToolRegistration] -> Either String [ToolRegistration]\\n        registerAll [] acc = acc\\n        registerAll (t:ts) (Right regs) =\\n            case registerOpenAPITool toolbox t of\\n                Left err -> Left err\\n                Right reg -> registerAll ts (Right (reg:regs))\\n        registerAll _ err = err\\n        \\n        tools = OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxTools toolbox\\n     in pure $ case registerAll tools (Right []) of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right regs -> Right (reverse regs)\\n\\n-- | Register an OpenAPI tool in the LLM system (alias for 'registerOpenAPITool').\\n--\\n-- This is the original function name from the OpenAPIToolbox module,\\n-- provided for backward compatibility.\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM = registerOpenAPITool\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Internal tool builders (copied from Tools to avoid import cycle)\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on a Bash script-description.\\nbashTool ::\\n    ScriptDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nbashTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = BashTool script\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        ret <- BashTools.runValue (contramap BashToolsTrace tracer) script (Just ctx) v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ BashToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an MCP toolbox tool.\\nmcpTool ::\\n    McpTools.Toolbox ->\\n    McpTools.ToolDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nmcpTool toolbox desc =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = MCPTool desc\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run _tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do\\n        ret <- callTool toolbox desc (Just v)\\n        case ret of\\n            (Just (Right rsp)) -> pure $ extractContentsFromToolCall rsp\\n            err -> pure $ McpToolError call (mconcat [\\\"calling error: \\\", show err])\\n    run _tracer _ctx _ = do\\n        pure $ McpToolError call (\\\"can only call McpTools with Aeson.Object\\\")\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall :: McpClient.CallToolResultRsp -> CallResult ()\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall rsp =\\n        McpToolResult call rsp.getCallToolResult\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an IO-tool script-description.\\nioTool ::\\n    (Aeson.FromJSON llmArg) =>\\n    IOScript llmArg ByteString ->\\n    Tool ()\\nioTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = IOTool script.description\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        let adaptTrace = IOToolsTrace . IOTools.adaptTraceInput (const v)\\n        ret <- IOTools.runValue (contramap adaptTrace tracer) script ctx v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ IOToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Schema adaptation helpers\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nadaptSchema :: Mcp.InputSchema -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\nadaptSchema schema =\\n    case schema.properties of\\n        Nothing -> Right []\\n        Just obj -> ifoldl' f (Right []) obj\\n  where\\n    f :: Aeson.Key -> Either String [ParamProperty] -> Aeson.Value -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\n    f _ err@(Left _) _ = err\\n    f k (Right xs) v =\\n        case adaptProperty k v of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right x -> Right (x : xs)\\n\\nadaptProperty :: Aeson.Key -> Aeson.Value -> Either String ParamProperty\\nadaptProperty k val =\\n    case propMappingResult of\\n        Aeson.Success prop ->\\n            Right $\\n                ParamProperty\\n                    (AesonKey.toText k)\\n                    (OpaqueParamType prop._type)\\n                    prop._description\\n        Aeson.Error err -> Left err\\n  where\\n    propMappingResult :: Aeson.Result PropertyHelper\\n    propMappingResult = Aeson.fromJSON val\\n\\ndata PropertyHelper\\n    = PropertyHelper {_type :: Text, _description :: Text}\\n\\ninstance Aeson.FromJSON PropertyHelper where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"PropertyHelper\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        PropertyHelper <$> o Aeson..: \\\"type\\\" <*> o Aeson..: \\\"description\\\"\\n\\n\""

---

## Step 12: LLM Turn

### Thinking Process

<details>
<summary>Click to expand reasoning</summary>

Now I understand the issue. The problem is that OpenAPI operation IDs might contain characters that are invalid for LLM function names (like dots, slashes, or other special characters). The error message says "function name is invalid, must start with a letter and can contain letters, numbers, underscores, and dashes".

Let me look at the `openapi2LLMName` function and understand the mapping better:

From the code I've read:
1. `OpenAPIToolbox.openapi2LLMName` creates a name like `openapi_<tboxName>_<operationId>`
2. This is used both when declaring tools to the LLM and when matching incoming tool calls
3. The issue is that OpenAPI operation IDs can contain invalid characters

The fix needs:
1. A normalization function that converts operation IDs to valid LLM function names
2. A bidirectional mapping system so we can map back from LLM names to original operation IDs

Let me also check the existing tests:
</details>

### Response



### Tool Calls

- **bash_read-file**
