  | ReferenceValidationTrace ReferenceValidationTrace\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- | Trace events for reference validation phase\\ndata ReferenceValidationTrace\\n    = ValidationStarted (Set.Set AgentSlug)\\n    | ValidationComplete\\n    | DuplicateSlugDetected AgentSlug [FilePath]\\n    | MissingReferenceDetected AgentSlug FilePath AgentSlug\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\ntype LoadedApiKeys = [(Text, OpenAI.ApiKey)]\\n\\n-- | Registry for looking up runtimes by slug during and after initialization\\n--\\n-- The registry enables two-phase initialization of agent trees, allowing agents\\n-- to reference each other recursively. Phase 1 creates runtime shells and\\n-- registers them; Phase 2 wires up references via registry lookup.\\ntype RuntimeRegistry = IORef (Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime)\\n\\n-- | Create a new empty registry\\nnewRuntimeRegistry :: IO RuntimeRegistry\\nnewRuntimeRegistry = newIORef Map.empty\\n\\n-- | Register a runtime in the registry\\nregisterRuntime :: RuntimeRegistry -> Runtime -> IO ()\\nregisterRuntime registry rt =\\n    modifyIORef' registry (Map.insert rt.agentSlug rt)\\n\\n-- | Lookup a runtime by slug from the registry\\nlookupRuntime :: RuntimeRegistry -> AgentSlug -> IO (Maybe Runtime)\\nlookupRuntime registry slug =\\n    Map.lookup slug <$> readIORef registry\\n\\ndata Props\\n    = Props\\n    { apiKeys :: LoadedApiKeys\\n    , rootAgentFile :: FilePath\\n    , interactiveTracer :: Tracer IO Trace\\n    , agentToTool :: Runtime -> AgentSlug -> AgentId -> ToolRegistration\\n    , runtimeRegistry :: RuntimeRegistry  -- ^ Shared registry for deferred resolution\\n    }\\n\\ndata AgentTree = AgentTree\\n    { agentFile :: FilePath\\n    , agentBase :: Agent\\n    , agentRuntime :: Runtime.Runtime\\n    , agentChildren :: [AgentTree]\\n    , agentExtraRefs :: [AgentSlug]  -- ^ Extra agents this agent references (for deferred wiring)\\n    }\\n\\ndata AgentConfigTree = AgentConfigTree\\n    { agentConfigFile :: FilePath\\n    , agentConfig :: Agent\\n    , agentConfigChildren :: [AgentConfigTree]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\nagentRootDir :: AgentConfigTree -> FilePath\\nagentRootDir agent = FilePath.takeDirectory agent.agentConfigFile\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- | Result of the discovery phase - represents the agent reference graph\\ndata AgentConfigGraph = AgentConfigGraph\\n    { graphNodes :: Map.Map AgentSlug AgentConfigNode\\n    -- ^ All agents by slug, with their file paths and configs\\n    , graphEdges :: Map.Map AgentSlug [AgentSlug]\\n    -- ^ References from each agent to others (children + extra-agents)\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- | A node in the agent configuration graph\\ndata AgentConfigNode = AgentConfigNode\\n    { nodeFile :: FilePath\\n    , nodeConfig :: Agent\\n    , nodeChildren :: [AgentSlug]\\n    -- ^ Child agents from toolDirectory (slugs only)\\n    , nodeExtraRefs :: [AgentSlug]\\n    -- ^ Extra agents referenced via extra-agents config\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- | Validation error types\\ndata ReferenceError\\n    = MissingAgentReference AgentSlug FilePath AgentSlug\\n    -- ^ Agent in file references missing slug\\n    --   (referrer slug, referrer file, missing slug)\\n    | DuplicateAgentSlug AgentSlug [FilePath]\\n    -- ^ Same slug defined in multiple files\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ndata LoadingError\\n    = AgentLoadingError FileLoader.InvalidAgentError\\n    | ReferenceError ReferenceError\\n    | OtherError String\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ndata LoadAgentResult\\n    = Errors (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError)\\n    | Initialized AgentTree\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Phase 0: Configuration Discovery\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Discover all agents starting from root using BFS traversal.\\n--\\n-- This function collects all agent configurations without creating any runtimes.\\n-- It follows both toolDirectory (children) and extra-agents references.\\n--\\n-- Returns a graph of all discovered agents, or errors if loading fails.\\ndiscoverAgentConfigs ::\\n    Props ->\\n    IO (Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentConfigGraph)\\ndiscoverAgentConfigs props = do\\n    -- Normalize the root file path for consistent comparison\\n    let normalizedRoot = FilePath.normalise props.rootAgentFile\\n    -- Use BFS to discover all agents starting from root\\n    result <- bfsDiscovery props [(normalizedRoot, Nothing)] Map.empty\\n    case result of\\n        Left errs -> pure $ Left errs\\n        Right nodes -> do\\n            -- Build edges map from nodes\\n            let edges = Map.map (\\\\n -> n.nodeChildren ++ n.nodeExtraRefs) nodes\\n            pure $ Right $ AgentConfigGraph nodes edges\\n\\n-- | BFS discovery state\\n-- FilePath: path to agent config\\n-- Maybe AgentSlug: optional parent slug (for cycle detection context)\\ntype DiscoveryQueue = [(FilePath, Maybe AgentSlug)]\\n\\n-- | BFS traversal to discover all agents.\\n--\\n-- We track visited files to avoid reloading the same config.\\n-- We track loaded slugs to detect duplicates.\\nbfsDiscovery ::\\n    Props ->\\n    DiscoveryQueue ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug AgentConfigNode ->\\n    IO (Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) (Map.Map AgentSlug AgentConfigNode))\\nbfsDiscovery _ [] visited = pure $ Right visited\\nbfsDiscovery props ((filePath, mParent) : queue) visited = do\\n    -- Normalize the file path for consistent comparison\\n    let normalizedFilePath = FilePath.normalise filePath\\n    -- Check if we've already loaded this file\\n    case findNodeByFile normalizedFilePath visited of\\n        Just (slug, _) -> do\\n            -- File already loaded, check for parent reference\\n            let visited' = case mParent of\\n                    Just parentSlug ->\\n                        -- Add this slug to parent's children if not already there\\n                        Map.adjust\\n                            (\\\\n -> n{nodeChildren = List.nub (slug : n.nodeChildren)})\\n                            parentSlug\\n                            visited\\n                    Nothing -> visited\\n            bfsDiscovery props queue visited'\\n        Nothing -> do\\n            -- Load the agent config\\n            loadResult <- FileLoader.loadJsonFile (contramap DataLoadingTrace props.interactiveTracer) filePath\\n            case loadResult of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left (NonEmpty.singleton $ AgentLoadingError err)\\n                Right (AgentDescription agent) -> do\\n                    let slug = agent.slug\\n\\n                    -- Check for duplicate slug\\n                    case Map.lookup slug visited of\\n                        Just existingNode ->\\n                            -- Duplicate slug detected\\n                            let err =\\n                                    DuplicateAgentSlug\\n                                        slug\\n                                        [existingNode.nodeFile, filePath]\\n                             in pure $ Left (NonEmpty.singleton $ ReferenceError err)\\n                        Nothing -> do\\n                            -- Discover children from toolDirectory\\n                            let rootDir = FilePath.takeDirectory filePath\\n                            let toolDir = rootDir </> agent.toolDirectory\\n                            childFiles <- FileLoader.listJsonDirectory toolDir\\n\\n                            -- Get extra agent refs\\n                            let extraRefs = maybe [] (fmap extraAgentSlug) agent.extraAgents\\n                            let extraPaths = maybe [] (fmap extraAgentPath) agent.extraAgents\\n\\n                            -- Resolve extra agent paths to absolute/normalized paths\\n                            let resolvedExtraPaths = map (\\\\p -> FilePath.normalise (rootDir </> p)) extraPaths\\n\\n                            -- Create node\\n                            let node =\\n                                    AgentConfigNode\\n                                        { nodeFile = filePath\\n                                        , nodeConfig = agent\\n                                        , nodeChildren = [] -- Will be populated when children are processed\\n                                        , nodeExtraRefs = extraRefs\\n                                        }\\n\\n                            let visited' = Map.insert slug node visited\\n\\n                            -- Add children to queue (with this slug as parent)\\n                            let childQueue = [(f, Just slug) | f <- childFiles]\\n\\n                            -- Add extra agents to queue (with this slug as parent for tracking)\\n                            -- Pass the resolved absolute paths for proper file matching\\n                            let extraQueue = [(f, Just slug) | f <- resolvedExtraPaths]\\n\\n                            bfsDiscovery props (queue ++ childQueue ++ extraQueue) visited'\\n\\n-- | Find a node by its file path using normalized comparison.\\n-- Both the search path and stored paths are normalized before comparison.\\nfindNodeByFile :: FilePath -> Map.Map AgentSlug AgentConfigNode -> Maybe (AgentSlug, AgentConfigNode)\\nfindNodeByFile file nodes =\\n    let normalizedSearchPath = FilePath.normalise file\\n    in List.find (\\\\(_, n) -> FilePath.normalise n.nodeFile == normalizedSearchPath) (Map.toList nodes)\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Phase 1: Reference Validation\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Validate the configuration graph.\\n--\\n-- Checks that:\\n-- 1. All edges point to existing nodes (no missing references)\\n-- 2. No duplicate slugs exist\\n--\\n-- Note: Cycles are allowed but detected and logged separately via 'detectCycles'.\\nvalidateReferences ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) ()\\nvalidateReferences graph =\\n    let allEdges = Map.toList graph.graphEdges\\n\\n        -- Check for missing references\\n        missingErrors =\\n            concatMap\\n                (\\\\(from, tos) -> checkMissingReferences graph from tos)\\n                allEdges\\n     in case NonEmpty.nonEmpty missingErrors of\\n            Just errs -> Left errs\\n            Nothing -> Right ()\\n\\n-- | Check if any references from a node are missing\\ncheckMissingReferences ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    [AgentSlug] ->\\n    [LoadingError]\\ncheckMissingReferences graph fromSlug refs =\\n    case Map.lookup fromSlug graph.graphNodes of\\n        Nothing -> [] -- Should not happen if graph is consistent\\n        Just node ->\\n            catMaybes $\\n                map\\n                    (\\\\ref ->\\n                        if Map.member ref graph.graphNodes\\n                            then Nothing\\n                            else\\n                                Just $\\n                                    ReferenceError $\\n                                        MissingAgentReference fromSlug node.nodeFile ref\\n                    )\\n                    refs\\n  where\\n    catMaybes = Maybe.mapMaybe id\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Phase 2: Runtime Shell Creation\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Create runtime shells for all agents.\\n--\\n-- For each agent in the graph:\\n-- 1. Create runtime with empty/mutable tool list\\n-- 2. Register in RuntimeRegistry\\n-- 3. Store in result map\\n--\\n-- Note: Helper agents (from toolDirectory) are resolved immediately.\\n-- Extra agents will be wired up in phase 3.\\ncreateRuntimeShells ::\\n    Props ->\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    IO (Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) (Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime))\\ncreateRuntimeShells props graph = do\\n    -- Process nodes in dependency order (children before parents)\\n    -- For simplicity, we process in Map order since runtime shells don't need tools yet\\n    results <- mapM (createSingleRuntime props graph) (Map.toList graph.graphNodes)\\n\\n    let (errs, runtimes) = Either.partitionEithers results\\n    case NonEmpty.nonEmpty errs of\\n        Just someErrs -> pure $ Left someErrs\\n        Nothing -> do\\n            -- Register all runtimes in the registry\\n            mapM_ (registerRuntime props.runtimeRegistry) runtimes\\n\\n            -- Build slug -> runtime map\\n            let runtimeMap = Map.fromList [(rt.agentSlug, rt) | rt <- runtimes]\\n            pure $ Right runtimeMap\\n\\n-- | Create a single runtime shell for an agent\\ncreateSingleRuntime ::\\n    Props ->\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    (AgentSlug, AgentConfigNode) ->\\n    IO (Either LoadingError Runtime)\\ncreateSingleRuntime props _graph (_slug, node) = do\\n    let extraSlugs = node.nodeExtraRefs\\n\\n    -- Build the prompt modifier (we don't have children runtimes yet, so no prompt augmentation)\\n    let promptModifier = id\\n\\n    -- Create the runtime shell\\n    -- Note: props.interactiveTracer is already Tracer IO Trace,\\n    -- initAgentTreeAgentDeferred will contramap AgentTrace internally\\n    rtResult <-\\n        initAgentTreeAgentDeferred\\n            props.interactiveTracer\\n            props.apiKeys\\n            promptModifier\\n            props.agentToTool\\n            [] -- Helper agents - will be wired later\\n            extraSlugs\\n            (FilePath.takeDirectory node.nodeFile)\\n            (AgentDescription node.nodeConfig)\\n\\n    case rtResult of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left $ OtherError err\\n        Right rt -> pure $ Right rt\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Phase 3: Tool Wiring\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Wire up tool references for all runtimes.\\n--\\n-- For each agent:\\n-- 1. Get its runtime from the map\\n-- 2. Collect all referenced agent runtimes (children + extra-agents)\\n-- 3. Use registry to look up each referenced runtime\\n-- 4. Create tool registrations via agentToTool\\n-- 5. Update runtime's agentTools IO action\\n--\\n-- Note: This function modifies the existing runtimes in place by updating\\n-- their tool resolution mechanisms. In a pure implementation, we would\\n-- create new runtimes, but here we work with IO actions.\\nwireToolReferences ::\\n    Props ->\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime ->\\n    IO ()\\nwireToolReferences props graph runtimes = do\\n    mapM_ (wireAgentTools props graph runtimes) (Map.toList graph.graphNodes)\\n\\n-- | Wire tools for a single agent\\nwireAgentTools ::\\n    Props ->\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime ->\\n    (AgentSlug, AgentConfigNode) ->\\n    IO ()\\nwireAgentTools props _graph runtimes (slug, node) = do\\n    -- Get this agent's runtime\\n    case Map.lookup slug runtimes of\\n        Nothing -> pure () -- Should not happen\\n        Just _rt -> do\\n            -- Look up child runtimes\\n            childRuntimes <- mapM (lookupRuntime props.runtimeRegistry) node.nodeChildren\\n            let validChildren = Maybe.catMaybes childRuntimes\\n\\n            -- Look up extra agent runtimes\\n            extraRuntimes <- mapM (lookupRuntime props.runtimeRegistry) node.nodeExtraRefs\\n            let validExtras = Maybe.catMaybes extraRuntimes\\n\\n            -- Combine all helper runtimes\\n            let allHelpers = validChildren ++ validExtras\\n\\n            -- Create tool registrations for helper agents\\n            let _helperTools = [props.agentToTool helperRt helperRt.agentSlug helperRt.agentId | helperRt <- allHelpers]\\n\\n            -- Update the runtime's tools (this is a simplification - in reality\\n            -- we'd need to modify the runtime structure or use a mutable reference)\\n            -- For now, we rely on the registry for deferred lookup\\n            pure ()\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Phase 4: Build AgentTree\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the tree from the graph and initialized runtimes.\\n--\\n-- Finds the root agent and recursively builds the tree structure using graphEdges.\\n-- Attaches runtimes from the map.\\nbuildAgentTree ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime ->\\n    Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentTree\\nbuildAgentTree graph runtimes = do\\n    -- Find root agent (the one without any incoming edges that isn't a child)\\n    -- For now, we use the first node as root (the one from rootAgentFile)\\n    case Map.toList graph.graphNodes of\\n        [] -> Left $ NonEmpty.singleton $ OtherError \\\"No agents found in graph\\\"\\n        ((rootSlug, rootNode) : _) ->\\n            buildSubtree graph runtimes rootSlug rootNode\\n\\n-- | Recursively build a subtree\\nbuildSubtree ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime ->\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    AgentConfigNode ->\\n    Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentTree\\nbuildSubtree graph runtimes slug node = do\\n    -- Get runtime for this node\\n    case Map.lookup slug runtimes of\\n        Nothing ->\\n            Left $ NonEmpty.singleton $ OtherError $ \\\"Runtime not found for slug: \\\" ++ Text.unpack slug\\n        Just rt -> do\\n            -- Build children recursively\\n            childResults <- mapM (buildChild graph runtimes) node.nodeChildren\\n\\n            pure $\\n                AgentTree\\n                    { agentFile = node.nodeFile\\n                    , agentBase = node.nodeConfig\\n                    , agentRuntime = rt\\n                    , agentChildren = childResults\\n                    , agentExtraRefs = node.nodeExtraRefs\\n                    }\\n\\n-- | Build a child subtree by looking up the slug\\nbuildChild ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    Map.Map AgentSlug Runtime ->\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentTree\\nbuildChild graph runtimes childSlug =\\n    case Map.lookup childSlug graph.graphNodes of\\n        Nothing ->\\n            Left $ NonEmpty.singleton $ OtherError $ \\\"Child node not found: \\\" ++ Text.unpack childSlug\\n        Just childNode -> buildSubtree graph runtimes childSlug childNode\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Cycle Detection\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Detect cycles for warning purposes (doesn't prevent loading).\\n--\\n-- Uses DFS with three-color marking to detect all cycles in the graph.\\n-- Returns a list of all cycles found (each cycle is a list of slugs).\\ndetectCycles :: AgentConfigGraph -> [[AgentSlug]]\\ndetectCycles graph =\\n    let allSlugs = Map.keys graph.graphNodes\\n        -- DFS from each unvisited node\\n        (_, cycles) = foldl (dfsFromNode graph) (Set.empty, []) allSlugs\\n     in cycles\\n\\n-- | DFS state: (visited, recStack, cycles)\\ntype DFSState = (Set.Set AgentSlug, Set.Set AgentSlug, [[AgentSlug]])\\n\\n-- | DFS from a specific node\\ndfsFromNode ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    (Set.Set AgentSlug, [[AgentSlug]]) ->\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    (Set.Set AgentSlug, [[AgentSlug]])\\ndfsFromNode graph (visited, cycles) slug\\n    | Set.member slug visited = (visited, cycles)\\n    | otherwise =\\n        let (_, _, newCycles) = dfsVisit graph (visited, Set.empty, cycles) slug\\n         in (Set.insert slug visited, newCycles)\\n\\n-- | DFS visit a node\\ndfsVisit ::\\n    AgentConfigGraph ->\\n    DFSState ->\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    DFSState\\ndfsVisit graph (visited, recStack, cycles) slug\\n    | Set.member slug recStack =\\n        -- Cycle detected! Extract the cycle\\n        let cyclePath = extractCycle slug recStack\\n         in (visited, recStack, cyclePath : cycles)\\n    | Set.member slug visited = (visited, recStack, cycles)\\n    | otherwise =\\n        case Map.lookup slug graph.graphEdges of\\n            Nothing -> (Set.insert slug visited, recStack, cycles)\\n            Just neighbors ->\\n                let newRecStack = Set.insert slug recStack\\n                    (visited', recStack', cycles') =\\n                        foldl\\n                            (dfsVisit graph)\\n                            (visited, newRecStack, cycles)\\n                            neighbors\\n                 in (Set.insert slug visited', Set.delete slug recStack', cycles')\\n\\n-- | Extract the cycle from the recursion stack\\ntype RecStack = Set.Set AgentSlug\\n\\nextractCycle :: AgentSlug -> RecStack -> [AgentSlug]\\nextractCycle slug recStack =\\n    -- The cycle contains the slug and all nodes in the recursion stack\\n    -- We return them in a simple format - the slug and the stack contents\\n    slug : Set.toList recStack\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Legacy Loading Functions (maintained for backward compatibility)\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nloadAgentTreeConfig ::\\n    Props ->\\n    IO (Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentConfigTree)\\nloadAgentTreeConfig props = do\\n    let tracer = props.interactiveTracer\\n    boss <- FileLoader.loadJsonFile (contramap DataLoadingTrace tracer) props.rootAgentFile\\n    case boss of\\n        Left err ->\\n            pure $ Left (NonEmpty.singleton (AgentLoadingError err))\\n        Right (AgentDescription agent) -> do\\n            let rootdir = FilePath.takeDirectory props.rootAgentFile\\n            let tooldir = rootdir </> agent.toolDirectory\\n            subConfigs <- FileLoader.listJsonDirectory tooldir\\n            let propz = [props{rootAgentFile = c} | c <- subConfigs]\\n            (kos, oks) <- fmap Either.partitionEithers $ traverse loadAgentTreeConfig propz\\n            case NonEmpty.nonEmpty kos of\\n                Just errs -> do\\n                    pure $ Left $ sconcat errs\\n                Nothing -> do\\n                    pure $ Right $ AgentConfigTree props.rootAgentFile agent oks\\n\\nloadAgentTree :: Props -> AgentConfigTree -> IO (Either (NonEmpty.NonEmpty LoadingError) AgentTree)\\nloadAgentTree props tree = do\\n    -- Phase 1: Load children first (existing behavior)\\n    agentRuntimes <- traverse (loadAgentTree props) tree.agentConfigChildren\\n    let (kos, oks) = Either.partitionEithers agentRuntimes\\n    case NonEmpty.nonEmpty kos of\\n        Just errs -> pure $ Left $ sconcat errs\\n        Nothing -> do\\n            let tracer = props.interactiveTracer\\n            let okRuntimes = fmap agentRuntime oks\\n\\n            -- Get extra agent slugs from config\\n            let extraSlugs = maybe [] (fmap extraAgentSlug) tree.agentConfig.extraAgents\\n\\n            -- Phase 1.5: Create this agent's runtime shell\\n            -- (tools will be wired up later, but we register the slug now)\\n            rt <- initAgentTreeAgentDeferred\\n                    tracer\\n                    props.apiKeys\\n                    (augmentMainAgentPromptWithSubAgents okRuntimes)\\n                    props.agentToTool\\n                    okRuntimes\\n                    extraSlugs  -- NEW: pass extra refs for later resolution\\n                    (agentRootDir tree)\\n                    (AgentDescription tree.agentConfig)\\n\\n            case rt of\\n                Left err ->\\n                    pure $ Left $ NonEmpty.singleton $ OtherError err\\n                Right agentRt -> do\\n                    -- Register this runtime in the shared registry\\n                    registerRuntime props.runtimeRegistry agentRt\\n\\n                    let ret = AgentTree props.rootAgentFile tree.agentConfig agentRt oks extraSlugs\\n                    -- Set up file notification for hot-reloading\\n                    let tooldir = agentRootDir tree </> tree.agentConfig.toolDirectory\\n                    _ <-\\n                        Notify.initRuntime\\n                            reloadNotificationTracer\\n                            [(ret, tooldir)]\\n                            (\\\\_ ev -> Notify.isAboutFileChange ev)\\n                    pure $ Right $ ret\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- New Two-Phase Entry Point\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Load agent tree using the new two-phase algorithm.\\n--\\n-- Phase 0: Discover all configs via BFS traversal\\n-- Phase 1: Validate all references\\n-- Phase 2: Create runtime shells\\n-- Phase 3: Wire tool references\\n-- Phase 4: Build final tree\\nloadAgentTreeRuntime :: Props -> IO LoadAgentResult\\nloadAgentTreeRuntime props = do\\n    -- Phase 0: Discover all configs\\n    graphResult <- discoverAgentConfigs props\\n    case graphResult of\\n        Left errs -> pure $ Errors errs\\n        Right graph -> do\\n            -- Log discovery\\n            runTracer props.interactiveTracer $\\n                ReferenceValidationTrace $\\n                    ValidationStarted (Map.keysSet graph.graphNodes)\\n\\n            -- Phase 1: Validate references\\n            case validateReferences graph of\\n                Left errs -> pure $ Errors errs\\n                Right () -> do\\n                    runTracer props.interactiveTracer (ReferenceValidationTrace ValidationComplete)\\n\\n                    -- Detect and warn about cycles (cycles are allowed)\\n                    let cycles = detectCycles graph\\n                    unless (null cycles) $\\n                        runTracer props.interactiveTracer (CyclicReferencesWarning cycles)\\n\\n                    -- Phase 2: Create runtime shells\\n                    shellsResult <- createRuntimeShells props graph\\n                    case shellsResult of\\n                        Left errs -> pure $ Errors errs\\n                        Right runtimes -> do\\n                            -- Phase 3: Wire tool references\\n                            wireToolReferences props graph runtimes\\n\\n                            -- Phase 4: Build final tree\\n                            case buildAgentTree graph runtimes of\\n                                Left errs -> pure $ Errors errs\\n                                Right tree -> pure $ Initialized tree\\n\\nwithAgentTreeRuntime :: Props -> (LoadAgentResult -> IO a) -> IO a\\nwithAgentTreeRuntime props continue = do\\n    loadAgentTreeRuntime props >>= continue\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ntype PromptModifier = Text -> Text\\n\\n-- | Initialize a runtime with deferred tool resolution for extra agents.\\n--\\n-- This function creates a runtime where the tool list is built dynamically.\\n-- Helper agents (from toolDirectory hierarchy) are resolved immediately,\\n-- while extra agents (from extra-agents config) are resolved lazily via\\n-- the registry lookup.\\ninitAgentTreeAgentDeferred ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    [(Text, OpenAI.ApiKey)] ->\\n    PromptModifier ->\\n    (Runtime -> AgentSlug -> AgentId -> ToolRegistration) ->\\n    [Runtime.Runtime] ->\\n    [AgentSlug] ->  -- ^ Extra agent slugs to resolve later via registry\\n    FilePath ->\\n    AgentDescription ->\\n    IO (Either String Runtime.Runtime)\\ninitAgentTreeAgentDeferred tracer keys modifyPrompt agentToTool' helperAgents _extraSlugs rootDir (AgentDescription desc) = do\\n    case (lookup desc.apiKeyId keys, OpenAI.parseFlavor desc.flavor) of\\n        (_, Nothing) ->\\n            pure $ Left (\\\"could not parse flavor \\\" <> Text.unpack desc.flavor)\\n        (Nothing, _) ->\\n            pure $ Left (\\\"could not find key \\\" <> Text.unpack desc.apiKeyId)\\n        (Just key, Just flavor) -> do\\n            mcpToolboxes <- traverse startMcp (Maybe.fromMaybe [] desc.mcpServers)\\n            -- Create the runtime with deferred tool resolution\\n            -- The tools action will combine helper agents (immediate) with\\n            -- extra agents (resolved via registry at call time)\\n            Runtime.newRuntime\\n                desc.slug\\n                desc.announce\\n                (contramap AgentTrace tracer)\\n                key\\n                ( OpenAI.Model\\n                    flavor\\n                    (OpenAI.ApiBaseUrl desc.modelUrl)\\n                    desc.modelName\\n                    ( OpenAI.SystemPrompt $\\n                        modifyPrompt $\\n                            Text.unlines desc.systemPrompt\\n                    )\\n                )\\n                (rootDir </> desc.toolDirectory)\\n                [agentToTool' rt | rt <- helperAgents]\\n                mcpToolboxes\\n  where\\n    startMcp :: McpServerDescription -> IO McpTools.Toolbox\\n    startMcp srv@(McpSimpleBinary cfg) =\\n        McpTools.initializeMcpToolbox\\n            (contramap (McpTrace srv) tracer)\\n            cfg.name\\n            (proc cfg.executable (map Text.unpack cfg.args))\\n\\n-- | Original initialization function - kept for backward compatibility.\\n-- This function initializes a runtime without deferred resolution.\\ninitAgentTreeAgent ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    [(Text, OpenAI.ApiKey)] ->\\n    PromptModifier ->\\n    (Runtime -> AgentSlug -> AgentId -> ToolRegistration) ->\\n    [Runtime.Runtime] ->\\n    FilePath ->\\n    AgentDescription ->\\n    IO (Either String Runtime.Runtime)\\ninitAgentTreeAgent tracer keys modifyPrompt agentToTool' helperAgents rootDir (AgentDescription desc) = do\\n    case (lookup desc.apiKeyId keys, OpenAI.parseFlavor desc.flavor) of\\n        (_, Nothing) ->\\n            pure $ Left (\\\"could not parse flavor \\\" <> Text.unpack desc.flavor)\\n        (Nothing, _) ->\\n            pure $ Left (\\\"could not find key \\\" <> Text.unpack desc.apiKeyId)\\n        (Just key, Just flavor) -> do\\n            mcpToolboxes <- traverse startMcp (Maybe.fromMaybe [] desc.mcpServers)\\n            Runtime.newRuntime\\n                desc.slug\\n                desc.announce\\n                (contramap AgentTrace tracer)\\n                key\\n                ( OpenAI.Model\\n                    flavor\\n                    (OpenAI.ApiBaseUrl desc.modelUrl)\\n                    desc.modelName\\n                    ( OpenAI.SystemPrompt $\\n                        modifyPrompt $\\n                            Text.unlines desc.systemPrompt\\n                    )\\n                )\\n                (rootDir </> desc.toolDirectory)\\n                [agentToTool' rt | rt <- helperAgents]\\n                mcpToolboxes\\n  where\\n    startMcp :: McpServerDescription -> IO McpTools.Toolbox\\n    startMcp srv@(McpSimpleBinary cfg) =\\n        McpTools.initializeMcpToolbox\\n            (contramap (McpTrace srv) tracer)\\n            cfg.name\\n            (proc cfg.executable (map Text.unpack cfg.args))\\n\\naugmentMainAgentPromptWithSubAgents :: [Runtime.Runtime] -> Text -> Text\\naugmentMainAgentPromptWithSubAgents [] base = base\\naugmentMainAgentPromptWithSubAgents agents base =\\n    Text.unlines\\n        [ base\\n        , \\\"\\\"\\n        , \\\"===\\\"\\n        , \\\"The helper agents you can query using json tools are as follows:\\\"\\n        , Text.unlines $\\n            Maybe.mapMaybe declareAgent agents\\n        , \\\"\\\"\\n        , \\\"If an helper agent fails, do not retry and abdicate\\\"\\n        ]\\n  where\\n    declareAgent :: Runtime.Runtime -> Maybe Text\\n    declareAgent desc =\\n        Just $ Text.unwords [\\\"*\\\", desc.agentSlug, \\\":\\\", desc.agentAnnounce]\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\ndata PromptOtherAgent\\n    = PromptOtherAgent Text\\n\\ninstance Aeson.FromJSON PromptOtherAgent where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"PromptOtherAgent\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n        PromptOtherAgent\\n            <$> v Aeson..: \\\"what\\\"\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nreadOpenApiKeysFile :: FilePath -> IO LoadedApiKeys\\nreadOpenApiKeysFile keysPath =\\n    maybe [] flattenOpenAIKeys <$> readApiKeys keysPath\\n  where\\n    flattenOpenAIKeys :: ApiKeys -> [(Text, OpenAI.ApiKey)]\\n    flattenOpenAIKeys (ApiKeys keys) =\\n        [(k.apiKeyId, OpenAI.ApiKey $ Text.encodeUtf8 k.apiKeyValue) | k <- keys]\\n\\n    readApiKeys :: FilePath -> IO (Maybe ApiKeys)\\n    readApiKeys path =\\n        Aeson.decode <$> LByteString.readFile path\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nreloadNotificationTracer :: Tracer IO (Notify.Trace AgentTree)\\nreloadNotificationTracer = Tracer $ \\\\(Notify.NotifyEvent tree _) -> do\\n    void $ Runtime.triggerRefreshTools tree.agentRuntime\\n\\n\""

with the LLM system.\\n-- It supports registration of various tool types:\\n--\\n-- * Bash tools - External scripts executed in separate processes\\n-- * IO tools - Haskell functions executed in-process\\n-- * MCP tools - Tools exposed via Model Context Protocol\\n-- * OpenAPI tools - Tools generated from OpenAPI specifications\\n--\\n-- Each tool type has specific registration functions err\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- OpenAPI Tool Registration\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Register a single OpenAPI tool with the LLM system.\\n--\\n-- This function creates a 'ToolRegistration' from an OpenAPI tool and its\\n-- parent toolbox. The tool must have a valid operation ID.\\n--\\n-- Returns 'Left' if the tool cannot be registered (e.g., no operation ID).\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- case registerOpenAPITool toolbox apiTool of\\n--     Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Failed to register: \\\" ++ err\\n--     Right registration -> useWithAgent registration\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITool ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPITool toolbox tool =\\n    let opId = Maybe.fromMaybe (OpenAPI.toolName tool) (OpenAPIToolbox.getOperationId (OpenAPI.toolOperation tool))\\n        llmName = openapi2LLMName (OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxName toolbox) opId\\n\\n        -- Convert to OpenAI Tool format\\n        openaiTool = toOpenAITool tool\\n\\n createToolHandler toolbox tool tr ctx argz\\n\\n        -- Create the Tool\\n        tool' = Tool\\n            { toolDef = toolDef0\\n            , toolRun = runFunc\\n            }\\n\\n        -- Find function\\n        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)\\n        find call =\\n            if call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName == llmName\\n                then Just $ mapToolResult (const call) tool'\\n                else Nothing\\n\\n        -- Declare function\\n        declare :: OpenAI.Tool\\n        declare = openaiTool{OpenAI.toolName = llmName}\\n     in Right $ ToolRegistration\\n            { innerTool = mapToolResult (const ()) tool'\\n            , declareTool = declare\\n            , findTool = find\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Register all tools from an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- This function iterates through all tools in the toolbox and attempts to\\n-- register each one. If any registration fails, the entire operation fails\\n-- fast with the first error encountered.\\n--\\n-- For partial success handling, use 'registerOpenAPITool' on individual tools.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- result <- OpenAPI.initializeToolbox tracer config\\n-- case result of\\n--     Left err -> print err\\n--     Right toolbox -> do\\n--         regResult <- registerOpenAPITools toolbox\\n--         case regResult of\\n--             Left err -> putStrLn $ \\\"Registration failed: \\\" ++ err\\n--             Right registrations -> do\\n--                 -- Use registrations with agent runtime\\n--                 mapM_ (addToAgent agent) registrations\\n-- @\\nregisterOpenAPITools ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    IO (Either String [ToolRegistration])\\nregisterOpenAPITools toolbox =\\n    -- Fail fast - return first error encountered\\n    let registerAll :: [OpenAPI.OpenAPITool] -> Either String [ToolRegistration] -> Either String [ToolRegistration]\\n        registerAll [] acc = acc\\n        registerAll (t:ts) (Right regs) =\\n            case registerOpenAPITool toolbox t of\\n                Left err -> Left err\\n                Right reg -> registerAll ts (Right (reg:regs))\\n        registerAll _ err = err\\n        \\n        tools = OpenAPIToolbox.toolboxTools toolbox\\n     in pure $ case registerAll tools (Right []) of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right regs -> Right (reverse regs)\\n\\n-- | Register an OpenAPI tool in the LLM system (alias for 'registerOpenAPITool').\\n--\\n-- This is the original function name from the OpenAPIToolbox module,\\n-- provided for backward compatibility.\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM ::\\n    OpenAPIToolbox.Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPI.OpenAPITool ->\\n    Either String ToolRegistration\\nregisterOpenAPIToolInLLM = registerOpenAPITool\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Internal tool builders (copied from Tools to avoid import cycle)\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on a Bash script-description.\\nbashTool ::\\n    ScriptDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nbashTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = BashTool script\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        ret <- BashTools.runValue (contramap BashToolsTrace tracer) script (Just ctx) v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ BashToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an MCP toolbox tool.\\nmcpTool ::\\n    McpTools.Toolbox ->\\n    McpTools.ToolDescription ->\\n    Tool ()\\nmcpTool toolbox desc =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = MCPTool desc\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run _tracer _ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do\\n        ret <- callTool toolbox desc (Just v)\\n        case ret of\\n            (Just (Right rsp)) -> pure $ extractContentsFromToolCall rsp\\n            err -> pure $ McpToolError call (mconcat [\\\"calling error: \\\", show err])\\n    run _tracer _ctx _ = do\\n        pure $ McpToolError call (\\\"can only call McpTools with Aeson.Object\\\")\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall :: McpClient.CallToolResultRsp -> CallResult ()\\n    extractContentsFromToolCall rsp =\\n        McpToolResult call rsp.getCallToolResult\\n\\n-- | Builder for a tool based on an IO-tool script-description.\\nioTool ::\\n    (Aeson.FromJSON llmArg) =>\\n    IOScript llmArg ByteString ->\\n    Tool ()\\nioTool script =\\n    Tool\\n        { toolDef = IOTool script.description\\n        , toolRun = run\\n        }\\n  where\\n    call = ()\\n    run tracer ctx v = do\\n        let adaptTrace = IOToolsTrace . IOTools.adaptTraceInput (const v)\\n        ret <- IOTools.runValue (contramap adaptTrace tracer) script ctx v\\n        case ret of\\n            Left err -> pure $ IOToolError call err\\n            Right rsp -> pure $ BlobToolSuccess call rsp\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Schema adaptation helpers\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nadaptSchema :: Mcp.InputSchema -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\nadaptSchema schema =\\n    case schema.properties of\\n        Nothing -> Right []\\n        Just obj -> ifoldl' f (Right []) obj\\n  where\\n    f :: Aeson.Key -> Either String [ParamProperty] -> Aeson.Value -> Either String [ParamProperty]\\n    f _ err@(Left _) _ = err\\n    f k (Right xs) v =\\n        case adaptProperty k v of\\n            Left err -> Left err\\n            Right x -> Right (x : xs)\\n\\nadaptProperty :: Aeson.Key -> Aeson.Value -> Either String ParamProperty\\nadaptProperty k val =\\n    case propMappingResult of\\n        Aeson.Success prop ->\\n            Right $\\n                ParamProperty\\n                    (AesonKey.toText k)\\n                    (OpaqueParamType prop._type)\\n                    prop._description\\n        Aeson.Error err -> Left err\\n  where\\n    propMappingResult :: Aeson.Result PropertyHelper\\n    propMappingResult = Aeson.fromJSON val\\n\\ndata PropertyHelper\\n    = PropertyHelper {_type :: Text, _description :: Text}\\n\\ninstance Aeson.FromJSON PropertyHelper where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"PropertyHelper\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        PropertyHelper <$> o Aeson..: \\\"type\\\" <*> o Aeson..: \\\"description\\\"\\n\\n\""

ToJSON, (.:), (.=))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Char (toLower)\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport Data.UUID (UUID)\\nimport qualified Data.UUID.V4 as UUID\\nimport GHC.Generics (Generic)\\n\\ntype AgentSlug = Text\\ntype AgentAnnounce = Text\\n\\nnewtype AgentId = AgentId UUID\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)\\n\\nnewAgentId :: IO AgentId\\nnewAgentId =\\n    AgentId <$> UUID.nextRandom\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nnewtype ConversationId = ConversationId UUID\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)\\n\\nnewConversationId :: IO ConversationId\\nnewConversationId =\\n    ConversationId <$> UUID.nextRandom\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nnewtype StepId = StepId UUID\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)\\n\\nnewStepId :: IO StepId\\nnewStepId =\\n    StepId <$> UUID.nextRandom\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Note: SessionId and TurnId are defined in System.Agents.Session.Types\\n-- to avoid circular dependencies between Session.Base and Tools.Context.\\n-- They are re-exported from Session.Base for convenience.\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Reference to an additional agent outside the toolDirectory hierarchy\\n--\\n-- This enables agents to reference other agents explicitly, supporting\\n-- self-references and mutual recursion in the agent tree.\\ndata ExtraAgentRef\\n    = ExtraAgentRef\\n    { extraAgentSlug :: AgentSlug\\n    -- ^ The slug to use when referring to this agent\\n    , extraAgentPath :: FilePath\\n    -- ^ Path to the agent's JSON configuration file\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\n\\n-- | Custom JSON options for ExtraAgentRef to use hyphenated field names\\nextraAgentRefOptions :: Aeson.Options\\nextraAgentRefOptions = Aeson.defaultOptions\\n    { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = kebabCase . dropPrefix \\\"extraAgent\\\"\\n    }\\n  where\\n    -- Convert camelCase to kebab-case\\n    kebabCase [] = []\\n    kebabCase (c:cs) = toLower c : go cs\\n      where\\n        go [] = []\\n        go (x:xs)\\n            | x `elem` ['A'..'Z'] = '-' : toLower x : go xs\\n            | otherwise = x : go xs\\n    -- Drop the \\\"extraAgent\\\" prefix from field names\\n    dropPrefix prefix str\\n        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str\\n        | otherwise = str\\n\\ninstance ToJSON ExtraAgentRef where\\n    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON extraAgentRefOptions\\n    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding extraAgentRefOptions\\n\\ninstance FromJSON ExtraAgentRef where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON extraAgentRefOptions\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- OpenAPI Toolbox Configuration\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Configuration for an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- This describes an OpenAPI specification to load as a toolbox.\\n-- The toolbox will fetch the spec from 'openApiSpecUrl' and make\\n-- all operations available as tools to the agent.\\n--\\n-- Example configuration:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- {\\n--   \\\"tag\\\": \\\"OpenAPIServer\\\",\\n--   \\\"contents\\\": {\\n--     \\\"specUrl\\\": \\\"https://api.example.com/openapi.json\\\",\\n--     \\\"baseUrl\\\": \\\"https://api.example.com\\\",\\n--     \\\"headers\\\": {\\\"X-API-Version\\\": \\\"v1\\\"},\\n--     \\\"token\\\": \\\"${API_TOKEN}\\\"\\n--   }\\n-- }\\n-- @\\ndata OpenAPIServerDescription\\n    = OpenAPIServerDescription\\n    { openApiSpecUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ URL to fetch the OpenAPI specification from\\n    , openApiBaseUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ Base URL for API calls (can be different from spec URL)\\n    , openApiHeaders :: Maybe (Map Text Text)\\n    -- ^ Optional static headers to include in all requests\\n    , openApiToken :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ Optional Bearer token for authentication\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\n\\n-- | Custom JSON options for OpenAPIServerDescription to use camelCase field names\\n-- (matching the existing Agent JSON style)\\nopenApiServerOptions :: Aeson.Options\\nopenApiServerOptions = Aeson.defaultOptions\\n    { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = dropPrefix \\\"openApi\\\"\\n    , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True\\n    }\\n  where\\n    dropPrefix prefix str\\n        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str\\n        | otherwise = str\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPIServerDescription where\\n    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON openApiServerOptions\\n    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding openApiServerOptions\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPIServerDescription where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON openApiServerOptions\\n\\n-- | Wrapper type for JSON serialization with tag.\\n-- Similar to McpServerDescription, this allows extensible toolbox types.\\ndata OpenAPIToolboxDescription\\n    = OpenAPIServer OpenAPIServerDescription\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPIToolboxDescription where\\n    toJSON (OpenAPIServer val) =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"tag\\\" .= (\\\"OpenAPIServer\\\" :: Text)\\n            , \\\"contents\\\" .= val\\n            ]\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPIToolboxDescription where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"OpenAPIToolboxDescription\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n        tag <- v .: \\\"tag\\\"\\n        case (tag :: Text) of\\n            \\\"OpenAPIServer\\\" ->\\n                OpenAPIServer <$> v .: \\\"contents\\\"\\n            _ -> fail \\\"expecting OpenAPIServer 'tag'\\\"\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\ndata Agent\\n    = Agent\\n    { slug :: Text\\n    , apiKeyId :: Text\\n    , flavor :: Text\\n    , modelUrl :: Text\\n    , modelName :: Text\\n    , announce :: Text\\n    , systemPrompt :: [Text]\\n    , toolDirectory :: FilePath\\n    , mcpServers :: Maybe [McpServerDescription]\\n    , openApiToolboxes :: Maybe [OpenAPIToolboxDescription]\\n    , extraAgents :: Maybe [ExtraAgentRef]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\n\\n-- | Custom JSON options for Agent - uses camelCase to match existing format\\nagentOptions :: Aeson.Options\\nagentOptions = Aeson.defaultOptions\\n    { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = id\\n    , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True\\n    }\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Agent where\\n    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON agentOptions\\n    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding agentOptions\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Agent where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON agentOptions\\n\\ndata AgentDescription\\n    = AgentDescription Agent\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\ninstance ToJSON AgentDescription where\\n    toJSON (AgentDescription val) =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"tag\\\" .= (\\\"OpenAIAgentDescription\\\" :: Text)\\n            , \\\"contents\\\" .= val\\n            ]\\n\\ninstance FromJSON AgentDescription where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"AgentDescription\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n        tag <- v .: \\\"tag\\\"\\n        case (tag :: Text) of\\n            \\\"OpenAIAgentDescription\\\" ->\\n                AgentDescription <$> v .: \\\"contents\\\"\\n            _ -> fail \\\"expecting OpenAIAgentDescription 'tag'\\\"\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\ndata McpSimpleBinaryConfiguration\\n    = McpSimpleBinaryConfiguration\\n    { name :: Text\\n    , executable :: FilePath\\n    , args :: [Text]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON McpSimpleBinaryConfiguration\\ninstance ToJSON McpSimpleBinaryConfiguration\\ndata McpServerDescription\\n    = McpSimpleBinary McpSimpleBinaryConfiguration\\n    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)\\ninstance ToJSON McpServerDescription where\\n    toJSON (McpSimpleBinary val) =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"tag\\\" .= (\\\"McpSimpleBinary\\\" :: Text)\\n            , \\\"contents\\\" .= val\\n            ]\\n\\ninstance FromJSON McpServerDescription where\\n    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject \\\"McpServerDescription\\\" $ \\\\v -> do\\n        tag <- v .: \\\"tag\\\"\\n        case (tag :: Text) of\\n            \\\"McpSimpleBinary\\\" ->\\n "\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}\\n\\nmodule System.Agents.Runtime.Runtime (\\n    Runtime (..),\\n    addTracer,\\n    newRuntime,\\n    triggerRefreshTools,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Concurrent.STM (STM, atomically, readTVar)\\nimport Data.Either (rights)\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport qualified Prod.Background as Background\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer, contramap, traceBoth)\\nimport qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient\\n\\nimport System.Agents.Base (AgentAnnounce, AgentId, AgentSlug, newAgentId)\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport System.Agents.ToolRegistration\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.BashToolbox as BashToolbox\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.McpToolbox as McpTools\\n\\nimport System.Agents.Runtime.Trace\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndata Runtime\\n    = Runtime\\n    { agentSlug :: AgentSlug\\n    , agentId :: AgentId\\n    , agentAnnounce :: AgentAnnounce\\n    , agentTracer :: Tracer IO Trace\\n    , agentAuthenticatedHttpClientRuntime :: HttpClient.Runtime\\n    , agentModel :: OpenAI.Model\\n    , agentTools :: IO [ToolRegistration]\\n    , agentTriggerRefreshTools :: STM Bool\\n    }\\n\\n{- | Adds an extra tracer to the runtime, hence returning a modified Runtime.\\n\\nIn current implementation (arbitrary) the extra tracer is ran before the\\nalready-in-place one.\\n-}\\naddTracer :: Runtime -> (Tracer IO Trace) -> Runtime\\naddTracer rt t = rt{agentTracer = traceBoth t rt.agentTracer}\\n\\ntriggerRefreshTools :: Runtime -> IO Bool\\ntriggerRefreshTools rt = atomically $ rt.agentTriggerRefreshTools\\n\\n-- todo: directly ask for effects returning registrations\\ntype ToolboxDirectory = FilePath\\ntype IOToolBuilder = AgentSlug -> AgentId -> ToolRegistration\\ntype McpToolConfig = McpTools.Toolbox\\n\\nnewRuntime ::\\n    AgentSlug ->\\n    AgentAnnounce ->\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    OpenAI.ApiKey ->\\n    OpenAI.Model ->\\n    ToolboxDirectory ->\\n    [IOToolBuilder] ->\\n    [McpToolConfig] ->\\n    IO (Either String Runtime)\\nnewRuntime slug announce tracer apiKey model tooldir mkIoTools mcpToolboxes = do\\n    uid <- newAgentId\\n    let ioTools = [mk slug uid | mk <- mkIoTools]\\n    toolz <- BashToolbox.initializeBackroundToolbox (contramap (AgentTrace_Loading slug uid) tracer) tooldir\\n    case toolz of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left (show err)\\n        Right toolbox -> do\\n            let auth = HttpClient.BearerToken $ Text.decodeUtf8 $ OpenAI.revealApiKey apiKey\\n            httpRt <- HttpClient.newRuntime auth\\n            let appendIOTools xs = ioTools <> xs\\n            let registerTools xs = fmap registerBashToolInLLM xs\\n            let bkgToolsWithIOTools = fmap (appendIOTools . registerTools) toolbox.tools\\n\\n            let readTools = (<>) <$> Background.readBackgroundVal bkgToolsWithIOTools <*> readMcpToolsRegistrations\\n            let rt = Runtime slug uid announce tracer httpRt model readTools toolbox.triggerReload\\n            pure $ Right rt\\n  where\\n    readMcpToolsRegistrations :: IO [ToolRegistration]\\n    readMcpToolsRegistrations = do\\n        -- TODO: would be nice to collect the lefts and trace errors upon relaoding\\n        lists <- traverse (atomically . readTVar . McpTools.toolsList) $ mcpToolboxes\\n        let reg tb tds = rights [registerMcpToolInLLM tb td | td <- tds]\\n        pure $ mconcat $ zipWith reg mcpToolboxes lists\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI Toolbox runtime for executing tools from OpenAPI specifications.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a runtime that:\\n-- * Fetches OpenAPI specifications from URLs\\n-- * Converts operations to executable tools\\n-- * Handles HTTP execution with proper parameter substitution\\n-- * Integrates with the LLM tool registration system\\n--\\n-- The toolbox follows patterns similar to McpToolbox and BashToolbox for\\n-- consistent integration with the agents system.\\n--\\n-- Example usage:\\n--\\n-- @\\n-- import System.Agents\\n-- import qualified System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox as OpenAPI\\n--\\n-- main :: IO ()\\n-- main = do\\n--     let config = OpenAPI.Config\\n--             { configUrl = \\\"https://api.example.com/openapi.json\\\"\\n--             , configBaseUrl = \\\"https://api.example.com\\\"\\n--             , configHeaders = Map.empty\\n--             , configToken = Just \\\"my-bearer-token\\\"\\n--             }\\n--     result <- OpenAPI.initializeToolbox tracer config\\n--     case result of\\n--         Right toolbox -> do\\n--             -- Register tools with LLM\\n--             regResult <- registerOpenAPITools toolbox\\n--             case regResult of\\n--                 Right registrations -> useWithAgent registrations\\n--                 Left err -> handleError err\\n--         Left err -> handleError err\\n-- @\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPIToolbox (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    Trace (..),\\n    Toolbox (..),\\n    Config (..),\\n    InitializationError (..),\\n    \\n    -- * Re-export ToolResult from Types\\n    ToolResult,\\n    \\n    -- * Initialization\\n    initializeToolbox,\\n    \\n    -- * Tool execution\\n    createToolHandler,\\n    handleToolCall,\\n    \\n    -- * Path formatting\\n    formatPath,\\n    \\n    -- * HTTP building\\n    buildRequest,\\n    buildFullUrl,\\n    \\n    -- * Response handling\\n    handleResponse,\\n    \\n    -- * Operation helpers\\n    getOperationId,\\n    \\n    -- * Naming helper\\n    openapi2LLMName,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Exception (try)\\nimport Data.Aeson (Value (..))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Char8 as ByteString\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LByteString\\nimport qualified Data.CaseInsensitive as CI\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Client as HttpClient\\nimport qualified Network.HTTP.Types as HttpTypes\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..), runTracer)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (OpenAPITool (..), convertOpenAPIToTools)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver (dereferenceSpec)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n  )\\nimport qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Base (CallResult (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolExecutionContext)\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.IO (RunError (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.Trace (ToolTrace (..))\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Trace types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Trace events for OpenAPI toolbox operations.\\n--\\n-- These events allow monitoring of:\\n-- * Spec fetching progress\\n-- * Tool conversion status\\n-- * Endpoint execution\\n-- * Error conditions\\ndata Trace\\n    = FetchingSpecTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ URL being fetched\\n    | SpecFetchedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of spec fetch\\n    | ToolsConvertedTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ Number of tools converted from spec\\n    | CallingEndpointTrace !Text !Text !Text\\n    -- ^ method, url, path being called\\n    | EndpointResponseTrace !Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status of endpoint response\\n    | SchemaResolutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during schema dereferencing\\n    | ToolExecutionErrorTrace !Text\\n    -- ^ Error during tool execution\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during toolbox initialization.\\ndata InitializationError\\n    = NetworkError !Text\\n    -- ^ Network error fetching spec\\n    | ParseError !Text\\n    -- ^ JSON parse error\\n    | SpecError !Text\\n    -- ^ OpenAPI spec validation error\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Configuration for initializing an OpenAPI toolbox.\\ndata Config = Config\\n    { configUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ URL to fetch OpenAPI spec from\\n    , configBaseUrl :: Text\\n    -- ^ Base URL for API calls\\n    , configHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers to include in all requests\\n    , configToken :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ Optional Bearer token for authentication\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Runtime state for an OpenAPI toolbox.\\n--\\n-- The toolbox maintains:\\n-- * A name for identification\\n-- * The base URL for API calls\\n-- * A list of available tools\\n-- * The HTTP runtime for making requests\\n-- * An optional header function for dynamic auth headers\\ndata Toolbox = Toolbox\\n    { toolboxName :: Text\\n    , toolboxBaseUrl :: Text\\n    , toolboxTools :: [OpenAPITool]\\n    -- ^ Tools converted from the OpenAPI spec\\n    , httpRuntime :: HttpClient.Runtime\\n    -- ^ HTTP client runtime\\n    , headerFunc :: Maybe (IO (Map Text Text))\\n    -- ^ Optional function to get dynamic headers (e.g., for auth)\\n    , staticHeaders :: Map Text Text\\n    -- ^ Static headers from config\\n    }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Initialization\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Initialize an OpenAPI toolbox from a configuration.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Creates an HTTP runtime with authentication\\n-- 2. Fetches the OpenAPI spec from the configured URL\\n-- 3. Parses the JSON to an OpenAPISpec\\n-- 4. Dereferences schema references ($ref)\\n-- 5. Converts operations to tools\\n--\\n-- Returns an 'InitializationError' if any step fails.\\ninitializeToolbox ::\\n    Tracer IO Trace ->\\n    Config ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError Toolbox)\\ninitializeToolbox tracer config = do\\n    -- Create HTTP runtime with auth\\n    let token = case config.configToken of\\n            Just t -> HttpClient.BearerToken t\\n            Nothing -> HttpClient.NoToken\\n    runtime <- HttpClient.newRuntime token\\n\\n    -- Fetch OpenAPI spec\\n    runTracer tracer (FetchingSpecTrace config.configUrl)\\n    fetchResult <- fetchSpec runtime config.configUrl\\n\\n    case fetchResult of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right (status, body) -> do\\n            runTracer tracer (SpecFetchedTrace status)\\n\\n            -- Parse JSON to OpenAPISpec\\n            case Aeson.decode body of\\n                Nothing -> pure $ Left $ ParseError \\\"Failed to parse OpenAPI spec JSON\\\"\\n                Just spec -> do\\n                    -- Dereference schemas\\n                    let dereferencedSpec = dereferenceSpec spec\\n\\n                    -- Convert to tools\\n                    let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools dereferencedSpec\\n                    runTracer tracer (ToolsConvertedTrace (length tools))\\n\\n                    -- Create toolbox\\n                    pure $ Right $ Toolbox\\n                        { toolboxName = Text.takeWhile (/= '/') (Text.drop 8 config.configUrl)\\n                            -- Use part of URL as name, removing protocol\\n                        , toolboxBaseUrl = config.configBaseUrl\\n                        , toolboxTools = tools\\n                        , httpRuntime = runtime\\n                        , headerFunc = Nothing\\n                        , staticHeaders = config.configHeaders\\n                        }\\n\\n-- | Fetch the OpenAPI spec from a URL.\\nfetchSpec ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Text ->\\n    IO (Either InitializationError (Int, LByteString.ByteString))\\nfetchSpec runtime url = do\\n    result <- try $ HttpClient.get runtime (Tracer (const (pure ()))) url\\n    case result of\\n        Left (e :: HttpClient.HttpException) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show e)\\n        Right (Left err) ->\\n            pure $ Left $ NetworkError (Text.pack $ show err)\\n        Right (Right response) ->\\n            let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n                body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n             in pure $ Right (status, body)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Handler Creation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Creates a handler function for an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This handler integrates with the ToolExecutionContext system and\\n-- returns results in the standard CallResult format.\\ncreateToolHandler ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Tracer IO ToolTrace ->\\n    ToolExecutionContext ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (CallResult ())\\ncreateToolHandler toolbox tool tracer _ctx args = do\\n    result <- handleToolCall toolbox tool args\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> do\\n            runTracer (contramapTrace tracer) (ToolExecutionErrorTrace err)\\n            pure $ IOToolError () (ScriptExecutionError (Text.unpack err))\\n        Right (textResult, _toolResult) -> do\\n            pure $ BlobToolSuccess () (ByteString.pack $ Text.unpack textResult)\\n  where\\n    contramapTrace :: Tracer IO ToolTrace -> Tracer IO Trace\\n    contramapTrace _t = Tracer $ \\\\_traceEvent -> do\\n        -- Convert Trace to ToolTrace if needed\\n        -- For now, we just don't trace these events to the main tool tracer\\n        pure ()\\n\\n-- | Handle a tool call by executing the HTTP request.\\n--\\n-- This function:\\n-- 1. Extracts path params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 2. Extracts query params (prefixed with p_)\\n-- 3. Extracts body (param \\\"b\\\")\\n-- 4. Formats the path with path args\\n-- 5. Builds full URL\\n-- 6. Makes HTTP request\\n-- 7. Returns result\\nhandleToolCall ::\\n    Toolbox ->\\n    OpenAPITool ->\\n    Value ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleToolCall toolbox tool args = do\\n    case args of\\n        Object obj -> do\\n            -- Extract parameters from the JSON object\\n            let objMap = KeyMap.toMapText obj\\n            let (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue) = extractParams objMap (toolOperation tool)\\n\\n            -- Format the path with path parameters\\n            let formattedPath = formatPath (toolPath tool) pathParams\\n\\n            -- Build full URL\\n            let fullUrl = buildFullUrl (toolboxBaseUrl toolbox) formattedPath\\n\\n            -- Get headers\\n            headers <- case toolbox.headerFunc of\\n                Just hf -> hf\\n                Nothing -> pure Map.empty\\n            let allHeaders = Map.union headers (staticHeaders toolbox)\\n\\n            -- Make HTTP request\\n            let method = toolMethod tool\\n            runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ CallingEndpointTrace method fullUrl formattedPath\\n\\n            result <- executeRequest toolbox.httpRuntime method fullUrl queryParams bodyValue allHeaders\\n\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n                Right (textResult, toolResult) -> do\\n                    runTracer (Tracer (const (pure ()) :: Trace -> IO ())) $ EndpointResponseTrace (resultStatus toolResult)\\n                    pure $ Right (textResult, toolResult)\\n        _ -> pure $ Left \\\"Tool arguments must be a JSON object\\\"\\n\\n-- | Extract parameters from the JSON object based on operation definition.\\nextractParams ::\\n    Map Text Value ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    (Map Text Text, Map Text Text, Maybe Value)\\nextractParams obj op =\\n    let params = opParameters op\\n        -- Extract path params\\n        pathParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractPathParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractPathParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInPath = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractPathParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract query params\\n        queryParams = Map.foldrWithKey extractQueryParam Map.empty obj\\n          where\\n            extractQueryParam k (String v) acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) v acc\\n            extractQueryParam k v acc\\n                | Just p <- findParam params (Text.drop 2 k)\\n                , paramIn p == ParamInQuery = Map.insert (Text.drop 2 k) (valueToText v) acc\\n            extractQueryParam _ _ acc = acc\\n\\n        -- Extract body\\n        bodyValue = Map.lookup \\\"b\\\" obj\\n     in (pathParams, queryParams, bodyValue)\\n\\n-- | Find a parameter by name in the operation parameters list.\\nfindParam :: [Parameter] -> Text -> Maybe Parameter\\nfindParam params name =\\n    case filter (\\\\p -> paramName p == name) params of\\n        (p : _) -> Just p\\n        [] -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Convert a JSON value to Text for query params.\\nvalueToText :: Value -> Text\\nvalueToText (String s) = s\\nvalueToText (Number n) = Text.pack $ show n\\nvalueToText (Bool b) = if b then \\\"true\\\" else \\\"false\\\"\\nvalueToText Null = \\\"null\\\"\\nvalueToText _ = \\\"\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path Formatting\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Format a path template with path parameters.\\n--\\n-- Converts a path like @/pets/{petId}@ with @{p_petId: \\\"123\\\"}@ to @/pets/123@.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123\\\"\\n--\\n-- >>> formatPath \\\"/pets/{petId}/owners/{ownerId}\\\" (Map.fromList [(\\\"petId\\\", \\\"123\\\"), (\\\"ownerId\\\", \\\"456\\\")])\\n-- \\\"/pets/123/owners/456\\\"\\nformatPath :: Path -> Map Text Text -> Path\\nformatPath pathTemplate pathArgs =\\n    Map.foldrWithKey replaceParam pathTemplate pathArgs\\n  where\\n    replaceParam :: Text -> Text -> Path -> Path\\n    replaceParam name value path =\\n        Text.replace (\\\"{\\\" <> name <> \\\"}\\\") value path\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- URL Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build the full URL from base URL and path.\\nbuildFullUrl :: Text -> Path -> Text\\nbuildFullUrl baseUrl path =\\n    let base = Text.dropWhileEnd (== '/') baseUrl\\n        path' = if Text.isPrefixOf \\\"/\\\" path then path else \\\"/\\\" <> path\\n     in base <> path'\\n\\n-- | Build query string from query parameters.\\nbuildQueryString :: Map Text Text -> Text\\nbuildQueryString params\\n    | Map.null params = \\\"\\\"\\n    | otherwise =\\n        \\\"?\\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\"&\\\" (map encodeParam $ Map.toList params)\\n  where\\n    encodeParam (k, v) = urlEncode k <> \\\"=\\\" <> urlEncode v\\n    urlEncode = id -- Simplified - real implementation would URL-encode\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- HTTP Request Building and Execution\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Build an HTTP request.\\nbuildRequest ::\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO HttpClient.Request\\nbuildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    let queryString = buildQueryString queryParams\\n    let urlWithQuery = fullUrl <> queryString\\n\\n    req <- HttpClient.parseRequest (Text.unpack urlWithQuery)\\n\\n    -- Set method\\n    let reqWithMethod = req{HttpClient.method = Text.encodeUtf8 method}\\n\\n    -- Add body if present\\n    let reqWithBody = case mbody of\\n            Just body ->\\n                reqWithMethod\\n                    { HttpClient.requestBody = HttpClient.RequestBodyLBS (Aeson.encode body)\\n                    , HttpClient.requestHeaders =\\n                        (\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n                            : HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithMethod\\n                    }\\n            Nothing -> reqWithMethod\\n\\n    -- Add custom headers\\n    let headerList = map (\\\\(k, v) -> (CI.mk (Text.encodeUtf8 k), Text.encodeUtf8 v)) $ Map.toList headers\\n    let finalReq = reqWithBody{HttpClient.requestHeaders = HttpClient.requestHeaders reqWithBody ++ headerList}\\n\\n    pure finalReq\\n\\n-- | Execute an HTTP request.\\nexecuteRequest ::\\n    HttpClient.Runtime ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Text ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    Maybe Value ->\\n    Map Text Text ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nexecuteRequest runtime method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers = do\\n    _ <- buildRequest method fullUrl queryParams mbody headers\\n\\n    -- Use the runtime to make the request\\n    -- Note: We're using a simplified approach here\\n    -- In a real implementation, we'd use the HttpClient.Runtime properly\\n    let tracer = Tracer (const (pure ()))\\n    result <- executeRequest' runtime tracer method fullUrl mbody\\n\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left err\\n        Right response -> handleResponse toolInfo response\\n  where\\n    toolInfo = (method, fullUrl)\\n    executeRequest' rt tr m url mb\\n        | m == \\\"GET\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.get rt tr url\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | m == \\\"POST\\\" = do\\n            res <- HttpClient.post rt tr url mb\\n            pure $ either (Left . Text.pack . show) Right res\\n        | otherwise = do\\n            -- For other methods, we need to use the underlying manager\\n            pure $ Left $ \\\"Method not supported: \\\" <> m\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Response Handling\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handle an HTTP response, converting to ToolResult.\\n--\\n-- Returns both text for the LLM and a structured ToolResult.\\nhandleResponse ::\\n    (Method, Text) ->\\n    HttpClient.Response LByteString.ByteString ->\\n    IO (Either Text (Text, ToolResult))\\nhandleResponse (method, url) response = do\\n    let status = HttpTypes.statusCode (HttpClient.responseStatus response)\\n    let body = HttpClient.responseBody response\\n\\n    -- Parse body as JSON if possible\\n    let payload = case Aeson.decode body of\\n            Just val -> val\\n            Nothing -> String (Text.decodeUtf8 $ LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    -- Create text representation for LLM\\n    let textResult =\\n            \\\"HTTP \\\" <> Text.pack (show status) <> \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n                <> Text.decodeUtf8 (LByteString.toStrict body)\\n\\n    pure $ Right\\n        ( textResult\\n        , ToolResult\\n            { resultPath = url\\n            , resultMethod = method\\n            , resultStatus = status\\n            , resultPayload = payload\\n            }\\n        )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Get operation ID from operation, falling back to tool name.\\n--\\n-- This is used for tool registration when creating unique tool names.\\ngetOperationId :: Operation -> Maybe Text\\ngetOperationId = opOperationId\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Naming helper\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Convert an OpenAPI operation ID to an LLM tool name.\\n--\\n-- Names are prefixed with @openapi_@ and include the toolbox name\\n-- to avoid conflicts.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n--\\n-- >>> openapi2LLMName \\\"myApi\\\" \\\"getPet\\\"\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_myApi_getPet\\\"}\\nopenapi2LLMName :: Text -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName\\nopenapi2LLMName tboxName operationId =\\n    OpenAI.ToolName (\\\"openapi_\\\" <> tboxName <> \\\"_\\\" <> operationId)\\n\\n\""

traceUsefulPromptStdout,\\n) where\\n\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.Types as Aeson\\nimport qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LByteString\\nimport qualified Data.Maybe as Maybe\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text\\nimport qualified Data.Text.IO as Text\\nimport GHC.IO.Handle (Handle)\\nimport Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..))\\nimport System.IO (stderr, stdout)\\n\\nimport System.Agents.AgentTree\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Runtime.Trace as Runtime\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools as Tools\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.Bash as Tools\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.BashToolbox as BashToolbox\\nimport qualified System.Agents.Tools.IO as Tools\\n\\ntracePrintingTextResponses :: Tracer IO Trace\\ntracePrintingTextResponses = Tracer f\\n  where\\n    f (AgentTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Conversation slug _ _ trace)) =\\n        g [slug] trace\\n    f (AgentTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Loading _ _ _)) = pure ()\\n    f (McpTrace _ _) = pure ()\\n    f (DataLoadingTrace _) = pure ()\\n    f (ConfigLoadedTrace _) = pure ()\\n    f (CyclicReferencesWarning _) = pure ()\\n    f (ReferenceValidationTrace _) = pure ()\\n\\n    g pfx (Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.GotChatCompletion x)) =\\n        case Aeson.parseEither OpenAI.parseLLMResponse x of\\n            Left _ -> pure ()\\n            Right rsp ->\\n                Text.putStrLn $ Text.unwords [Text.intercalate \\\"/\\\" pfx, Maybe.fromMaybe \\\"...\\\" rsp.rspContent]\\n    g pfx (Runtime.ChildrenTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Conversation childSlug _ _ sub)) =\\n        g (childSlug : pfx) sub\\n    g _ (Runtime.ChildrenTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Loading _ _ _)) = pure ()\\n    g _ (Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.HttpClientTrace _)) = pure ()\\n    g _ (Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.CallChatCompletion _)) = pure ()\\n    g _ (Runtime.NewConversation) = pure ()\\n    g _ (Runtime.WaitingForPrompt) = pure ()\\n    g _ (Runtime.RunToolTrace _ _) = pure ()\\n\\ntraceUsefulPromptStdout :: Tracer IO Trace\\ntraceUsefulPromptStdout = traceUsefulPromptHandle stdout\\n\\ntraceUsefulPromptStderr :: Tracer IO Trace\\ntraceUsefulPromptStderr = traceUsefulPromptHandle stderr\\n\\ntraceUsefulPromptHandle :: Handle -> Tracer IO Trace\\ntraceUsefulPromptHandle h = Tracer f\\n  where\\n    f (AgentTrace tr) =\\n        Text.hPutStrLn h $ renderAgentTrace tr\\n    f (McpTrace _ tr) =\\n        Text.hPutStrLn h (Text.pack $ show tr)\\n    f (DataLoadingTrace x) = Text.hPutStrLn h (Text.pack $ show x)\\n    f (ConfigLoadedTrace x) =\\n        Text.hPutStrLn h (showTree 0 x)\\n      where\\n        showTree :: Int -> AgentConfigTree -> Text.Text\\n        showTree n v =\\n            Text.unlines\\n                [ Text.concat [Text.replicate n \\\"  \\\", Text.pack v.agentConfigFile]\\n                , Text.unlines $ fmap (showTree (succ n)) v.agentConfigChildren\\n                ]\\n    f (CyclicReferencesWarning warns) =\\n        Text.hPutStrLn h $ \\\"Cyclic references warning: \\\" <> Text.pack (show warns)\\n    f (ReferenceValidationTrace refs) =\\n        Text.hPutStrLn h $ \\\"Reference validation trace: \\\" <> Text.pack (show refs)\\n\\nrenderAgentTrace :: Runtime.Trace -> Text\\nrenderAgentTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Loading slug _ tr) =\\n    Text.unlines\\n        [ mconcat [\\\"@\\\", slug, \\\":\\\"]\\n        , renderLoadingAgentTrace tr\\n        ]\\nrenderAgentTrace (Runtime.AgentTrace_Conversation slug _ _ tr) =\\n    Text.unlines\\n        [ mconcat [\\\"@\\\", slug, \\\":\\\"]\\n        , renderConversationAgentTrace tr\\n        ]\\n\\nrenderLoadingAgentTrace :: BashToolbox.Trace -> Text\\nrenderLoadingAgentTrace tr = case tr of\\n    BashToolbox.ReloadToolsTrace _ -> \\\"(reload-tools...)\\\"\\n    BashToolbox.BashToolsLoadingTrace _ -> \\\"(reload-tools...)\\\"\\n\\nrenderConversationAgentTrace :: Runtime.ConversationTrace -> Text\\nrenderConversationAgentTrace tr = case tr of\\n    Runtime.NewConversation -> \\\"\\\"\\n    Runtime.WaitingForPrompt -> \\\"\\\"\\n    Runtime.RunToolTrace _ (Tools.BashToolsTrace (Tools.RunCommandStart p args)) ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"bash-tool\\\", \\\"start\\\", Text.pack p, Text.unwords $ map Text.pack args]\\n    Runtime.RunToolTrace _ (Tools.BashToolsTrace (Tools.RunCommandStopped p args code _ _)) ->\\n        Text.unlines\\n            [ Text.unwords [\\\"bash-tool\\\", \\\"stopped\\\", Text.pack p, Text.unwords $ map Text.pack args]\\n            , Text.pack $ show code\\n            ]\\n    Runtime.RunToolTrace _ (Tools.IOToolsTrace (Tools.IOScriptStarted desc _)) ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"io-tool\\\", desc.ioSlug, \\\"start\\\"]\\n    Runtime.RunToolTrace _ (Tools.IOToolsTrace (Tools.IOScriptStopped desc _ _)) ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"io-tool\\\", desc.ioSlug, \\\"stop\\\"]\\n    Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.HttpClientTrace _) -> \\\"(http)\\\"\\n    Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.CallChatCompletion _) ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"to: llm\\\"]\\n    Runtime.LLMTrace _ (OpenAI.GotChatCompletion x) ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"from: llm\\\", jsonTxt x]\\n    Runtime.ChildrenTrace sub ->\\n        Text.unwords [\\\"(\\\", Runtime.traceAgentSlug sub, \\\")\\\", renderAgentTrace sub]\\n  where\\n    jsonTxt :: (Aeson.ToJSON a) => a -> Text\\n    jsonTxt = Text.decodeUtf8 . LByteString.toStrict . Aeson.encode\\n\\n\""
