                       { ExportImport.gitRemote = gitImportUrl gitSource\\n                        , ExportImport.gitBranch = gitImportRef gitSource\\n                        , ExportImport.gitPath = parseNamespaceToMaybeNs (importNamespace opts)\\n                        }\\n                let gitOpts = ExportImport.GitImportOptions\\n                        { ExportImport.gitRef = gitImportRef gitSource\\n                        , ExportImport.gitSparsePaths = []\\n                        }\\n                \\n                if importToolsOnly opts\\n                    then do\\n                        ePkg <- ExportImport.importToolsFromGit gitUrl0 (parseNamespaceToMaybeNs (importNamespace opts))\\n                        case ePkg of\\n                            Left err -> pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show err\\n                            Right pkg -> do\\n                                let installOpts = ExportInstall.InstallOptions\\n                                        { ExportInstall.installForce = importMode opts == ImportOverwrite || importMode opts == ImportMerge\\n                                        , ExportInstall.installLink = False\\n                                        , ExportInstall.installPrefix = Nothing\\n                                        }\\n                                result <- ExportInstall.importToolPackage pkg (importDestination opts) installOpts\\n                                case result of\\n                                    Left err -> pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show err\\n                                    Right () -> pure $ Right ()\\n                    else do\\n                        ePkg <- ExportImport.importFromGit gitUrl0 gitOpts\\n                        case ePkg of\\n                            Left err -> pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show err\\n                            Right _pkg -> do\\n                                -- TODO: Handle full package import\\n                                pure $ Right ()\\n        \\n        case eResult of\\n            Left err -> do\\n                Text.hPutStrLn stderr $ \\\"Import failed: \\\" <> err\\n                exitFailure\\n            Right () -> do\\n                Text.putStrLn \\\"Import successful\\\"\\n                exitSuccess\\n\\nhandleListNamespaces :: ImportOptions -> IO ()\\nhandleListNamespaces opts = do\\n    case importSource opts of\\n        ImportFromGit gitSource -> do\\n            let gitUrl0 = ExportImport.GitUrl\\n                    { ExportImport.gitRemote = gitImportUrl gitSource\\n                    , ExportImport.gitBranch = gitImportRef gitSource\\n                    , ExportImport.gitPath = parseNamespaceToMaybeNs (importNamespace opts)\\n                    }\\n            result <- ExportImport.listGitNamespaces gitUrl0\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> do\\n                    Text.hPutStrLn stderr $ \\\"Failed to list namespaces: \\\" <> Text.pack (show err)\\n                    exitFailure\\n                Right namespaces -> do\\n                    Text.putStrLn \\\"Available namespaces:\\\"\\n                    mapM_ (Text.putStrLn . (\\\"  \\\" <>) . Text.intercalate \\\".\\\" . ExportImport.unNamespace) namespaces\\n                    exitSuccess\\n        _ -> do\\n            Text.hPutStrLn stderr \\\"List namespaces only supported for git sources\\\"\\n            exitFailure\\n\\nhandleListTools :: ImportOptions -> IO ()\\nhandleListTools opts = do\\n    case importSource opts of\\n        ImportFromGit gitSource -> do\\n            let gitUrl0 = ExportImport.GitUrl\\n                    { ExportImport.gitRemote = gitImportUrl gitSource\\n                    , ExportImport.gitBranch = gitImportRef gitSource\\n                        , ExportImport.gitPath = parseNamespaceToMaybeNs (importNamespace opts)\\n                    }\\n            result <- ExportImport.listGitTools gitUrl0\\n            case result of\\n                Left err -> do\\n                    Text.hPutStrLn stderr $ \\\"Failed to list tools: \\\" <> Text.pack (show err)\\n                    exitFailure\\n                Right tools -> do\\n                    Text.putStrLn \\\"Available tools:\\\"\\n                    mapM_ (\\\\(ns, info0) -> do\\n                        Text.putStrLn $ \\\"  \\\" <> Text.intercalate \\\".\\\" (ExportImport.unNamespace ns) <> \\\":\\\"\\n                        Text.putStrLn $ \\\"    \\\" <> Bash.scriptSlug info0 <> \\\" - \\\" <> Bash.scriptDescription info0\\n                        ) tools\\n                    exitSuccess\\n        _ -> do\\n            Text.hPutStrLn stderr \\\"List tools only supported for git sources\\\"\\n            exitFailure\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper Functions\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nloadAgentFromFile :: FilePath -> IO (Either Text Agent)\\nloadAgentFromFile path = do\\n    result <- Aeson.eitherDecodeFileStrict' path\\n    case result of\\n        Left err -> pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ \\\"Failed to parse agent file: \\\" ++ err\\n        Right (AgentDescription agent) -> pure $ Right agent\\n\\nloadToolsForAgent :: FilePath -> Agent -> IO [ExportImport.ToolExport]\\nloadToolsForAgent agentFile agent = do\\n    let toolDir = takeDirectory agentFile </> toolDirectory agent\\n    exists <- doesDirectoryExist toolDir\\n    if not exists\\n        then pure []\\n        else do\\n            toolFiles <- ExportInstall.discoverTools toolDir\\n            mapM (loadToolExport toolDir) toolFiles\\n  where\\n    loadToolExport :: FilePath -> FilePath -> IO ExportImport.ToolExport\\n    loadToolExport _baseDir toolPath = do\\n        content <- ByteString.readFile toolPath\\n        perms <- ExportImport.getFileMode toolPath\\n        let tName = Text.pack $ takeFileName toolPath\\n        -- Run describe to get metadata\\n        _ <- ExportInstall.validateTool toolPath\\n        pure $ ExportImport.ToolExport\\n            { ExportImport.toolName = tName\\n            , ExportImport.toolContent = content\\n            , ExportImport.toolPermissions = perms\\n            , ExportImport.toolMetadata = Nothing  -- Could be populated by running describe\\n            , ExportImport.toolNamespace = Nothing\\n            }\\n\\nloadStandaloneToolsForAgent :: FilePath -> Agent -> IO [ExportImport.StandaloneToolExport]\\nloadStandaloneToolsForAgent agentFile agent = do\\n    let toolDir = takeDirectory agentFile </> toolDirectory agent\\n    exists <- doesDirectoryExist toolDir\\n    if not exists\\n        then pure []\\n        else do\\n            toolFiles <- ExportInstall.discoverTools toolDir\\n            mapM (loadStandaloneTool toolDir) toolFiles\\n  where\\n    loadStandaloneTool :: FilePath -> FilePath -> IO ExportImport.StandaloneToolExport\\n    loadStandaloneTool _baseDir toolPath = do\\n        content <- ByteString.readFile toolPath\\n        perms <- ExportImport.getFileMode toolPath\\n        -- Run describe to get metadata\\n        (code, out, _) <- Process.readProcessWithExitCode toolPath [\\\"describe\\\"] \\\"\\\"\\n        let metadata = case code of\\n                ExitSuccess ->\\n                    case Aeson.eitherDecode (LByteString.fromStrict $ TextEncoding.encodeUtf8 $ Text.pack out) of\\n                        Left _ -> defaultMetadata toolPath\\n                        Right info0 -> info0\\n                _ -> defaultMetadata toolPath\\n        pure $ ExportImport.StandaloneToolExport\\n            { ExportImport.standaloneToolInfo = metadata\\n            , ExportImport.standaloneToolScript = content\\n            , ExportImport.standaloneToolPermissions = perms\\n            , ExportImport.standaloneToolAuxFiles = []\\n            }\\n    \\n    defaultMetadata :: FilePath -> Bash.ScriptInfo\\n    defaultMetadata path = Bash.ScriptInfo\\n        { Bash.scriptArgs = []\\n        , Bash.scriptSlug = Text.pack $ takeFileName path\\n        , Bash.scriptDescription = \\\"Imported tool\\\"\\n        , Bash.scriptEmptyResultBehavior = Nothing\\n        }\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Check Command Helper\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nprintAgentCheck :: AgentTree.AgentTree -> IO ()\\nprintAgentCheck tree = do\\n    tools <- Runtime.agentTools tree.agentRuntime\\n    let toolCount = length tools\\n    Text.putStrLn $\\n        Runtime.agentSlug tree.agentRuntime\\n        <> \\\": \\\"\\n        <> Runtime.agentAnnounce tree.agentRuntime\\n        <> \\\" (\\\"\\n        <> Text.pack (show toolCount)\\n        <> \\\" tools)\\\"\\n\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Utility Functions\\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nmaybeToEither :: Maybe a -> Either () a\\nmaybeToEither Nothing = Left ()\\nmaybeToEither (Just v) = Right v\\n\\ntoJsonTrace :: AgentTree.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\ntoJsonTrace x = case x of\\n    AgentTree.AgentTrace v -> encodeAgentTrace v\\n    AgentTree.McpTrace cfg v -> encodeMcpTrace cfg v\\n    AgentTree.DataLoadingTrace _ -> Nothing\\n    AgentTree.ConfigLoadedTrace _ -> Nothing\\n    AgentTree.CyclicReferencesWarning _ -> Nothing\\n    AgentTree.ReferenceValidationTrace _ -> Nothing\\n  where\\n    encodeAgentTrace :: RuntimeTrace.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeAgentTrace tr = do\\n        baseVal <- encodeBaseAgentTrace tr\\n        Just $\\n            Aeson.object\\n                [ \\\"e\\\"\\n                    .= Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"slug\\\" .= RuntimeTrace.traceAgentSlug tr\\n                        , \\\"agent-id\\\" .= RuntimeTrace.traceAgentId tr\\n                        , \\\"val\\\" .= baseVal\\n                        ]\\n                ]\\n\\n    encodeBaseAgentTrace :: RuntimeTrace.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeBaseAgentTrace (RuntimeTrace.AgentTrace_Loading _ _ tr) =\\n        encodeBaseTrace_Loading tr\\n    encodeBaseAgentTrace (RuntimeTrace.AgentTrace_Conversation _ _ convId tr) = do\\n        baseVal <- encodeBaseTrace_Conversation tr\\n        Just $\\n            Aeson.object\\n                [ \\\"conv-id\\\" .= convId\\n                , \\\"val\\\" .= baseVal\\n                ]\\n\\n    encodeBaseTrace_Loading :: BashToolbox.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeBaseTrace_Loading bt =\\n        case bt of\\n            (BashToolbox.BashToolsLoadingTrace _) -> Nothing\\n            (BashToolbox.ReloadToolsTrace _) -> Nothing\\n\\n    encodeBaseTrace_Conversation :: RuntimeTrace.ConversationTrace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeBaseTrace_Conversation bt =\\n        case bt of\\n            (RuntimeTrace.NewConversation) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"new-conversation\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.WaitingForPrompt) ->\\n                Nothing\\n            (RuntimeTrace.LLMTrace _ (LLMTrace.HttpClientTrace _)) ->\\n                Nothing\\n            (RuntimeTrace.LLMTrace uuid (LLMTrace.CallChatCompletion val)) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"llm\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"call\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"call-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"val\\\" .= val\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.LLMTrace uuid (LLMTrace.GotChatCompletion val)) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"llm\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"result\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"call-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"val\\\" .= val\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.RunToolTrace uuid (ToolsTrace.BashToolsTrace (ToolsTrace.RunCommandStart cmd targs))) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"run-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"flavor\\\" .= (\\\"bash\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"start\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"cmd\\\" .= cmd\\n                        , \\\"args\\\" .= targs\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.RunToolTrace uuid (ToolsTrace.BashToolsTrace (ToolsTrace.RunCommandStopped cmd targs code _ _))) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"run-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"flavor\\\" .= (\\\"bash\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"stop\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"code-str\\\" .= show code\\n                        , \\\"cmd\\\" .= cmd\\n                        , \\\"args\\\" .= targs\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.RunToolTrace uuid (ToolsTrace.IOToolsTrace (ToolsTrace.IOScriptStarted desc input))) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"run-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"flavor\\\" .= (\\\"io\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"start\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"tool\\\" .= desc.ioSlug\\n                        , \\\"input\\\" .= input\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.RunToolTrace uuid (ToolsTrace.IOToolsTrace (ToolsTrace.IOScriptStopped desc input _))) ->\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"run-id\\\" .= uuid\\n                        , \\\"flavor\\\" .= (\\\"io\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"action\\\" .= (\\\"stop\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"tool\\\" .= desc.ioSlug\\n                        , \\\"input\\\" .= input\\n                        ]\\n            (RuntimeTrace.ChildrenTrace sub) -> do\\n                subVal <- encodeAgentTrace sub\\n                Just $ Aeson.object [\\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"child\\\" :: Text.Text), \\\"sub\\\" .= subVal]\\n\\n    encodeMcpTrace :: McpServerDescription -> McpTools.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeMcpTrace (McpSimpleBinary cfg) tr = do\\n        baseVal <- encodeBaseMcpTrace tr\\n        Just $\\n            Aeson.object\\n                [ \\\"e\\\"\\n                    .= Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"server\\\" .= cfg.name\\n                        , \\\"val\\\" .= baseVal\\n                        ]\\n                ]\\n\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace :: McpTools.Trace -> Maybe Aeson.Value\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace (McpTools.McpClientClientTrace _) = Nothing\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientRunTrace (McpClient.RunBufferMoved _ _)) =\\n            Nothing\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientRunTrace (McpClient.RunCommandStart _)) =\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"program-start\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        ]\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientRunTrace (McpClient.RunCommandStopped _ code)) =\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"program-end\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"code-str\\\" .= show code\\n                        ]\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientLoopTrace McpClient.ExitingToolCallLoop) =\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"loop-end\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        ]\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientLoopTrace (McpClient.ToolsRefreshed _)) =\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tools-reloaded\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        ]\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientLoopTrace (McpClient.StartToolCall n _)) =\\n                    Just $\\n                        Aeson.object\\n                                [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool-call-start\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                                , \\\"name\\\" .= n\\n                                ]\\n    encodeBaseMcpTrace\\n        (McpTools.McpClientLoopTrace (McpClient.EndToolCall n _ _)) =\\n                Just $\\n                    Aeson.object\\n                        [ \\\"x\\\" .= (\\\"tool-call-end\\\" :: Text.Text)\\n                        , \\\"name\\\" .= n\\n                        ]\\n\\nmakeHttpJsonTrace :: (Aeson.ToJSON a) => Prod.Tracer IO HttpClient.Trace -> Text.Text -> IO (Prod.Tracer IO a)\\nmakeHttpJsonTrace baseTracer url = do\\n    rt <- HttpLogger.Runtime <$> HttpClient.newRuntime HttpClient.NoToken <*> pure url\\n    pure $ HttpLogger.httpTracer rt baseTracer\\n\\ninterpretPromptScript :: PromptScript -> IO Text.Text\\ninterpretPromptScript [] =\\n    Text.unlines <$> traverse interpretPromptScriptDirective [FileContents \\\"/dev/stdin\\\"]\\ninterpretPromptScript directives =\\n    Text.unlines <$> traverse interpretPromptScriptDirective directives\\n\\ninterpretPromptScriptDirective :: PromptScriptDirective -> IO Text.Text\\ninterpretPromptScriptDirective x =\\n    case x of\\n        Str s -> pure s\\n        FileContents p -> Text.readFile p\\n        Separator n s -> pure $ Text.replicate n s\\n        ShellOutput cmd -> Text.pack <$> Process.readCreateProcess (Process.shell cmd) (\\\"\\\" :: String)\\n\\n\""

All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

The goal was to turn the `openApiToolboxes` of `AgentDescription` into `ToolRegistration` in the `newRuntime` call of `initAgentTreeAgent`.

### Changes made:

1. **`src/System/Agents/Runtime/Runtime.hs`**:
   - Added a new parameter `[ToolRegistration]` to `newRuntime` function to accept pre-initialized OpenAPI tool registrations
   - Modified the `readTools` function to include OpenAPI tool registrations alongside MCP tools

2. **`src/System/Agents/AgentTree.hs`**:
   - Added import for `OpenAPIToolbox` module
   - Added new `Trace` constructor `OpenAPITrace` for tracing OpenAPI toolbox events
   - Added new `LoadingError` constructor `OpenAPIInitError` for handling OpenAPI initialization errors
   - Added `openApiDescToConfig` function to convert `OpenAPIServerDescription` to `OpenAPIToolbox.Config`
   - Added `initializeOpenAPIToolboxes` function to initialize OpenAPI toolboxes and convert them to `ToolRegistration` values
   - Modified both `initAgentTreeAgent` and `initAgentTreeAgentDeferred` to:
     - Extract `openApiToolboxes` from the agent description
     - Initialize each OpenAPI toolbox using `initializeOpenAPIToolboxes`
     - Pass the resulting tool registrations to `Runtime.newRuntime`

3. **`src/System/Agents/CLI/TraceUtils.hs`**:
   - Added handler for `OpenAPITrace` in trace functions

4. **`app/Main.hs`**:
   - Added handler for `OpenAPITrace` in `toJsonTrace` function
   
The implementation now seamlessly integrates OpenAPI toolbox configurations from agent JSON files, transforming them into usable tool registrations during runtime initialization.
</details>

### Response

All 130 tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

The task was to turn the `openApiToolboxes` of `AgentDescription` into `ToolRegistration` in the `newRuntime` call of `initAgentTreeAgent`.

### Changes Made:

1. **`src/System/Agents/Runtime/Runtime.hs`**:
   - Added a new parameter `[ToolRegistration]` to `newRuntime` to accept pre-initialized OpenAPI tool registrations
   - Modified `readAdditionalTools` to combine MCP tools with OpenAPI tool registrations

2. **`src/System/Agents/AgentTree.hs`**:
   - Added `OpenAPITrace` constructor to the `Trace` type for tracing OpenAPI events
   - Added `OpenAPIInitError` constructor to `LoadingError` for error handling
   - Added `openApiDescToConfig` function to convert `OpenAPIServerDescription` to `OpenAPIToolbox.Config`
   - Added `initializeOpenAPIToolboxes` function that:
     - Takes OpenAPI toolbox descriptions from agent config
     - Initializes each toolbox using `OpenAPIToolbox.initializeToolbox`
     - Registers all tools using `registerOpenAPITools` to produce `[ToolRegistration]`
   - Modified `initAgentTreeAgent` and `initAgentTreeAgentDeferred` to:
     - Extract `openApiToolboxes` from agent description
     - Initialize and register OpenAPI tools
     - Pass resulting tool registrations to `Runtime.newRuntime`

3. **`src/System/Agents/CLI/TraceUtils.hs`** and **`app/Main.hs`**:
   - Added handlers for the new `OpenAPITrace` constructor in trace processing functions

The OpenAPI tools defined in agent JSON configurations (via the `openApiToolboxes` field) are now automatically initialized and registered as LLM tools when agents are created.

Format your final message as follows:\\n- Start with a one-liner commit message in imperative mode (e.g., 'Fix bug in user authentication')\\n- The one-liner should reference the fixed issue (e.g., 'Fix bug in user authentication (#12345)')\\n- Follow with a blank line, then a body with a nice summary of the changes\\n- In the body, reference the fixed issue using 'Closes #12345' format\\n\\nExample format:\\nFix critical bug in session handling (#75)\\n\\nThis commit resolves the memory leak caused by not properly\\nclosing session handles. The fix ensures all resources are\\nreleased when a session is terminated.\\n\\nCloses #75\\n```\\n\\n### User Query\\n\\n# Implement OpenAPI specification types and JSON parsers\\n\\n## Overview\\nCreate the data types and JSON parsers needed to parse OpenAPI 3.x specifications.\\n\\n## Scope\\nCreate a new module `src/System/Agents/Tools/OpenAPI/Types.hs` with:\\n\\n### Data Types\\n```haskell\\n-- Top-level OpenAPI spec\\ndata OpenAPISpec = OpenAPISpec\\n    { specPaths :: Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n    , specComponents :: Maybe Components\\n    }\\n\\n-- Path and method types\\ntype Path = Text\\ntype Method = Text -- GET, POST, etc.\\n\\n-- Operation (an endpoint)\\ndata Operation = Operation\\n    { opOperationId :: Maybe Text\\n    , opSummary :: Maybe Text\\n    , opDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , opParameters :: [Parameter]\\n    , opRequestBody :: Maybe RequestBody\\n    }\\n\\n-- Parameter (path, query, header)\\ndata Parameter = Parameter\\n    { paramName :: Text\\n    , paramIn :: ParamLocation  -- Path | Query | Header\\n    , paramDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , paramRequired :: Bool\\n    , paramSchema :: Maybe Schema\\n    }\\n\\ndata ParamLocation = ParamInPath | ParamInQuery | ParamInHeader\\n\\n-- Request body\\ndata RequestBody = RequestBody\\n    { reqBodyDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , reqBodyRequired :: Bool\\n    , reqBodyContent :: Map MediaType Schema  -- key: \\\"application/json\\\"\\n    }\\n\\ntype MediaType = Text\\n\\n-- JSON Schema (subset needed for OpenAPI)\\ndata Schema = Schema\\n    { schemaType :: Maybe Text          -- \\\"string\\\", \\\"integer\\\", \\\"array\\\", \\\"object\\\"\\n    , schemaDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaEnum :: Maybe [Aeson.Value] -- For enum values\\n    , schemaProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    , schemaItems :: Maybe Schema       -- For arrays\\n    , schemaAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema]     -- For anyOf unions\\n    , schemaRef :: Maybe Text           -- $ref like \\\"#/components/schemas/Foo\\\"\\n    , schemaRequired :: Maybe [Text]\\n    }\\n\\n-- Components section for reusable schemas\\ndata Components = Components\\n    { componentsSchemas :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    }\\n```\\n\\n### JSON Instances\\n- Implement `FromJSON` for all types\\n- Handle optional fields correctly\\n- Parse `$ref` fields properly\\n\\n### Let me provide a summary of the implementation:\\n\\nImplement OpenAPI specification types and JSON parsers (#95)\\n\\nThis commit adds the data types and JSON parsers needed to parse OpenAPI 3.x specifications for LLM tool generation.\\n\\nChanges:\\n- Created `src/System/Agents/Tools/OpenAPI/Types.hs` with complete OpenAPI types:\\n  - `OpenAPISpec` - Top-level specification with paths and components\\n  - `Operation` - API endpoint representation (operationId, summary, description, etc.)\\n  - `Parameter` - Path/query/header parameters with location, required flag, and schema\\n  - `ParamLocation` - Enum for parameter locations (path/query/header)\\n  - `RequestBody` - Request body with content type to schema mapping\\n  - `Schema` - JSON Schema subset supporting type, properties, items, anyOf, $ref, etc.\\n  - `Components` - Reusable schema definitions\\n\\n- Implemented `FromJSON` and `ToJSON` instances for all types following existing\\n  codebase patterns from `System.Agents.MCP.Base`\\n\\n- Added the module to `agents.cabal` exposed modules\\n\\n- Added 10 comprehensive unit tests covering:\\n  - Minimal spec parsing\\n  - Path and method parsing\\n  - Parameter parsing (path, query locations)\\n  - Schema parsing ($ref references and inline with properties)\\n  - Request body parsing\\n  - Components section parsing\\n  - Round-trip JSON serialization\\n\\nThe implementation focuses on the OpenAPI subset needed for tool generation\\nrather than full spec compliance. Reference resolution ($ref) will be handled\\nin a separate issue as noted in the tradeoffs.\\n\\nCloses #95\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI 3.x specification types and JSON parsers.\\n--\\n-- This module provides a subset of OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 types needed for\\n-- LLM tool generation. It focuses on parsing paths, operations,\\n-- parameters, request bodies, and schemas.\\n--\\n-- Note: This is not a full OpenAPI implementation - only the subset\\n-- needed for tool generation is supported.\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    -- * OpenAPI spec types\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Path,\\n    Method,\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    ParamLocation (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n    Components (..),\\n    MediaType,\\n    \\n    -- * Tool execution result\\n    ToolResult (..),\\n    \\n    -- * JSON helpers\\n    Pair,\\n    object,\\n    parseParamLocation,\\n    paramLocationToText,\\n    (.=?),\\n    (.=),\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Applicative ((<|>))\\nimport Data.Aeson (FromJSON (..), ToJSON (..), Value (..), withObject, (.:), (.:?))\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Aeson.Key (fromText, toText, Key)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap\\nimport Data.Aeson.Types (Parser)\\nimport qualified Data.HashMap.Strict as HashMap\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (catMaybes, fromMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\n-- | Aeson Pair type for building JSON objects.\\ntype Pair = (Key, Value)\\n\\n-- | Build JSON object from a list of optional pairs.\\nobject :: [Maybe Pair] -> Value\\nobject = Aeson.object . catMaybes\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Top-level OpenAPI spec\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Top-level OpenAPI specification document.\\ndata OpenAPISpec = OpenAPISpec\\n    { specPaths :: Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n    , specComponents :: Maybe Components\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"OpenAPISpec\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        -- Parse paths object into a Map Path (Map Method Operation)\\n        pathsObj <- o .: \\\"paths\\\"\\n        paths <- parsePaths pathsObj\\n        OpenAPISpec\\n            <$> pure paths\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"components\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON OpenAPISpec where\\n    toJSON spec =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"paths\\\" .= specPaths spec\\n            , \\\"components\\\" .=? specComponents spec\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parse the paths object where each path maps to a methods object.\\nparsePaths :: Value -> Parser (Map Path (Map Method Operation))\\nparsePaths = withObject \\\"paths\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parsePathItem o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single path item from the paths object.\\nparsePathItem :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Path, Map Method Operation)\\nparsePathItem o key = do\\n    let pathText = toText key\\n    val <- o .: key\\n    operations <- parseOperations val\\n    pure (pathText, operations)\\n\\n-- | Parse an operations object (the value of a path).\\n-- This contains the HTTP methods like \\\"get\\\", \\\"post\\\", etc.\\nparseOperations :: Value -> Parser (Map Method Operation)\\nparseOperations = withObject \\\"pathItem\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let methodKeys = filter (not . isExtensionKey) (KeyMap.keys o)\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMethodOperation o) methodKeys\\n  where\\n    -- Skip OpenAPI extension fields (starting with \\\"x-\\\") and other non-method fields\\n    isExtensionKey k =\\n        let t = toText k\\n         in Text.isPrefixOf \\\"x-\\\" t || t `elem` [\\\"parameters\\\", \\\"summary\\\", \\\"description\\\", \\\"servers\\\"]\\n\\n-- | Parse a single method operation.\\nparseMethodOperation :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (Method, Operation)\\nparseMethodOperation o key = do\\n    let methodText = Text.toUpper $ toText key\\n    op <- o .: key\\n    pure (methodText, op)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Path and method types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | URL path like \\\"/pets\\\" or \\\"/pets/{petId}\\\".\\ntype Path = Text\\n\\n-- | HTTP method like \\\"GET\\\", \\\"POST\\\", \\\"PUT\\\", \\\"DELETE\\\", etc.\\ntype Method = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Operation (an endpoint)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents an API operation (endpoint).\\n-- Corresponds to an Operation Object in OpenAPI.\\ndata Operation = Operation\\n    { opOperationId :: Maybe Text\\n    , opSummary :: Maybe Text\\n    , opDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , opParameters :: [Parameter]\\n    , opRequestBody :: Maybe RequestBody\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Operation where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Operation\\\" $ \\\\o ->\\n        Operation\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"operationId\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"summary\\\"\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe [] <$> o .:? \\\"parameters\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"requestBody\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Operation where\\n    toJSON op =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"operationId\\\" .=? op.opOperationId\\n            , \\\"summary\\\" .=? op.opSummary\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? op.opDescription\\n            , \\\"parameters\\\" .=? if null op.opParameters then Nothing else Just op.opParameters\\n            , \\\"requestBody\\\" .=? op.opRequestBody\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter (path, query, header)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parameter (path, query, or header parameter).\\ndata Parameter = Parameter\\n    { paramName :: Text\\n    , paramIn :: ParamLocation\\n    , paramDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , paramRequired :: Bool\\n    , paramSchema :: Maybe Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Parameter where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Parameter\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        locationStr <- o .: \\\"in\\\"\\n        location <- parseParamLocation locationStr\\n        Parameter\\n            <$> o .: \\\"name\\\"\\n            <*> pure location\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> o .:? \\\"schema\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Parameter where\\n    toJSON param =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"name\\\" .= param.paramName\\n            , \\\"in\\\" .= paramLocationToText param.paramIn\\n            , \\\"description\\\" .=? param.paramDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if param.paramRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"schema\\\" .=? param.paramSchema\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parameter location: path, query, or header.\\ndata ParamLocation\\n    = ParamInPath\\n    | ParamInQuery\\n    | ParamInHeader\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a parameter location from text.\\nparseParamLocation :: Text -> Parser ParamLocation\\nparseParamLocation \\\"path\\\" = pure ParamInPath\\nparseParamLocation \\\"query\\\" = pure ParamInQuery\\nparseParamLocation \\\"header\\\" = pure ParamInHeader\\nparseParamLocation other = fail $ \\\"Unknown parameter location: \\\" ++ Text.unpack other\\n\\n-- | Convert a parameter location to text.\\nparamLocationToText :: ParamLocation -> Text\\nparamLocationToText ParamInPath = \\\"path\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInQuery = \\\"query\\\"\\nparamLocationToText ParamInHeader = \\\"header\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Request body\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a request body for an operation.\\ndata RequestBody = RequestBody\\n    { reqBodyDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , reqBodyRequired :: Bool\\n    , reqBodyContent :: Map MediaType Schema\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON RequestBody where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"RequestBody\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        contentObj <- o .: \\\"content\\\"\\n        content <- parseContentMap contentObj\\n        RequestBody\\n            <$> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n            <*> (fromMaybe False <$> o .:? \\\"required\\\")\\n            <*> pure content\\n\\ninstance ToJSON RequestBody where\\n    toJSON rb =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"description\\\" .=? rb.reqBodyDescription\\n            , \\\"required\\\" .=? if rb.reqBodyRequired then Just True else Nothing\\n            , \\\"content\\\" .= KeyMap.fromList (map mediaTypeSchemaToPair $ Map.toList rb.reqBodyContent)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Parse a content map (media type -> media type object with schema).\\nparseContentMap :: Value -> Parser (Map MediaType Schema)\\nparseContentMap = withObject \\\"content\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    let keys = KeyMap.keys o\\n    fmap Map.fromList $ traverse (parseMediaTypeSchema o) keys\\n\\n-- | Parse a single media type entry.\\nparseMediaTypeSchema :: KeyMap.KeyMap Value -> Key -> Parser (MediaType, Schema)\\nparseMediaTypeSchema o key = do\\n    let mediaType = toText key\\n    mediaObj <- o .: key\\n    schema <- mediaObj .: \\\"schema\\\"\\n    pure (mediaType, schema)\\n\\n-- | Convert media type/schema pair to an Aeson pair.\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair :: (MediaType, Schema) -> Pair\\nmediaTypeSchemaToPair (mediaType, schema) =\\n    (fromText mediaType, Aeson.object [\\\"schema\\\" Aeson..= schema])\\n\\n-- | Media type like \\\"application/json\\\" or \\\"application/xml\\\".\\ntype MediaType = Text\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- JSON Schema (subset needed for OpenAPI)\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema subset used in OpenAPI.\\n-- Supports common schema types needed for tool generation.\\ndata Schema = Schema\\n    { schemaType :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaDescription :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaEnum :: Maybe [Aeson.Value]\\n    , schemaProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    , schemaItems :: Maybe Schema\\n    , schemaAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema]\\n    , schemaRef :: Maybe Text\\n    , schemaRequired :: Maybe [Text]\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Schema where\\n    parseJSON v =\\n        parseRefSchema v\\n            <|> parseInlineSchema v\\n\\n-- | Parse a schema that uses $ref.\\nparseRefSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseRefSchema = withObject \\\"Schema ($ref)\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    ref <- o .: \\\"$ref\\\"\\n    pure $\\n        Schema\\n            { schemaType = Nothing\\n            , schemaDescription = Nothing\\n            , schemaEnum = Nothing\\n            , schemaProperties = Nothing\\n            , schemaItems = Nothing\\n            , schemaAnyOf = Nothing\\n            , schemaRef = Just ref\\n            , schemaRequired = Nothing\\n            }\\n\\n-- | Parse an inline schema definition.\\nparseInlineSchema :: Value -> Parser Schema\\nparseInlineSchema = withObject \\\"Schema\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n    mType <- o .:? \\\"type\\\"\\n    mProps <- o .:? \\\"properties\\\"\\n    -- Convert properties from HashMap to Map if present\\n    let props = fmap HashMap.toList mProps >>= \\\\pairs ->\\n            if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n    Schema\\n        <$> pure mType\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"description\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"enum\\\"\\n        <*> pure props\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"items\\\"\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"anyOf\\\"\\n        <*> pure Nothing\\n        <*> o .:? \\\"required\\\"\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Schema where\\n    toJSON schema =\\n        case schema.schemaRef of\\n            Just ref -> Aeson.object [\\\"$ref\\\" Aeson..= ref]\\n            Nothing ->\\n                object\\n                    [ \\\"type\\\" .=? schema.schemaType\\n                    , \\\"description\\\" .=? schema.schemaDescription\\n                    , \\\"enum\\\" .=? schema.schemaEnum\\n                    , \\\"properties\\\" .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaPropertyToPair . Map.toList) schema.schemaProperties)\\n                    , \\\"items\\\" .=? schema.schemaItems\\n                    , \\\"anyOf\\\" .=? schema.schemaAnyOf\\n                    , \\\"required\\\" .=? schema.schemaRequired\\n                    ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema property to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaPropertyToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaPropertyToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Components section for reusable schemas\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Components section containing reusable schemas and other definitions.\\ndata Components = Components\\n    { componentsSchemas :: Maybe (Map Text Schema)\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\ninstance FromJSON Components where\\n    parseJSON = withObject \\\"Components\\\" $ \\\\o -> do\\n        mSchemas <- o .:? \\\"schemas\\\"\\n        -- Convert from HashMap to Map\\n        let schemas = mSchemas >>= \\\\hm ->\\n                let pairs = HashMap.toList hm\\n                 in if null pairs then Nothing else Just $ Map.fromList $ map (\\\\(k, v) -> (toText k, v)) pairs\\n        pure $ Components schemas\\n\\ninstance ToJSON Components where\\n    toJSON comps =\\n        object\\n            [ \\\"schemas\\\"\\n                .=? (fmap (Aeson.object . map schemaDefinitionToPair . Map.toList) comps.componentsSchemas)\\n            ]\\n\\n-- | Convert schema definition to an Aeson pair.\\nschemaDefinitionToPair :: (Text, Schema) -> Pair\\nschemaDefinitionToPair (key, val) = (fromText key, toJSON val)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool execution result\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Result of executing an OpenAPI tool.\\n--\\n-- This type captures the structured result from executing an OpenAPI-based\\n-- tool call, including the HTTP response details.\\ndata ToolResult = ToolResult\\n    { resultPath :: Text\\n    -- ^ Path that was called\\n    , resultMethod :: Text\\n    -- ^ HTTP method used\\n    , resultStatus :: Int\\n    -- ^ HTTP status code\\n    , resultPayload :: Value\\n    -- ^ Response payload as JSON\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show)\\n\\ninstance ToJSON ToolResult where\\n    toJSON tr =\\n        Aeson.object\\n            [ \\\"path\\\" Aeson..= resultPath tr\\n            , \\\"method\\\" Aeson..= resultMethod tr\\n            , \\\"status\\\" Aeson..= resultStatus tr\\n            , \\\"payload\\\" Aeson..= resultPayload tr\\n            ]\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Helper functions for JSON construction\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Optional field helper - only include if value is present.\\n(.=?) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> Maybe v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=?) k v = fmap ((Aeson..=) k) v\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=?\\n\\n-- | Required field helper.\\n(.=) :: (ToJSON v) => Key -> v -> Maybe Pair\\n(.=) k v = Just ((Aeson..=) k v)\\n\\ninfixr 8 .=\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | JSON Schema reference resolution ($ref dereferencing) for OpenAPI specs.\\n--\\n-- This module provides functions to recursively resolve '$ref' pointers in\\n-- OpenAPI schemas. This is essential because OpenAPI specs use references\\n-- to avoid duplication and improve maintainability.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\n-- >>> let petSchema = Schema (Just \\\"object\\\") Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing\\n-- >>> let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n-- >>> let refSchema = Schema Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\") Nothing\\n-- >>> resolveSchema refSchema components\\n-- Schema {schemaType = Just \\\"object\\\", ...}\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Resolver (\\n    -- * Core resolution functions\\n    resolveSchema,\\n    dereferenceSpec,\\n    \\n    -- * Reference path handling\\n    RefPath (..),\\n    parseRef,\\n    \\n    -- * Resolution errors\\n    ResolutionError (..),\\n    \\n    -- * Low-level resolution\\n    resolveRef,\\n    resolveSchemaWithDepth,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Control.Monad (when)\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)\\nimport Data.Set (Set)\\nimport qualified Data.Set as Set\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Components (..),\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Reference path representation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Represents a parsed JSON reference path.\\n--\\n-- A reference like @#/components/schemas/Pet@ is parsed into:\\n-- @\\n-- RefPath { refComponents = \\\"components\\\"\\n--         , refSection = \\\"schemas\\\"\\n--         , refName = \\\"Pet\\\"\\n--         }\\n-- @\\ndata RefPath = RefPath\\n    { refComponents :: Text\\n    -- ^ The first path segment (usually \\\"components\\\")\\n    , refSection :: Text\\n    -- ^ The second path segment (e.g., \\\"schemas\\\", \\\"parameters\\\")\\n    , refName :: Text\\n    -- ^ The name of the referenced entity\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parse a reference string into a 'RefPath'.\\n--\\n-- Supports internal references of the form @#/components/schemas/Name@.\\n-- External references (URLs) are not supported and will return 'Nothing'.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> parseRef \\\"#/components/schemas/Pet\\\"\\n-- Just (RefPath {refComponents = \\\"components\\\", refSection = \\\"schemas\\\", refName = \\\"Pet\\\"})\\n--\\n-- >>> parseRef \\\"http://example.com/schema.json\\\"\\n-- Nothing\\nparseRef :: Text -> Maybe RefPath\\nparseRef ref\\n    -- External refs (http/https) are not supported\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"http://\\\" ref = Nothing\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"https://\\\" ref = Nothing\\n    -- Internal refs starting with #/\\n    | Text.isPrefixOf \\\"#/\\\" ref =\\n        let parts = Text.splitOn \\\"/\\\" (Text.drop 2 ref)\\n         in case parts of\\n                (comp : sect : name : _) ->\\n                    Just $ RefPath comp sect name\\n                _ -> Nothing\\n    -- Relative refs not starting with #/ are not supported\\n    | otherwise = Nothing\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Resolution errors\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Errors that can occur during reference resolution.\\ndata ResolutionError\\n    = -- | The reference path is malformed or unsupported\\n      InvalidRefPath Text\\n    | -- | The referenced schema was not found in components\\n      SchemaNotFound Text\\n    | -- | A circular reference was detected\\n      CircularReference [Text]\\n    | -- | Maximum recursion depth exceeded (safety limit)\\n      MaxDepthExceeded Int\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core resolution functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Default maximum recursion depth to prevent infinite loops.\\ndefaultMaxDepth :: Int\\ndefaultMaxDepth = 100\\n\\n-- | Recursively resolves all '$ref' pointers in a schema.\\n--\\n-- This function will follow references transitively until no more\\n-- references remain, or an error condition is encountered.\\n--\\n-- For simple resolution without error handling, use 'resolveSchema'.\\nresolveSchemaWithDepth ::\\n    -- | Schema to resolve\\n    Schema ->\\n    -- | Components containing schema definitions\\n    Components ->\\n    -- | Current resolution depth (for tracking)\\n    Int ->\\n    -- | Set of already-visited refs (for cycle detection)\\n    Set Text ->\\n    -- | Either an error or the fully-resolved schema\\n    Either ResolutionError Schema\\nresolveSchemaWithDepth schema components depth visited = do\\n    -- Check recursion depth\\n    when (depth > defaultMaxDepth) $\\n        Left (MaxDepthExceeded defaultMaxDepth)\\n\\n    case schema.schemaRef of\\n        -- If this schema has a $ref, resolve it\\n        Just ref -> do\\n            -- Check for circular reference\\n            when (Set.member ref visited) $\\n                Left (CircularReference (Set.toList $ Set.insert ref visited))\\n\\n            -- Parse and resolve the reference\\n            resolved <- resolveRef ref components\\n            -- Recursively resolve the referenced schema\\n            resolveSchemaWithDepth\\n                resolved\\n                components\\n                (depth + 1)\\n                (Set.insert ref visited)\\n        -- No $ref, recursively resolve nested schemas\\n        Nothing -> do\\n            resolvedProps <- resolveProperties schema.schemaProperties\\n            resolvedItems <- resolveItems schema.schemaItems\\n            resolvedAnyOf <- resolveAnyOf schema.schemaAnyOf\\n            pure $\\n                schema\\n                    { schemaProperties = resolvedProps\\n                    , schemaItems = resolvedItems\\n                    , schemaAnyOf = resolvedAnyOf\\n                    }\\n  where\\n    resolveProperties :: Maybe (Map Text Schema) -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe (Map Text Schema))\\n    resolveProperties Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveProperties (Just props) =\\n        Just <$> traverse (\\\\s -> resolveSchemaWithDepth s components depth visited) props\\n\\n    resolveItems :: Maybe Schema -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe Schema)\\n    resolveItems Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveItems (Just items) =\\n        Just <$> resolveSchemaWithDepth items components depth visited\\n\\n    resolveAnyOf :: Maybe [Schema] -> Either ResolutionError (Maybe [Schema])\\n    resolveAnyOf Nothing = Right Nothing\\n    resolveAnyOf (Just schemas) =\\n        Just <$> traverse (\\\\s -> resolveSchemaWithDepth s components depth visited) schemas\\n\\n-- | Resolve a reference path to a schema from components.\\n--\\n-- Looks up the schema in the components/schemas section.\\nresolveRef :: Text -> Components -> Either ResolutionError Schema\\nresolveRef ref components = do\\n    refPath <- maybe (Left $ InvalidRefPath ref) Right (parseRef ref)\\n\\n    schemas <-\\n        maybe (Left $ SchemaNotFound ref) Right (components.componentsSchemas)\\n\\n    schema <-\\n        maybe (Left $ SchemaNotFound ref) Right (Map.lookup refPath.refName schemas)\\n\\n    pure schema\\n\\n-- | Recursively resolves all '$ref' pointers in a schema.\\n--\\n-- This is the main entry point for schema resolution. It handles:\\n--\\n-- * Simple references like @#/components/schemas/Pet@\\n-- * Nested references (schema A refs B refs C)\\n-- * Arrays with item references\\n-- * Object properties with references\\n-- * anyOf schemas with references\\n--\\n-- For circular references, the original $ref is returned to prevent infinite loops.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let components = Components (Just (Map.fromList [(\\\"Pet\\\", petSchema)]))\\n-- >>> resolveSchema refSchema components\\n-- Schema {schemaType = Just \\\"object\\\", ...}\\nresolveSchema :: Schema -> Components -> Schema\\nresolveSchema schema components =\\n    case resolveSchemaWithDepth schema components 0 Set.empty of\\n        Left err ->\\n            -- On error, return a schema with an error description\\n            -- but preserve the original to avoid losing information\\n            schema\\n                { schemaDescription =\\n                    Just $ \\\"Reference resolution failed: \\\" <> Text.pack (show err)\\n                }\\n        Right resolved -> resolved\\n\\n-- | Resolves all schemas within an OpenAPI spec.\\n--\\n-- This function walks through the entire spec and resolves all\\n-- schema references in:\\n--\\n-- * Path parameters\\n-- * Operation parameters\\n-- * Request bodies\\n-- * Response schemas (when implemented)\\n--\\n-- The returned spec has all $ref pointers fully resolved.\\ndereferenceSpec :: OpenAPISpec -> OpenAPISpec\\ndereferenceSpec spec =\\n    let comps = fromMaybe emptyComponents spec.specComponents\\n        resolvedPaths = Map.map (Map.map (resolveOperation comps)) spec.specPaths\\n     in spec{specPaths = resolvedPaths}\\n  where\\n    emptyComponents :: Components\\n    emptyComponents = Components Nothing\\n\\n    resolveOperation :: Components -> Operation -> Operation\\n    resolveOperation comps op =\\n        op\\n            { opParameters = map (resolveParameter comps) op.opParameters\\n            , opRequestBody = fmap (resolveRequestBody comps) op.opRequestBody\\n            }\\n\\n    resolveParameter :: Components -> Parameter -> Parameter\\n    resolveParameter comps param =\\n        param{paramSchema = fmap (\\\\s -> resolveSchema s comps) param.paramSchema}\\n\\n    resolveRequestBody :: Components -> RequestBody -> RequestBody\\n    resolveRequestBody comps body =\\n        body{reqBodyContent = Map.map (\\\\s -> resolveSchema s comps) body.reqBodyContent}\\n\\n\""

"\"----\\n{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}\\n{-# LANGUAGE RecordWildCards #-}\\n\\n-- | OpenAPI to Tool conversion logic.\\n--\\n-- This module converts parsed and dereferenced OpenAPI operations into the\\n-- internal Tool representation used by the agents system. It handles:\\n--\\n-- * Converting OpenAPI specs to lists of tools\\n-- * Building tool parameter schemas from OpenAPI parameters\\n-- * Naming conventions (operationId or path/method based)\\n-- * Parameter naming (p_ prefix for path/query, b for body)\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\n-- >>> import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") Nothing [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nmodule System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Converter (\\n    -- * Core types\\n    OpenAPITool (..),\\n    ToolParameters (..),\\n    PropertySchema (..),\\n    ToolHandler,\\n\\n    -- * Main conversion functions\\n    convertOpenAPIToTools,\\n    convertOperation,\\n\\n    -- * Parameter building\\n    buildToolProperty,\\n    paramToToolName,\\n    buildToolParameters,\\n\\n    -- * Description building\\n    buildToolDescription,\\n\\n    -- * Required parameters\\n    getRequiredParams,\\n\\n    -- * Naming\\n    deriveToolName,\\n    sanitizePathForName,\\n\\n    -- * OpenAI conversion\\n    toOpenAITool,\\n\\n    -- * Handler helpers\\n    defaultHandler,\\n) where\\n\\nimport Data.Aeson (Value)\\nimport qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson\\nimport Data.Map.Strict (Map)\\nimport qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map\\nimport Data.Maybe (fromMaybe, mapMaybe)\\nimport Data.Text (Text)\\nimport qualified Data.Text as Text\\n\\nimport qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI\\nimport System.Agents.ToolSchema (ParamProperty (..), ParamType (..))\\nimport System.Agents.Tools.OpenAPI.Types (\\n    Method,\\n    OpenAPISpec (..),\\n    Operation (..),\\n    Parameter (..),\\n    Path,\\n    RequestBody (..),\\n    Schema (..),\\n )\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Core Types\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Handler function type for executing OpenAPI tool calls.\\n-- This is a placeholder type - actual execution would be implemented\\n-- by the caller based on their HTTP client needs.\\ntype ToolHandler = Map Text Value -> IO (Either Text Text)\\n\\n-- | Internal representation of a tool derived from OpenAPI.\\ndata OpenAPITool = OpenAPITool\\n    { toolOperation :: Operation\\n    , toolPath :: Path\\n    , toolMethod :: Method\\n    , toolName :: Text\\n    , toolDescription :: Text\\n    , toolParameters :: ToolParameters\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Parameters structure for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata ToolParameters = ToolParameters\\n    { paramsType :: Text\\n    -- ^ Always \\\"object\\\" for OpenAPI tools\\n    , paramsProperties :: Map Text PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ Map of parameter name to property schema\\n    , paramsRequired :: [Text]\\n    -- ^ List of required parameter names\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- | Property schema for LLM tool parameters.\\n-- Mirrors JSON Schema structure needed for LLM tool definitions.\\ndata PropertySchema = PropertySchema\\n    { propType :: Maybe Text\\n    -- ^ JSON Schema type (string, integer, boolean, etc.)\\n    , propDescription :: Text\\n    -- ^ Description for the LLM\\n    , propEnum :: Maybe [Text]\\n    -- ^ For enum values, stores the allowed values\\n    , propItems :: Maybe PropertySchema\\n    -- ^ For arrays, the schema of array items\\n    , propAnyOf :: Maybe [PropertySchema]\\n    -- ^ For anyOf schemas, the possible schemas\\n    }\\n    deriving (Show, Eq)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Main Conversion Functions\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPI spec into a list of tools.\\n--\\n-- This function walks through all paths and methods in the spec,\\n-- converting each operation into an 'OpenAPITool'.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let spec = OpenAPISpec (Map.singleton \\\"/pets\\\" (Map.singleton \\\"GET\\\" op)) Nothing\\n-- >>> let tools = convertOpenAPIToTools spec\\n-- >>> length tools\\n-- 1\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools ::\\n    OpenAPISpec ->\\n    [OpenAPITool]\\nconvertOpenAPIToTools spec = do\\n    (path, methods) <- Map.toList (specPaths spec)\\n    (method, operation) <- Map.toList methods\\n    pure $ convertOperation path method operation\\n\\n-- | Converts a single OpenAPI operation to a tool.\\n--\\n-- This is the core conversion function that:\\n-- * Derives a tool name from operationId or path/method\\n-- * Builds the tool description from summary and description\\n-- * Converts parameters to property schemas\\n-- * Tracks required parameters\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"getPet\\\") (Just \\\"Get pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let tool = convertOperation \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> tool.toolName\\n-- \\\"openapi_getPet\\\"\\nconvertOperation ::\\n    Path ->\\n    Method ->\\n    Operation ->\\n    OpenAPITool\\nconvertOperation path method op =\\n    OpenAPITool\\n        { toolOperation = op\\n        , toolPath = path\\n        , toolMethod = method\\n        , toolName = deriveToolName path method op\\n        , toolDescription = buildToolDescription op\\n        , toolParameters = buildToolParameters op\\n        }\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Tool Name Derivation\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Derives a tool name from an OpenAPI operation.\\n--\\n-- Uses operationId if available, otherwise falls back to {method}_{path}.\\n-- All names are prefixed with \\\"openapi_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation (Just \\\"listPets\\\") Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_listPets\\\"\\n-- >>> deriveToolName \\\"/pets/{id}\\\" \\\"GET\\\" (Operation Nothing Nothing Nothing [] Nothing)\\n-- \\\"openapi_get_pets__id_\\\"\\nderiveToolName :: Path -> Method -> Operation -> Text\\nderiveToolName path method op =\\n    \\\"openapi_\\\" <> case opOperationId op of\\n        Just opId -> opId\\n        Nothing -> sanitizePathForName method path\\n\\n-- | Sanitizes a path for use in a tool name.\\n--\\n-- Converts the path/method combination to a valid identifier.\\n-- Replaces special characters with underscores.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/pets\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_pets\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"POST\\\" \\\"/pets/{id}/owner\\\"\\n-- \\\"post_pets__id__owner\\\"\\n-- >>> sanitizePathForName \\\"GET\\\" \\\"/api/v1/users\\\"\\n-- \\\"get_api_v1_users\\\"\\nsanitizePathForName :: Method -> Path -> Text\\nsanitizePathForName method path =\\n    let methodPart = Text.toLower method\\n        -- Remove leading slash and replace special chars with underscore\\n        pathPart =\\n            Text.replace \\\"/\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"{\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.replace \\\"}\\\" \\\"_\\\"\\n                . Text.dropWhile (== '/')\\n                $ path\\n        -- Clean up multiple consecutive underscores\\n        cleaned = Text.replace \\\"__\\\" \\\"_\\\" pathPart\\n     in if Text.null cleaned\\n            then methodPart\\n            else methodPart <> \\\"_\\\" <> cleaned\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Description Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Builds a tool description from an operation's summary and description.\\n--\\n-- Combines both fields with a colon separator if both are present.\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation Nothing (Just \\\"Get a pet\\\") (Just \\\"Returns a single pet\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op\\n-- \\\"Get a pet:\\\\nReturns a single pet\\\"\\n-- >>> let op2 = Operation Nothing Nothing (Just \\\"Just description\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> buildToolDescription op2\\n-- \\\":\\\\nJust description\\\"\\nbuildToolDescription :: Operation -> Text\\nbuildToolDescription op =\\n    fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opSummary op) <> \\\":\\\\n\\\" <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (opDescription op)\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Parameter Building\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts a parameter to its tool property name.\\n--\\n-- Prefixes path and query parameters with \\\"p_\\\" to avoid conflicts.\\n-- Header parameters are also prefixed with \\\"p_\\\".\\n--\\n-- Examples:\\n-- >>> let param = Parameter \\\"petId\\\" ParamInPath Nothing True Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param\\n-- \\\"p_petId\\\"\\n-- >>> let param2 = Parameter \\\"limit\\\" ParamInQuery (Just \\\"Max results\\\") False Nothing\\n-- >>> paramToToolName param2\\n-- \\\"p_limit\\\"\\nparamToToolName :: Parameter -> Text\\nparamToToolName param = \\\"p_\\\" <> paramName param\\n\\n-- | Builds tool parameters structure from an operation.\\n--\\n-- Collects all parameters (path, query, header) and body into a\\n-- unified parameter schema structure.\\nbuildToolParameters :: Operation -> ToolParameters\\nbuildToolParameters op =\\n    ToolParameters\\n        { paramsType = \\\"object\\\"\\n        , paramsProperties = paramProps <> bodyProps\\n        , paramsRequired = getRequiredParams op\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Convert operation parameters to properties\\n    paramProps = Map.fromList $ mapMaybe paramToProperty (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property if present\\n    bodyProps = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body -> bodyToProperty body\\n        Nothing -> Map.empty\\n\\n    -- Convert a single parameter to (name, schema) if it has a schema\\n    paramToProperty :: Parameter -> Maybe (Text, PropertySchema)\\n    paramToProperty param = do\\n        schema <- paramSchema param\\n        pure (paramToToolName param, buildToolProperty schema (paramDescription param))\\n\\n    -- Convert request body to property map\\n    bodyToProperty :: RequestBody -> Map Text PropertySchema\\n    bodyToProperty body =\\n        -- Use \\\"b\\\" as the body parameter name\\n        -- For simplicity, we take the first schema from content types\\n        case Map.elems (reqBodyContent body) of\\n            [] -> Map.empty\\n            (schema : _) -> Map.singleton \\\"b\\\" (schemaToProperty schema (bodyDescription body))\\n\\n    bodyDescription :: RequestBody -> Text\\n    bodyDescription body = fromMaybe \\\"Request body\\\" (reqBodyDescription body)\\n\\n-- | Builds a property schema from an OpenAPI schema.\\n--\\n-- Handles:\\n-- * Enum values: copies type and augments description\\n-- * Arrays: copies items schema\\n-- * AnyOf: copies anyOf schemas\\n-- * Basic types: copies type and description\\n--\\n-- The parameter description is merged with the schema description.\\nbuildToolProperty ::\\n    Schema ->\\n    Maybe Text ->\\n    PropertySchema\\nbuildToolProperty schema paramDesc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = buildDescription schema\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`buildToolProperty` Nothing) (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`buildToolProperty` Nothing)) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    -- Build description from parameter description, schema description, and enum values\\n    buildDescription :: Schema -> Text\\n    buildDescription s =\\n        let baseDesc = fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (paramDesc <|> schemaDescription s)\\n            enumDesc = case schemaEnum s of\\n                Just vals -> \\\"\\\\nAllowed values: \\\" <> formatEnumValues vals\\n                Nothing -> \\\"\\\"\\n         in baseDesc <> enumDesc\\n\\n    -- Format enum values as a readable string\\n    formatEnumValues :: [Aeson.Value] -> Text\\n    formatEnumValues vals =\\n        let txtVals = mapMaybe extractString vals\\n         in Text.intercalate \\\", \\\" txtVals\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n    -- Extract enum values as Text list\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n-- | Converts a Schema to PropertySchema without parameter description.\\nschemaToProperty :: Schema -> Text -> PropertySchema\\nschemaToProperty schema desc =\\n    PropertySchema\\n        { propType = schemaType schema\\n        , propDescription = desc <> fromMaybe \\\"\\\" (fmap (\\\"\\\\n\\\" <>) (schemaDescription schema))\\n        , propEnum = extractEnum schema\\n        , propItems = fmap (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\") (schemaItems schema)\\n        , propAnyOf = fmap (map (`schemaToProperty` \\\"\\\")) (schemaAnyOf schema)\\n        }\\n  where\\n    extractEnum :: Schema -> Maybe [Text]\\n    extractEnum s =\\n        case schemaEnum s of\\n            Just vals -> Just $ mapMaybe extractString vals\\n            Nothing -> Nothing\\n\\n    extractString :: Aeson.Value -> Maybe Text\\n    extractString (Aeson.String s) = Just s\\n    extractString _ = Nothing\\n\\n-- Alternative operator for Maybe\\n(<|>) :: Maybe a -> Maybe a -> Maybe a\\n(<|>) (Just x) _ = Just x\\n(<|>) Nothing y = y\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Required Parameters\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Gets the list of required parameter names for a tool.\\n--\\n-- Includes:\\n-- * Parameters marked with required=true\\n-- * Body parameter \\\"b\\\" if request body is required\\ngetRequiredParams :: Operation -> [Text]\\ngetRequiredParams op =\\n    paramNames <> bodyName\\n  where\\n    -- Get required parameter names (prefixed)\\n    paramNames =\\n        map paramToToolName $\\n            filter paramRequired (opParameters op)\\n\\n    -- Check if body is required\\n    bodyName = case opRequestBody op of\\n        Just body | reqBodyRequired body -> [\\\"b\\\"]\\n        _ -> []\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Conversion to OpenAI Tool Format\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Converts an OpenAPITool to the OpenAI Tool format.\\n--\\n-- This allows OpenAPITools to be used directly with the LLM interface.\\n--\\n-- Example:\\n-- >>> let op = Operation (Just \\\"test\\\") (Just \\\"Test op\\\") (Just \\\"A test\\\") [] Nothing\\n-- >>> let apiTool = convertOperation \\\"/test\\\" \\\"GET\\\" op\\n-- >>> let tool = toOpenAITool apiTool\\n-- >>> OpenAI.toolName tool\\n-- ToolName {getToolName = \\\"openapi_test\\\"}\\ntoOpenAITool :: OpenAPITool -> OpenAI.Tool\\ntoOpenAITool apiTool =\\n    OpenAI.Tool\\n        { OpenAI.toolName = OpenAI.ToolName (toolName apiTool)\\n        , OpenAI.toolDescription = toolDescription apiTool\\n        , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = map propertyToParamProperty $ Map.toList (paramsProperties (toolParameters apiTool))\\n        }\\n  where\\n    propertyToParamProperty :: (Text, PropertySchema) -> ParamProperty\\n    propertyToParamProperty (name, prop) =\\n        ParamProperty\\n            { propertyKey = name\\n            , propertyType = schemaTypeToParamType prop\\n            , propertyDescription = propDescription prop\\n            }\\n\\n    schemaTypeToParamType :: PropertySchema -> ParamType\\n    schemaTypeToParamType prop =\\n        case propEnum prop of\\n            Just vals -> EnumParamType vals\\n            Nothing -> case propType prop of\\n                Just \\\"string\\\" -> StringParamType\\n                Just \\\"integer\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"number\\\" -> NumberParamType\\n                Just \\\"boolean\\\" -> BoolParamType\\n                Just \\\"array\\\" -> case propItems prop of\\n                    Just items -> OpaqueParamType (fromMaybe \\\"any\\\" (propType items))\\n                    Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"array\\\"\\n                Just \\\"object\\\" -> ObjectParamType []\\n                Just other -> OpaqueParamType other\\n                Nothing -> OpaqueParamType \\\"any\\\"\\n\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n-- Handler Helpers\\n-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n-- | Default handler that always returns an error.\\n-- The actual handler should be provided by the caller.\\ndefaultHandler :: ToolHandler\\ndefaultHandler _ = pure $ Left \\\"Tool handler not implemented\\\"\\n\\n\""
